USERMESSAGE #8
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→# AI Aquarium — QA Test Suite
     2→
     3→Playwright regression tests for [AI Aquarium](https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app).
     4→
     5→Tests run against the live deployment and cover all M1 features.
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Setup
    10→
    11→```bash
    12→npm install
    13→npx playwright install chromium
    14→```
    15→
    16→---
    17→
    18→## Running Tests
    19→
    20→### Full gate (smoke → regression → critical)
    21→
    22→```bash
    23→npx playwright test
    24→```
    25→
    26→### By tier
    27→
    28→```bash
    29→npm run test:smoke       # Fast smoke (8 tests, ~15s)
    30→npm run test:regression  # Full regression suite
    31→npm run test:critical    # Critical loop integrity
    32→```
    33→
    34→### Specific spec files
    35→
    36→```bash
    37→# M1 debug dashboard
    38→npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js
    39→
    40→# M1 activity duration + AI cycle
    41→npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js
    42→
    43→# Slot system / capacity
    44→npx playwright test tests/regression/slot-system.spec.js
    45→
    46→# Talk handshaking
    47→npx playwright test tests/regression/talk-handshaking.spec.js
    48→
    49→# Fallback logic
    50→npx playwright test tests/regression/fallback-logic.spec.js
    51→```
    52→
    53→### With UI (for debugging)
    54→
    55→```bash
    56→npx playwright test --ui
    57→```
    58→
    59→---
    60→
    61→## Configuration
    62→
    63→| Setting | Default | Override |
    64→|---------|---------|----------|
    65→| `BASE_URL` | `https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app` | `.env` file or env var |
    66→| Timeout | 60s per test | `playwright.config.js` |
    67→| Retries | 1 | `playwright.config.js` |
    68→
    69→To run against a local or staging deployment:
    70→
    71→```bash
    72→BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000 npx playwright test
    73→```
    74→
    75→---
    76→
    77→## Test Structure
    78→
    79→```
    80→tests/
    81→├── helpers.js                          # Shared helpers (waitForEngine, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard)
    82→├── smoke/
    83→│   └── app-loads.spec.js               # Page loads, engine init, 3 characters, 10 objects
    84→├── regression/
    85→│   ├── ai-decisions.spec.js            # AI LLM requests, valid action types, 30s interval, dialogue fields
    86→│   ├── character-movement.spec.js      # A* pathfinding, tile movement, bounds check
    87→│   ├── character-facing.spec.js        # FRG-244: facing toward objects + characters during talk
    88→│   │                                   #   (runs against local dev server at localhost:5173)
    89→│   ├── walk-animation.spec.js          # FRG-204: walk animation stutter/freeze fix
    90→│   │                                   #   moveTo resets walk state, large-delta frame accumulation,
    91→│   │                                   #   full 4-frame cycle, walkTimer remainder invariant
    92→│   │                                   #   (runs against local dev server at localhost:5174)
    93→│   ├── contextual-interaction-animations.spec.js  # FRG-243: contextual anim per object type,
    94→│   │                                   #   spriteKey/interactAnim during+after interaction, live sim,
    95→│   │                                   #   all 9 interaction sprites (runs against localhost:3000)
    96→│   ├── object-interaction.spec.js      # Object state transitions, debug panel
    97→│   ├── speech-bubbles.spec.js          # Dialogue lines, pendingSpeech, speech timer
    98→│   ├── m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js      # Debug page layout, real-time updates, pause/resume,
    99→│   │                                   #   position/facing/target fields, reset behavior, speed controls
   100→│   ├── m1-activity-duration.spec.js    # Activity timers, 30s AI cycle, action types
   101→│   ├── slot-system.spec.js             # Slot/capacity display, pip indicators, reservations,
   102→│   │                                   #   multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu/tv)
   103→│   ├── talk-handshaking.spec.js        # TALK/REJECT events, talking state, canTalk filter
   104→│   └── fallback-logic.spec.js          # FALLBACK events, plan changes, reservation release
   105→└── critical/
   106→    └── sim-loop-integrity.spec.js      # End-to-end: LLM → move → object change → no errors
   107→```
   108→
   109→---
   110→
   111→## Feature Coverage Map
   112→
   113→| M1 Feature | Spec File(s) |
   114→|------------|-------------|
   115→| Characters: Maple, Sol, Nyx | `smoke/app-loads.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
   116→| AI decisions every ~30s | `ai-decisions.spec.js`, `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` |
   117→| Action types: move, interact, idle, talk | `ai-decisions.spec.js`, `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` |
   118→| LLM response: dialogue + description fields | `ai-decisions.spec.js` |
   119→| A* pathfinding, bounds | `character-movement.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
   120→| Object state machines (idle → active → idle) | `object-interaction.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
   121→| Activity hold timers | `m1-activity-duration.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
   122→| Smart objects slot system (capacity, pips) | `slot-system.spec.js` |
   123→| Multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu, tv) | `slot-system.spec.js` |
   124→| Walk animation: moveTo reset, large-delta drain, 4-frame cycle | `walk-animation.spec.js` |
   125→| Character facing toward objects on interact | `character-facing.spec.js` |
   126→| Character facing toward each other on talk | `character-facing.spec.js` |
   127→| Contextual interaction animations (reading/sit/phone) | `contextual-interaction-animations.spec.js` |
   128→| Talk handshaking (TALK/REJECT events) | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` |
   129→| Fallback logic (FALLBACK events) | `fallback-logic.spec.js` |
   130→| Debug dashboard: layout + real-time | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
   131→| Debug dashboard: position, facing, target fields | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
   132→| Speed controls (functional 2x/4x verify) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
   133→| Reset button (behavior, not just visibility) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
   134→| Full sim loop integrity | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` |
   135→
   136→---
   137→
   138→## CI (GitHub Actions)
   139→
   140→Tests run automatically on every push and pull request via `.github/workflows/playwright.yml`.
   141→
   142→Results are stored as artifacts (JSON report + test-results folder).
   143→
   144→To run CI locally with the same config:
   145→
   146→```bash
   147→npm ci
   148→npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
   149→npx playwright test
   150→```
   151→
   152→---
   153→
   154→## Known Issues
   155→
   156→| Issue | File | Notes |
   157→|-------|------|-------|
   158→| `talk` action rarely observed in short runs | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | AI is non-deterministic; tests gracefully skip if not observed |
   159→| REJECT events are probabilistic | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | Only fires when two characters attempt simultaneous talk |
   160→| FALLBACK events require 30s+ observation | `fallback-logic.spec.js` | Events appear after AI cycle + object contention |
   161→| Sim clock ratio ~36x in headless (vs 60x in browser) | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` | RAF throttled in headless — documented, not a product bug |
   162→
   163→---
   164→
   165→## Test Results (last run: 2026-03-09, FRG-204 added)
   166→
   167→| Suite | Tests | Status |
   168→|-------|-------|--------|
   169→| Smoke | 8 | ✅ 8/8 |
   170→| Regression (ai-decisions) | 6 | ✅ 6/6 |
   171→| Regression (character-movement) | 4 | ✅ 4/4 |
   172→| Regression (character-facing) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
   173→| Regression (walk-animation) | 4 | ✅ 4/4 |
   174→| Regression (contextual-interaction-animations) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
   175→| Regression (object-interaction) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
   176→| Regression (speech-bubbles) | 4 | ✅ 4/4 |
   177→| Regression (m1-debug-dashboard) | 24 | ✅ 24/24 |
   178→| Regression (m1-activity-duration) | 10 | ✅ 10/10 |
   179→| Regression (slot-system) | 11 | ✅ 11/11 |
   180→| Regression (talk-handshaking) | 6 | ✅ 6/6 |
   181→| Regression (fallback-logic) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
   182→| Critical | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
   183→| **Total** | **102** | **✅ 102/102** |
   184→

139\u2192\n   140\u2192Tests run automatically on every push and pull request via `.github/workflows/playwright.yml`.\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192Results are stored as artifacts (JSON report + test-results folder).\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192To run CI locally with the same config:\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192```bash\n   147\u2192npm ci\n   148\u2192npx playwright install --with-deps chromium\n   149\u2192npx playwright test\n   150\u2192```\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192---\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192## Known Issues\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192| Issue | File | Notes |\n   157\u2192|-------|------|-------|\n   158\u2192| `talk` action rarely observed in short runs | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | AI is non-deterministic; tests gracefully skip if not observed |\n   159\u2192| REJECT events are probabilistic | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | Only fires when two characters attempt simultaneous talk |\n   160\u2192| FALLBACK events require 30s+ observation | `fallback-logic.spec.js` | Events appear after AI cycle + object contention |\n   161\u2192| Sim clock ratio ~36x in headless (vs 60x in browser) | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` | RAF throttled in headless \u2014 documented, not a product bug |\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192---\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192## Test Results (last run: 2026-03-09, FRG-204 added)\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192| Suite | Tests | Status |\n   168\u2192|-------|-------|--------|\n   169\u2192| Smoke | 8 | \u2705 8/8 |\n   170\u2192| Regression (ai-decisions) | 6 | \u2705 6/6 |\n   171\u2192| Regression (character-movement) | 4 | \u2705 4/4 |\n   172\u2192| Regression (character-facing) | 5 | \u2705 5/5 |\n   173\u2192| Regression (walk-animation) | 4 | \u2705 4/4 |\n   174\u2192| Regression (contextual-interaction-animations) | 5 | \u2705 5/5 |\n   175\u2192| Regression (object-interaction) | 5 | \u2705 5/5 |\n   176\u2192| Regression (speech-bubbles) | 4 | \u2705 4/4 |\n   177\u2192| Regression (m1-debug-dashboard) | 24 | \u2705 24/24 |\n   178\u2192| Regression (m1-activity-duration) | 10 | \u2705 10/10 |\n   179\u2192| Regression (slot-system) | 11 | \u2705 11/11 |\n   180\u2192| Regression (talk-handshaking) | 6 | \u2705 6/6 |\n   181\u2192| Regression (fallback-logic) | 5 | \u2705 5/5 |\n   182\u2192| Critical | 5 | \u2705 5/5 |\n   183\u2192| **Total** | **102** | **\u2705 102/102** |\n   184\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }

USERMESSAGE #19
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-204
     4→ * Walk animations stutter/freeze mid-movement due to timer accumulation issues.
     5→ *
     6→ * Root cause: walk frame timer was not using a while-loop, so large deltaMs values
     7→ * (e.g. when tab was backgrounded or frame rate dropped) only advanced walkFrame
     8→ * once per update() call, leaving walkTimer > walkFrameMs and causing the animation
     9→ * to stutter or freeze.
    10→ *
    11→ * Fix: advance walkFrame in a while-loop, consuming all accumulated intervals so
    12→ * walkTimer is always < walkFrameMs after each update().
    13→ *
    14→ * Tests run against the local dev server (http://localhost:5174) which serves
    15→ * the feature/frg-204-fix-walk-animation branch.
    16→ *
    17→ * The page does NOT expose window._gameEngine by default, so we intercept
    18→ * index.html via page.route() and inject the exposure before the script executes.
    19→ *
    20→ * Test layout:
    21→ *   1. moveTo resets walk state — walkFrame=0 and walkTimer=0 on new movement
    22→ *   2. Large deltaMs — update(1000) advances all 5 frames, walkTimer holds only remainder
    23→ *   3. moveTo resets dirty walk state — pre-set walkFrame/walkTimer are cleared on moveTo
    24→ *   4. Live sim regression — no character gets stuck on same walkFrame while walking for 30s
    25→ */
    26→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    27→
    28→const LOCAL_URL = 'http://localhost:5174';
    29→
    30→// walkFrameMs is 200ms per frame (4-frame cycle)
    31→const WALK_FRAME_MS = 200;
    32→const WALK_FRAME_COUNT = 4;
    33→
    34→// ── helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    35→
    36→/**
    37→ * Intercept index.html served at / and inject window._gameEngine = engine
    38→ * so tests can call page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.*).
    39→ */
    40→async function injectEngineExposure(page) {
    41→  await page.route('**/', async (route) => {
    42→    const response = await route.fetch();
    43→    let body = await response.text();
    44→    body = body.replace(
    45→      '// ── Initial state ──',
    46→      '// ── Initial state (QA injection) ──
window._gameEngine = engine;'
    47→    );
    48→    await route.fulfill({ response, body });
    49→  });
    50→}
    51→
    52→/**
    53→ * Click start overlay (if present) and set timeScale.
    54→ */
    55→async function startEngine(page, timeScale = 1) {
    56→  const overlay = page.locator('#start-overlay');
    57→  if (await overlay.isVisible()) {
    58→    await overlay.click();
    59→  }
    60→  if (timeScale !== 1) {
    61→    await page.evaluate((scale) => {
    62→      if (window._gameEngine) window._gameEngine.timeScale = scale;
    63→    }, timeScale);
    64→  }
    65→}
    66→
    67→/**
    68→ * Read walk state from all characters.
    69→ */
    70→async function getWalkStates(page) {
    71→  return page.evaluate(() =>
    72→    window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({
    73→      id: c.id,
    74→      action: c.action,
    75→      walkFrame: c.walkFrame,
    76→      walkTimer: c.walkTimer,
    77→      walkFrameMs: c.walkFrameMs,
    78→      tx: c.tx,
    79→      ty: c.ty,
    80→    }))
    81→  );
    82→}
    83→
    84→// ── Tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    85→
    86→test.describe('FRG-204: Walk Animation', () => {
    87→
    88→  // ── TEST 1: moveTo resets walk state ──────────────────────────────────────
    89→
    90→  test('moveTo resets walkFrame to 0 and walkTimer to 0', async ({ page }) => {
    91→    test.setTimeout(30_000);
    92→
    93→    await injectEngineExposure(page);
    94→    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);
    95→    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });
    96→
    97→    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {
    98→      const engine = window._gameEngine;
    99→      const nyx = engine.characters.find(c => c.id === 'nyx');
   100→
   101→      // Start from nyx's initial position in the apartment sector
   102→      nyx.tx = 3; nyx.ty = 4;
   103→      nyx.prevTx = 3; nyx.prevTy = 4;
   104→      nyx.action = 'idle';
   105→      nyx.path = [];
   106→      nyx.moveProgress = 0;
   107→
   108→      // Call moveTo and verify it resets walk state
   109→      const ok = nyx.moveTo(7, 4);
   110→
   111→      return {
   112→        moveToSucceeded: ok,
   113→        walkFrame: nyx.walkFrame,
   114→        walkTimer: nyx.walkTimer,
   115→        action: nyx.action,
   116→        pathLength: nyx.path.length,
   117→      };
   118→    });
   119→
   120→    console.log('moveTo result:', JSON.stringify(result));
   121→
   122→    expect(result.moveToSucceeded, 'moveTo should find a valid path').toBe(true);
   123→    expect(result.action, 'action should be walking after moveTo').toBe('walking');
   124→    expect(result.walkFrame, 'walkFrame should be 0 when movement starts').toBe(0);
   125→    expect(result.walkTimer, 'walkTimer should be 0 when movement starts').toBe(0);
   126→    expect(result.pathLength, 'path should not be empty after moveTo').toBeGreaterThan(0);
   127→  });
   128→
   129→  // ── TEST 2: Large deltaMs advances all frames without stutter ─────────────
   130→
   131→  test('update(1000ms) consumes all accumulated walk frames — walkTimer < walkFrameMs', async ({ page }) => {
   132→    test.setTimeout(30_000);
   133→
   134→    await injectEngineExposure(page);
   135→    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);
   136→    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });
   137→
   138→    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {
   139→      const engine = window._gameEngine;
   140→      const nyx = engine.characters.find(c => c.id === 'nyx');
   141→
   142→      // Place nyx at a known walkable position in the apartment and start walking
   143→      // with a long path (7 tiles) so movement doesn't complete within 1000ms
   144→      // (moveSpeed=2 tiles/s → 500ms/tile → 7 tiles = 3500ms to complete)
   145→      nyx.tx = 3;  nyx.ty = 4;
   146→      nyx.prevTx = 3; nyx.prevTy = 4;
   147→      nyx.path = [
   148→        { tx: 4, ty: 4 }, { tx: 5, ty: 4 }, { tx: 6, ty: 4 },
   149→        { tx: 7, ty: 4 }, { tx: 8, ty: 4 }, { tx: 9, ty: 4 }, { tx: 10, ty: 4 },
   150→      ];
   151→      nyx.action = 'walking';
   152→      nyx.walkFrame = 0;
   153→      nyx.walkTimer = 0;
   154→      nyx.moveProgress = 0;
   155→
   156→      const before = { walkFrame: nyx.walkFrame, walkTimer: nyx.walkTimer };
   157→
   158→      // Simulate 1000ms — at walkFrameMs=200 this is exactly 5 frame intervals
   159→      nyx.update(1000);
   160→
   161→      const after = { walkFrame: nyx.walkFrame, walkTimer: nyx.walkTimer, action: nyx.action };
   162→
   163→      // Expected frame advances: floor(1000/200) = 5
   164→      // Starting at frame 0: 0→1→2→3→0→1 → final walkFrame = (0+5)%4 = 1
   165→      // Remainder: 1000 - 5*200 = 0ms
   166→      const expectedFrameAdvances = Math.floor(1000 / 200);  // 5
   167→      const expectedWalkFrame = (0 + expectedFrameAdvances) % 4;  // 1
   168→      const expectedWalkTimerRemainder = 1000 % 200;  // 0
   169→
   170→      return { before, after, expectedWalkFrame, expectedWalkTimerRemainder };
   171→    });
   172→
   173→    console.log('Before update(1000):', JSON.stringify(result.before));
   174→    console.log('After  update(1000):', JSON.stringify(result.after));
   175→    console.log('Expected walkFrame:', result.expectedWalkFrame, '| Expected walkTimer remainder:', result.expectedWalkTimerRemainder);
   176→
   177→    // The critical assertion: walkTimer must be less than walkFrameMs (200ms)
   178→    // — if the bug were present, walkTimer would be 800ms (only 1 frame consumed)
   179→    expect(result.after.walkTimer,
   180→      `walkTimer should be < ${200}ms after consuming all intervals — got ${result.after.walkTimer}ms`
   181→    ).toBeLessThan(200);
   182→
   183→    // Verify the exact frame position (all 5 advances consumed)
   184→    expect(result.after.walkFrame,
   185→      `walkFrame should be ${result.expectedWalkFrame} after 5 frame advances (0→1→2→3→0→1)`
   186→    ).toBe(result.expectedWalkFrame);
   187→
   188→    // Character should still be walking (path not exhausted)
   189→    expect(result.after.action, 'character should still be walking after 1000ms on a long path').toBe('walking');
   190→  });
   191→
   192→  // ── TEST 3: moveTo resets dirty walk state ────────────────────────────────
   193→
   194→  test('moveTo clears non-zero walkFrame and walkTimer from previous movement', async ({ page }) => {
   195→    test.setTimeout(30_000);
   196→
   197→    await injectEngineExposure(page);
   198→    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);
   199→    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });
   200→
   201→    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {
   202→      const engine = window._gameEngine;
   203→      const nyx = engine.characters.find(c => c.id === 'nyx');
   204→
   205→      // Simulate mid-walk dirty state (as it would be during ongoing movement)
   206→      nyx.tx = 3; nyx.ty = 4;
   207→      nyx.prevTx = 3; nyx.prevTy = 4;
   208→      nyx.action = 'walking';
   209→      nyx.walkFrame = 3;   // non-zero
   210→      nyx.walkTimer = 150; // non-zero
   211→      nyx.path = [{ tx: 4, ty: 4 }];
   212→      nyx.moveProgress = 0.5;
   213→
   214→      const before = { walkFrame: nyx.walkFrame, walkTimer: nyx.walkTimer };
   215→
   216→      // Issue a new moveTo — this should reset walk state regardless of prior state
   217→      const ok = nyx.moveTo(8, 4);
   218→
   219→      const after = { walkFrame: nyx.walkFrame, walkTimer: nyx.walkTimer, action: nyx.action };
   220→
   221→      return { moveToSucceeded: ok, before, after };
   222→    });
   223→
   224→    console.log('Before moveTo (dirty state):', JSON.stringify(result.before));
   225→    console.log('After  moveTo:', JSON.stringify(result.after));
   226→
   227→    expect(result.moveToSucceeded, 'moveTo should find a valid path').toBe(true);
   228→    expect(result.after.walkFrame,
   229→      `walkFrame should reset to 0 — was ${result.before.walkFrame}`
   230→    ).toBe(0);
   231→    expect(result.after.walkTimer,
   232→      `walkTimer should reset to 0 — was ${result.before.walkTimer}ms`
   233→    ).toBe(0);
   234→  });
   235→
   236→  // ── TEST 4: Live sim — walkFrame cycles and walkTimer stays < walkFrameMs ──
   237→
   238→  test('walkFrame cycles through all 4 frames and walkTimer never accumulates above walkFrameMs', async ({ page }) => {
   239→    test.setTimeout(30_000);
   240→
   241→    await injectEngineExposure(page);
   242→    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);
   243→    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });
   244→
   245→    // Drive the sim via engine._updateCharacters() — the same method the live RAF loop calls.
   246→    // This avoids headless RAF throttling issues while still exercising the real engine path.
   247→    // We advance 5 seconds of sim time in 200ms steps (25 steps) — the same granularity as
   248→    // the walk frame interval — and observe walkFrame and walkTimer throughout.
   249→    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {
   250→      const engine = window._gameEngine;
   251→      const nyx = engine.characters.find(c => c.id === 'nyx');
   252→
   253→      // Place nyx at a known walkable apartment position with a 20-tile path.
   254→      // At moveSpeed=2, this takes 10 sim-seconds — far longer than our 5s observation window.
   255→      nyx.tx = 3;  nyx.ty = 4;
   256→      nyx.prevTx = 3; nyx.prevTy = 4;
   257→      nyx.path = Array.from({ length: 20 }, (_, i) => ({ tx: 4 + i, ty: 4 }));
   258→      nyx.action = 'walking';
   259→      nyx.walkFrame = 0;
   260→      nyx.walkTimer = 0;
   261→      nyx.moveProgress = 0;
   262→
   263→      const STEP_MS = 200;   // advance 200ms per step (= 1 walk frame interval)
   264→      const STEPS = 25;      // 25 * 200ms = 5 sim-seconds of walking
   265→
   266→      const seenFrames = new Set();
   267→      const timerViolations = []; // walkTimer >= walkFrameMs at any point after update()
   268→      const observations = [];
   269→
   270→      for (let i = 0; i < STEPS; i++) {
   271→        nyx.update(STEP_MS);
   272→        seenFrames.add(nyx.walkFrame);
   273→        observations.push({ walkFrame: nyx.walkFrame, walkTimer: nyx.walkTimer });
   274→
   275→        if (nyx.walkTimer >= nyx.walkFrameMs) {
   276→          timerViolations.push({ step: i, walkTimer: nyx.walkTimer, walkFrameMs: nyx.walkFrameMs });
   277→        }
   278→
   279→        // Stop early if nyx is no longer walking (shouldn't happen with 20-tile path)
   280→        if (nyx.action !== 'walking') break;
   281→      }
   282→
   283→      return {
   284→        seenFrames: [...seenFrames],
   285→        timerViolations,
   286→        observations: observations.slice(0, 8), // first 8 for logging
   287→        finalAction: nyx.action,
   288→        finalWalkTimer: nyx.walkTimer,
   289→        finalWalkFrame: nyx.walkFrame,
   290→      };
   291→    });
   292→
   293→    console.log('Unique walkFrames seen:', result.seenFrames.sort().join(', '));
   294→    console.log('Timer violations (walkTimer >= 200ms after update):', result.timerViolations.length);
   295→    console.log('First 8 observations:', result.observations.map(o =>
   296→      `f${o.walkFrame}/t${o.walkTimer}ms`
   297→    ).join(' → '));
   298→    console.log('Final state: action=%s walkFrame=%d walkTimer=%dms',
   299→      result.finalAction, result.finalWalkFrame, result.finalWalkTimer);
   300→
   301→    // Character should still be walking (20-tile path, only 5s simulated)
   302→    expect(result.finalAction, 'character should still be walking after 5 sim-seconds on 20-tile path').toBe('walking');
   303→
   304→    // All 4 walk frames must appear: 25 steps / 4 frames = 6.25 full cycles
   305→    expect(result.seenFrames.length,
   306→      `walkFrame must cycle through all 4 values (0–3), only saw: ${result.seenFrames.sort().join(', ')}`
   307→    ).toBe(4);
   308→
   309→    // walkTimer must never exceed walkFrameMs after an update() — this is the fix assertion
   310→    // (without the while-loop fix, walkTimer could be up to 1000ms after a large-delta update)
   311→    expect(result.timerViolations.length,
   312→      `walkTimer exceeded walkFrameMs (${WALK_FRAME_MS}ms) on ${result.timerViolations.length} step(s): ` +
   313→      JSON.stringify(result.timerViolations)
   314→    ).toBe(0);
   315→  });
   316→});
   317→

Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

{
  "content": {
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_01QJzQrNjyPyJncY1busRZ8M",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "     1\u2192'use strict';\n     2\u2192/**\n     3\u2192 * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-204\n     4\u2192 * Walk animations stutter/freeze mid-movement due to timer accumulation issues.\n     5\u2192 *\n     6\u2192 * Root cause: walk frame timer was not using a while-loop, so large deltaMs values\n     7\u2192 * (e.g. when tab was backgrounded or frame rate dropped) only advanced walkFrame\n     8\u2192 * once per update() call, leaving walkTimer > walkFrameMs and causing the animation\n     9\u2192 * to stutter or freeze.\n    10\u2192 *\n    11\u2192 * Fix: advance walkFrame in a while-loop, consuming all accumulated intervals so\n    12\u2192 * walkTimer is always < walkFrameMs after each update().\n    13\u2192 *\n    14\u2192 * Tests run against the local dev server (http://localhost:5174) which serves\n    15\u2192 * the feature/frg-204-fix-walk-animation branch.\n    16\u2192 *\n    17\u2192 * The page does NOT expose window._gameEngine by default, so we intercept\n    18\u2192 * index.html via page.route() and inject the exposure before the script executes.\n    19\u2192 *\n    20\u2192 * Test layout:\n    21\u2192 *   1. moveTo resets walk state \u2014 walkFrame=0 and walkTimer=0 on new movement\n    22\u2192 *   2. Large deltaMs \u2014 update(1000) advances all 5 frames, walkTimer holds only remainder\n    23\u2192 *   3. moveTo resets dirty walk state \u2014 pre-set walkFrame/walkTimer are cleared on moveTo\n    24\u2192 *   4. Live sim regression \u2014 no character gets stuck on same walkFrame while walking for 30s\n    25\u2192 */\n    26\u2192const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192const LOCAL_URL = 'http://localhost:5174';\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192// walkFrameMs is 200ms per frame (4-frame cycle)\n    31\u2192const WALK_FRAME_MS = 200;\n    32\u2192const WALK_FRAME_COUNT = 4;\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192// \u2500\u2500 helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192/**\n    37\u2192 * Intercept index.html served at / and inject window._gameEngine = engine\n    38\u2192 * so tests can call page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.*).\n    39\u2192 */\n    40\u2192async function injectEngineExposure(page) {\n    41\u2192  await page.route('**/', async (route) => {\n    42\u2192    const response = await route.fetch();\n    43\u2192    let body = await response.text();\n    44\u2192    body = body.replace(\n    45\u2192      '// \u2500\u2500 Initial state \u2500\u2500',\n    46\u2192      '// \u2500\u2500 Initial state (QA injection) \u2500\u2500\\nwindow._gameEngine = engine;'\n    47\u2192    );\n    48\u2192    await route.fulfill({ response, body });\n    49\u2192  });\n    50\u2192}\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192/**\n    53\u2192 * Click start overlay (if present) and set timeScale.\n    54\u2192 */\n    55\u2192async function startEngine(page, timeScale = 1) {\n    56\u2192  const overlay = page.locator('#start-overlay');\n    57\u2192  if (await overlay.isVisible()) {\n    58\u2192    await overlay.click();\n    59\u2192  }\n    60\u2192  if (timeScale !== 1) {\n    61\u2192    await page.evaluate((scale) => {\n    62\u2192      if (window._gameEngine) window._gameEngine.timeScale = scale;\n    63\u2192    }, timeScale);\n    64\u2192  }\n    65\u2192}\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192/**\n    68\u2192 * Read walk state from all characters.\n    69\u2192 */\n    70\u2192async function getWalkStates(page) {\n    71\u2192  return page.evaluate(() =>\n    72\u2192    window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({\n    73\u2192      id: c.id,\n    74\u2192      action: c.action,\n    75\u2192      walkFrame: c.walkFrame,\n    76\u2192      walkTimer: c.walkTimer,\n    77\u2192      walkFrameMs: c.walkFrameMs,\n    78\u2192      tx: c.tx,\n    79\u2192      ty: c.ty,\n    80\u2192    }))\n    81\u2192  );\n    82\u2192}\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Tests \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192test.describe('FRG-204: Walk Animation', () => {\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 1: moveTo resets walk state \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192  test('moveTo resets walkFrame to 0 and walkTimer to 0', async ({ page }) => {\n    91\u2192    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    94\u2192    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    95\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    98\u2192      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n    99\u2192      const nyx = engine.characters.find(c => c.id === 'nyx');\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192      // Start from nyx's initial position in the apartment sector\n   102\u2192      nyx.tx = 3; nyx.ty = 4;\n   103\u2192      nyx.prevTx = 3; nyx.prevTy = 4;\n   104\u2192      nyx.action = 'idle';\n   105\u2192      nyx.path = [];\n   106\u2192      nyx.moveProgress = 0;\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192      // Call moveTo and verify it resets walk state\n   109\u2192      const ok = nyx.moveTo(7, 4);\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192      return {\n   112\u2192        moveToSucceeded: ok,\n   113\u2192        walkFrame: nyx.walkFrame,\n   114\u2192        walkTimer: result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192    expect(result.moveToSucceeded, 'moveTo should find a valid path').toBe(true);\n   123\u2192    expect(result.action, 'action should be walking after moveTo').toBe('walking');\n   124\u2192    expect(result.walkFrame, 'walkFrame should be 0 when movement starts').toBe(0);\n   125\u2192    expect(result.walkTimer, 'walkTimer should be 0 when movement starts').toBe(0);\n   126\u2192    expect(result.pathLength, 'path should not be empty after moveTo').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   127\u2192  });\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 2: Large deltaMs advances all frames without stutter \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192  test('update(1000ms) consumes all accumulated walk frames \u2014 walkTimer < walkFrameMs', async ({ page }) => {\n   132\u2192    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n   135\u2192    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n   136\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n   139\u2192      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n   140\u2192      const nyx = engine.characters.find(c => c.id === 'nyx');\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192      // Place nyx at a known walkable position in the apartment and start walking\n   143\u2192      // with a long path (7 tiles) so movement doesn't complete within 1000ms\n   144\u2192      // (moveSpeed=2 tiles/s \u2192 500ms/tile \u2192 7 tiles = 3500ms to complete)\n   145\u2192      nyx.tx = 3;  nyx.ty = 4;\n   146\u2192      nyx.prevTx = 3; nyx.prevTy = 4;\n   147\u2192      nyx.path = [\n   148\u2192        { tx: 4, ty: 4 }, { tx: 5, ty: 4 }, { tx: 6, ty: 4 },\n   149\u2192        { tx: 7, ty: 4 }, { tx: 8, ty: 4 }, { tx: 9, ty: 4 }, { tx: 10, ty: 4 },\n   150\u2192      ];\n   151\u2192      nyx.action = 'walking';\n   152\u2192      nyx.walkFrame = 0;\n   153\u2192      nyx.walkTimer = 0;\n   154\u2192      nyx.moveProgress = 0;\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192      const before = { walkFrame: nyx.walkFrame, walkTimer: nyx.walkTimer };\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192      // Simulate 1000ms \u2014 at walkFrameMs=200 this is exactly 5 frame intervals\n   159\u2192      nyx.update(1000);\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192      const after = { walkFrame: nyx.walkFrame, walkTimer: nyx.walkTimer, action: nyx.action };\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192      // Expected frame advances: floor(1000/200) = 5\n   164\u2192      // Starting at frame 0: 0\u21921\u21922\u21923\u21920\u21921 \u2192 final walkFrame = (0+5)%4 = 1\n   165\u2192      // Remainder: 1000 - 5*200 = 0ms\n   166\u2192      const expectedFrameAdvances = Math.floor(1000 / 200);  // 5\n   167\u2192      const expectedWalkFrame = (0 + expectedFrameAdvances) % 4;  // 1\n   168\u2192      const expectedWalkTimerRemainder = 1000 % 200;  // 0\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192      return { before, after, expectedWalkFrame, expectedWalkTimerRemainder };\n   171\u2192    });\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192    console.log('Before update(1000):', JSON.stringify(result.before));\n   174\u2192    console.log('After  update(1000):', JSON.stringify(result.after));\n   175\u2192    console.log('Expected walkFrame:', result.expectedWalkFrame, '| Expected walkTimer remainder:', result.expectedWalkTimerRemainder);\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192    // The critical assertion: walkTimer must be less than walkFrameMs (200ms)\n   178\u2192    // \u2014 if the bug were present, walkTimer would be 800ms (only 1 frame consumed)\n   179\u2192    expect(result.after.walkTimer,\n   180\u2192      `walkTimer should be < ${200}ms after consuming all intervals \u2014 got ${result.after.walkTimer}ms`\n   181\u2192    ).toBeLessThan(200);\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192    // Verify the exact frame position (all 5 advances consumed)\n   184\u2192    expect(result.after.walkFrame,\n   185\u2192      `walkFrame should be ${result.expectedWalkFrame} after 5 frame advances (0\u21921\u21922\u21923\u21920\u21921)`\n   186\u2192    ).toBe(result.expectedWalkFrame);\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192    // Character should still be walking (path not exhausted)\n   189\u2192    expect(result.after.action, 'character should still be walking after 1000ms on a long path').toBe('walking');\n   190\u2192  });\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 3: moveTo resets dirty walk state \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n 238\u2192  test('walkFrame cycles through all 4 frames and walkTimer never accumulates above walkFrameMs', async ({ page }) => {\n   239\u2192    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192 walkFrames seen:', result.seenFrames.sort().join(', '));\n   294\u2192    console.log('Timer violations (walkTimer >= 200ms after update):', result.timerViolations.length);\n   295\u2192    console.log('First 8 observations:', result.observations.map(o =>\n   296\u2192      `f${o.walkFrame}/t${o.walkTimer}ms`\n   297\u2192    ).join(' \u2192 '));\n   298\u2192    console.log('Final state: action=%s walkFrame=%d walkTimer=%dms',\n   299\u2192      result.finalAction, result.finalWalkFrame, result.finalWalkTimer);\n   300\u2192\n   301\u2192    // Character should still be walking (20-tile path, only 5s simulated)\n   302\u2192    expect(result.finalAction, 'character should still be walking after 5 sim-seconds on 20-tile path').toBe('walking');\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192    // All 4 walk frames must appear: 25 steps / 4 frames = 6.25 full cycles\n   305\u2192    expect(result.seenFrames.length,\n   306\u2192      `walkFrame must cycle through all 4 values (0\u20133), only saw: ${result.seenFrames.sort().join(', ')}`\n   307\u2192    ).toBe(4);\n   308\u2192\n   309\u2192    // walkTimer must never exceed walkFrameMs after an update() \u2014 this is the fix assertion\n   310\u2192    // (without the while-loop fix, walkTimer could be up to 1000ms after a large-delta update)\n   311\u2192    expect(result.timerViolations.length,\n   312\u2192      `walkTimer exceeded walkFrameMs (${WALK_FRAME_MS}ms) on ${result.timerViolations.length} step(s): ` +\n   313\u2192      JSON.stringify(result.timerViolations)\n   314\u2192    ).toBe(0);\n   315\u2192  });\n   316\u2192});\n   317\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}

{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/character-facing.spec.js"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_019MZB7rHprX51wRaRh6wBRq",
    "name": "Read",

    },

USERMESSAGE #21
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-244
     4→ * Characters face toward objects when interacting (and toward each other when talking).
     5→ *
     6→ * Tests run against the local dev server (http://localhost:5173) because this
     7→ * feature lives in the feature/frg-244 branch, not yet deployed to Vercel.
     8→ *
     9→ * The page is served as a static file and does NOT expose window._gameEngine
    10→ * by default, so we intercept index.html via page.route() and inject the
    11→ * exposure before the script executes.
    12→ *
    13→ * Test layout:
    14→ *   1. Object-facing regression — character faces the object it interacts with
    15→ *   2. Talk facing (direct) — facing logic is correct for character-to-character
    16→ *   3. Talk arrival facing — when a character walks to another and arrives, both face each other
    17→ */
    18→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    19→
    20→const LOCAL_URL = 'http://localhost:5173';
    21→
    22→// ── helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    23→
    24→/**
    25→ * Intercept index.html served at / and inject window._gameEngine = engine
    26→ * so tests can call page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.*).
    27→ */
    28→async function injectEngineExposure(page) {
    29→  await page.route('**/', async (route) => {
    30→    const response = await route.fetch();
    31→    let body = await response.text();
    32→    // Inject just before "// ── Initial state ──" at the bottom of the module script
    33→    body = body.replace(
    34→      '// ── Initial state ──',
    35→      '// ── Initial state (QA injection) ──
window._gameEngine = engine;'
    36→    );
    37→    await route.fulfill({ response, body });
    38→  });
    39→}
    40→
    41→/**
    42→ * Start the engine — click the overlay (click-to-start) then boost speed.
    43→ */
    44→async function startEngine(page, timeScale = 20) {
    45→  // Click the overlay to start the sim
    46→  const overlay = page.locator('#start-overlay');
    47→  if (await overlay.isVisible()) {
    48→    await overlay.click();
    49→  }
    50→
    51→  // Boost speed for faster interactions
    52→  await page.evaluate((scale) => {
    53→    if (window._gameEngine) window._gameEngine.timeScale = scale;
    54→  }, timeScale);
    55→}
    56→
    57→/**
    58→ * Poll engine snapshot until predicate returns truthy.
    59→ */
    60→async function pollSnapshot(page, predicate, timeoutMs = 45_000, intervalMs = 300) {
    61→  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;
    62→  while (Date.now() < deadline) {
    63→    const snap = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.snapshot());
    64→    if (predicate(snap)) return snap;
    65→    await page.waitForTimeout(intervalMs);
    66→  }
    67→  throw new Error(`pollSnapshot timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`);
    68→}
    69→
    70→/**
    71→ * Given a character (tx, ty) and an object position ({tx, ty, tw?, th?}),
    72→ * return the expected facing direction the character should have.
    73→ */
    74→function expectedFacingTowardObject(charTx, charTy, objPos) {
    75→  const objCx = objPos.tx + (objPos.tw || 1) / 2;
    76→  const objCy = objPos.ty + (objPos.th || 1) / 2;
    77→  const dx = objCx - charTx;
    78→  const dy = objCy - charTy;
    79→  if (Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)) {
    80→    return dx > 0 ? 'right' : 'left';
    81→  } else {
    82→    return dy > 0 ? 'down' : 'up';
    83→  }
    84→}
    85→
    86→/**
    87→ * Given two tile positions, return the expected facing direction from pos1 → pos2.
    88→ */
    89→function expectedFacingTowardChar(fromTx, fromTy, toTx, toTy) {
    90→  const dx = toTx - fromTx;
    91→  const dy = toTy - fromTy;
    92→  if (Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)) {
    93→    return dx > 0 ? 'right' : 'left';
    94→  } else {
    95→    return dy > 0 ? 'down' : 'up';
    96→  }
    97→}
    98→
    99→// ── Tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   100→
   101→test.describe('FRG-244: Character Facing', () => {
   102→  // ── TEST 1: Object facing regression ──────────────────────────────────────
   103→
   104→  test('character faces toward the object it interacts with', async ({ page }) => {
   105→    test.setTimeout(90_000);
   106→
   107→    await injectEngineExposure(page);
   108→    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);
   109→
   110→    // Wait for engine to be exposed
   111→    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });
   112→    await startEngine(page, 20);
   113→
   114→    // Poll until any character is in 'interacting' state with a targetObjectId
   115→    const snap = await pollSnapshot(
   116→      page,
   117→      s => s.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting' && c.targetObjectId),
   118→      60_000
   119→    );
   120→
   121→    const interacting = snap.characters.filter(c => c.action === 'interacting' && c.targetObjectId);
   122→    expect(interacting.length, 'At least one character should be interacting with an object').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
   123→
   124→    for (const char of interacting) {
   125→      // Get the object's position from the engine
   126→      const objPos = await page.evaluate((objId) => {
   127→        const obj = window._gameEngine.objectEngine.get(objId);
   128→        return obj ? { tx: obj.position.tx, ty: obj.position.ty, tw: obj.position.tw, th: obj.position.th } : null;
   129→      }, char.targetObjectId);
   130→
   131→      expect(objPos, `Object ${char.targetObjectId} should exist`).not.toBeNull();
   132→
   133→      const expected = expectedFacingTowardObject(char.tx, char.ty, objPos);
   134→
   135→      console.log(
   136→        `${char.name} at (${char.tx},${char.ty}) → ${char.targetObjectId} at ` +
   137→        `(${objPos.tx},${objPos.ty}): facing=${char.facing}, expected=${expected}`
   138→      );
   139→
   140→      expect(char.facing,
   141→        `${char.name} should face ${expected} toward ${char.targetObjectId} but is facing ${char.facing}`
   142→      ).toBe(expected);
   143→    }
   144→  });
   145→
   146→  // ── TEST 2: Talk facing — direct engine manipulation ──────────────────────
   147→
   148→  test('_updateFacing correctly orients a character toward another character', async ({ page }) => {
   149→    test.setTimeout(30_000);
   150→
   151→    await injectEngineExposure(page);
   152→    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);
   153→    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });
   154→
   155→    // _updateFacing(dx, dy): dx is checked first (horizontal priority), then dy only if dx===0.
   156→    // This is by design — the engine resolves the dominant axis BEFORE calling _updateFacing
   157→    // for object interactions, but character-to-character facing passes raw deltas directly,
   158→    // so horizontal direction takes priority when both dx and dy are non-zero.
   159→    const facingResults = await page.evaluate(() => {
   160→      const chars = window._gameEngine.characters;
   161→      const maple = chars.find(c => c.id === 'maple');
   162→
   163→      const results = [];
   164→
   165→      const testCases = [
   166→        // Pure cardinal directions (dx or dy === 0)
   167→        { name: 'right (pure horizontal)',    dx:  3, dy:  0, expected: 'right' },
   168→        { name: 'left (pure horizontal)',     dx: -3, dy:  0, expected: 'left'  },
   169→        { name: 'down (pure vertical)',       dx:  0, dy:  3, expected: 'down'  },
   170→        { name: 'up (pure vertical)',         dx:  0, dy: -3, expected: 'up'    },
   171→        // Both non-zero: horizontal (dx) takes priority in _updateFacing
   172→        { name: 'right wins over down',       dx:  1, dy:  3, expected: 'right' },
   173→        { name: 'left wins over up',          dx: -1, dy: -3, expected: 'left'  },
   174→      ];
   175→
   176→      for (const tc of testCases) {
   177→        maple._updateFacing(tc.dx, tc.dy);
   178→        results.push({ case: tc.name, expected: tc.expected, actual: maple.facing });
   179→      }
   180→
   181→      return results;
   182→    });
   183→
   184→    for (const result of facingResults) {
   185→      console.log(`Facing case "${result.case}": expected=${result.expected}, actual=${result.actual}`);
   186→      expect(result.actual, `Facing case "${result.case}" failed`).toBe(result.expected);
   187→    }
   188→  });
   189→
   190→  // ── TEST 3: Talk arrival — characters face each other when one arrives ─────
   191→
   192→  test('characters face each other when one arrives near the other for conversation', async ({ page }) => {
   193→    test.setTimeout(30_000);
   194→
   195→    await injectEngineExposure(page);
   196→    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);
   197→    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });
   198→
   199→    // Simulate the arrival handshake: set maple walking with a path to her destination,
   200→    // set targetCharId = sol, then advance the engine enough to trigger arrival.
   201→    // _updateCharacters checks: wasWalking && char.action === 'idle' (after update)
   202→    // → if targetCharId set: face each other and clear targetCharId.
   203→    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {
   204→      const engine = window._gameEngine;
   205→      const chars = engine.characters;
   206→      const maple = chars.find(c => c.id === 'maple');
   207→      const sol   = chars.find(c => c.id === 'sol');
   208→
   209→      // Position sol to the right of maple's destination
   210→      sol.tx   = 8;  sol.ty   = 5;
   211→
   212→      // Set maple walking with a single-tile path (moveSpeed=2 tiles/s → 500ms/tile)
   213→      maple.tx       = 4;  maple.ty = 5;
   214→      maple.prevTx   = 4;  maple.prevTy = 5;
   215→      maple.action   = 'walking';
   216→      maple.path     = [{ tx: 5, ty: 5 }];  // destination tile
   217→      maple.moveProgress = 0;
   218→      maple.targetCharId = sol.id;
   219→
   220→      // Reset facings to neutral so we can see the change
   221→      maple.facing = 'down';
   222→      sol.facing   = 'left';
   223→
   224→      // Advance 1000ms (2 tile periods) → maple's path clears → action becomes 'idle'
   225→      // then arrival handler fires: face each other, clear targetCharId
   226→      engine._updateCharacters(1000);
   227→
   228→      return {
   229→        maple: { facing: maple.facing, tx: maple.tx, ty: maple.ty },
   230→        sol:   { facing: sol.facing,   tx: sol.tx,   ty: sol.ty   },
   231→        targetCharIdCleared: maple.targetCharId === null,
   232→      };
   233→    });
   234→
   235→    console.log('After arrival, maple facing:', result.maple.facing, '| sol facing:', result.sol.facing);
   236→    console.log('targetCharId cleared:', result.targetCharIdCleared);
   237→
   238→    // Maple is at x=5, sol at x=8 → maple should face right, sol should face left
   239→    expect(result.maple.facing, 'Maple should face right (toward Sol)').toBe('right');
   240→    expect(result.sol.facing,   'Sol should face left (toward Maple)').toBe('left');
   241→    expect(result.targetCharIdCleared, 'targetCharId should be cleared after arrival').toBe(true);
   242→  });
   243→
   244→  // ── TEST 4: Facing toward object when already adjacent (no walk needed) ────
   245→
   246→  test('character faces object immediately when already adjacent at wander time', async ({ page }) => {
   247→    test.setTimeout(30_000);
   248→
   249→    await injectEngineExposure(page);
   250→    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);
   251→    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });
   252→
   253→    // Directly invoke _doInteraction to verify facing is applied at that call site.
   254→    // Uses cafe_espresso (tx=2, ty=11) — a single-tile object in this branch.
   255→    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {
   256→      const engine = window._gameEngine;
   257→      const chars  = engine.characters;
   258→      const maple  = chars.find(c => c.id === 'maple');
   259→
   260→      // Use cafe_espresso (tx=2, ty=11) — exists in feature/frg-244 world layout
   261→      const espresso = engine.objectEngine.get('cafe_espresso');
   262→      if (!espresso) return { error: 'cafe_espresso not found' };
   263→
   264→      // Place maple below the espresso so vertical (dy) delta dominates after pre-resolution:
   265→      // objCx = 2 + 0.5 = 2.5, objCy = 11 + 0.5 = 11.5
   266→      // maple at (2, 14): dx = 2.5-2 = 0.5, dy = 11.5-14 = -2.5 → |dy| > |dx| → face up
   267→      maple.tx = 2;
   268→      maple.ty = 14;
   269→      maple.facing = 'right'; // Wrong facing — should be corrected to 'up'
   270→
   271→      // Reset espresso to idle so the interaction succeeds
   272→      espresso.state = 'idle';
   273→
   274→      // Call the interaction handler directly — this is what happens when a char arrives at object
   275→      engine._doInteraction(maple, espresso, 'use');
   276→
   277→      const objCx = espresso.position.tx + (espresso.position.tw || 1) / 2;
   278→      const objCy = espresso.position.ty + (espresso.position.th || 1) / 2;
   279→      const dx = objCx - maple.tx;
   280→      const dy = objCy - maple.ty;
   281→      const expectedFacing = Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)
   282→        ? (dx > 0 ? 'right' : 'left')
   283→        : (dy > 0 ? 'down' : 'up');
   284→
   285→      return {
   286→        maplePos: { tx: maple.tx, ty: maple.ty },
   287→        objPos:   { tx: espresso.position.tx, ty: espresso.position.ty },
   288→        facing:   maple.facing,
   289→        expected: expectedFacing,
   290→      };
   291→    });
   292→
   293→    if (result.error) {
   294→      console.log('[SKIP]', result.error);
   295→      return;
   296→    }
   297→
   298→    console.log(
   299→      `Maple at (${result.maplePos.tx},${result.maplePos.ty}) → ` +
   300→      `espresso at (${result.objPos.tx},${result.objPos.ty}): ` +
   301→      `facing=${result.facing}, expected=${result.expected}`
   302→    );
   303→
   304→    expect(result.facing, `Maple should face ${result.expected} toward espresso`).toBe(result.expected);
   305→  });
   306→
   307→  // ── TEST 5: Talk already-nearby — face each other immediately (AI path) ────
   308→
   309→  test('characters face each other immediately when already close (AI talk path)', async ({ page }) => {
   310→    test.setTimeout(30_000);
   311→
   312→    await injectEngineExposure(page);
   313→    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);
   314→    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });
   315→
   316→    // Simulate the AI branch where dist <= 2 → face each other immediately
   317→    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {
   318→      const engine = window._gameEngine;
   319→      const chars  = engine.characters;
   320→      const nyx   = chars.find(c => c.id === 'nyx');
   321→      const maple  = chars.find(c => c.id === 'maple');
   322→
   323→      // Place them 1 tile apart (dist ≤ 2 → should face immediately)
   324→      nyx.tx   = 10; nyx.ty   = 5;
   325→      maple.tx = 10; maple.ty = 6;
   326→
   327→      nyx.facing   = 'left';  // Wrong — should be corrected
   328→      maple.facing = 'right'; // Wrong — should be corrected
   329→
   330→      // Simulate the already-nearby branch in ai.js:
   331→      //   char._updateFacing(target.tx - char.tx, target.ty - char.ty);
   332→      //   target._updateFacing(char.tx - target.tx, char.ty - target.ty);
   333→      nyx._updateFacing(maple.tx - nyx.tx, maple.ty - nyx.ty);
   334→      maple._updateFacing(nyx.tx - maple.tx, nyx.ty - maple.ty);
   335→
   336→      return {
   337→        nyx:   { facing: nyx.facing   },
   338→        maple: { facing: maple.facing },
   339→      };
   340→    });
   341→
   342→    // nyx is at y=5, maple at y=6 → nyx should face down (toward maple), maple should face up (toward nyx)
   343→    console.log('Nyx facing:', result.nyx.facing, '| Maple facing:', result.maple.facing);
   344→    expect(result.nyx.facing,   'Nyx should face down toward Maple').toBe('down');
   345→    expect(result.maple.facing, 'Maple should face up toward Nyx').toBe('up');
   346→  });
   347→});
   348→

    "content": "     1\u2192'use strict';\n     2\u2192/**\n     3\u2192 * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-244\n     4\u2192 * Characters face toward objects when interacting (and toward each other when talking).\n     5\u2192 *\n     6\u2192 * Tests run against the local dev server (http://localhost:5173) because this\n     7\u2192 * feature lives in the feature/frg-244 branch, not yet deployed to Vercel.\n     8\u2192 *\n     9\u2192 * The page is served as a static file and does NOT expose window._gameEngine\n    10\u2192 * by default, so we intercept index.html via page.route() and inject the\n    11\u2192 * exposure before the script executes.\n    12\u2192 *\n    13\u2192 * Test layout:\n    14\u2192 *   1.    23\u2192\n    24\u2192/**\n    25\u2192 * Intercept index.html served at / and inject window._gameEngine = engine\n    26\u2192 * so tests can call page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.*).\n    57\u2192/**\n    58\u2192 * Poll engine snapshot until predicate returns truthy.\n    59\u2192 */\n    60\u2192async function pollSnapshot(page, predicate, timeoutMs = 45_000, intervalMs = 300) {\n    61\u2192  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;\n    62\u2192  while (Date.now() < deadline) {\n    63\u2192    const snap = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.snapshot());\n    64\u2192    if (predicate(snap)) return snap;\n    65\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(intervalMs);\n    66\u2192  }\n    67\u2192  throw new Error(`pollSnapshot timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`);\n    68\u2192}\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192/**\n    71\u2192 * Given a character (tx, ty) and an object position ({tx, ty, tw?, th?}),\n    72\u2192 * return the expected facing direction the character should have.\n    73\u2192 */\n    74\u2192function expectedFacingTowardObject(charTx, charTy, objPos) {\n    75\u2192  const objCx = objPos.tx + (objPos.tw || 1) / 2;\n    76\u2192  const objCy = objPos.ty + (objPos.th || 1) / 2;\n    77\u2192  const dx = objCx - charTx;\n    78\u2192  const dy = objCy - charTy;\n    79\u2192  if (Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)) {\n    80\u2192    return dx > 0 ? 'right' : 'left';\n    81\u2192  } else {\n    82\u2192    return dy > 0 ? 'down' : 'up';\n    83\u2192  }\n    84\u2192}\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192/**\n    87\u2192 * Given two tile positions, return the expected facing direction from pos1 \u2192 pos2.\n    88\u2192 */\n    89\u2192function expectedFacingTowardChar(fromTx, fromTy, toTx, toTy) {\n    90\u2192  const dx = toTx - fromTx;\n    91\u2192  const dy = toTy - fromTy;\n    92\u2192  if (Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)) {\n    93\u2192    return dx > 0 ? 'right' : 'left';\n    94\u2192  } else {\n    95\u2192    return dy > 0 ? 'down' : 'up';\n    96\u2192  }\n    97\u2192}\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Tests \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192test.describe('FRG-244: Character Facing', () => {\n   102\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 1: Object facing regression \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192  test('character faces toward the object it interacts with', async ({ page }) => {\n   105\u2192    test.setTimeout(90_000);\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n   108\u2192    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192    // Wait for engine to be exposed\n   111\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n   112\u2192    await startEngine(page, 20);\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192    // Poll until any character is in 'interacting' state with a targetObjectId\n   115\u2192    const snap = await pollSnapshot(\n   116\u2192      page,\n   117\u2192      s => s.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting' && c.targetObjectId),\n   118\u2192      60_000\n   119\u2192    );\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192    const interacting = snap.characters.filter(c => c.action === 'interacting' && c.targetObjectId);\n   122\u2192    expect(interacting.length, 'At least one character should be interacting with an object').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192    for (const char of interacting) {\n   125\u2192      // Get the object's position from the engine\n   126\u2192      const objPos = await page.evaluate((objId) => {\n   127\u2192        const obj = window._gameEngine.objectEngine.get(objId);\n   128\u2192        return obj ? { tx: obj.position.tx, ty: obj.position.ty, tw: obj.position.tw, th: obj.position.th } : null;\n   129\u2192      }, char.targetObjectId);\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192      expect(objPos, `Object ${char.targetObjectId} should exist`).not.toBeNull();\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192      const expected = expectedFacingTowardObject(char.tx, char.ty, objPos);\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192      console.log(\n   136\u2192        `${char.name} at (${char.tx},${char.ty}) \u2192 ${char.targetObjectId} at ` +\n   137\u2192        `(${objPos.tx},${objPos.ty}): facing=${char.facing}, expected=${expected}`\n   138\u2192      );\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192      expect(char.facing,\n   141\u2192        `${char.name} should face ${expected} toward ${char.targetObjectId} but is facing ${char.facing}`\n   142\u2192      ).toBe(expected);\n   143\u2192    }\n   144\u2192  });\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 2: Talk facing \u2014 direct engine manipulation \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192  test('_updateFacing correctly orients a character toward another character', async ({ page }) => {\n   149\u2192    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n   152\u2192    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n   153\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192    // _updateFacing(dx, dy): dx is checked first (horizontal priority), then dy only if dx===0.\n   156\u2192    // This is by design \u2014 the engine resolves the dominant axis BEFORE calling _updateFacing\n   157\u2192    // for object interactions, but character-to-character facing passes raw deltas directly,\n   158\u2192    // so horizontal direction takes priority when both dx and dy are non-zero.\n   159\u2192    const facingResults = await page.evaluate(() => {\n   160\u2192      const chars = window._gameEngine.characters;\n   161\u2192      const maple = chars.find(c => c.id === 'maple');\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192      const results = [];\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192      const testCases = [\n   166\u2192        // Pure cardinal directions (dx or dy === 0)\n   167\u2192        { name: 'right (pure horizontal)',    dx:  3, dy:  0, expected: 'right' },\n   168\u2192        { name: 'left (pure horizontal)',     dx: -3, dy:  0, expected: 'left'  },\n   169\u2192        { name: 'down (pure vertical)',       dx:  0, dy:  3, expected: 'down'  },\n   170\u2192        { name: 'up (pure vertical)',         dx:  0, dy: -3, expected: 'up'    },\n   171\u2192        // Both non-zero: horizontal (dx) takes priority in _updateFacing\n   172\u2192        { name: 'right wins over down',       dx:  1, dy:  3, expected: 'right' },\n   173\u2192        { name: 'left wins over up',          dx: -1, dy: -3, expected: 'left'  },\n   174\u2192      ];\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192      for (const tc of testCases) {\n   177\u2192        maple._updateFacing(tc.dx, tc.dy);\n   178\u2192        results.push({ case: tc.name, expected: tc.expected, actual: maple.facing });\n   179\u2192      }\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192      return results;\n   182\u2192    });\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192    for (const result of facingResults) {\n   185\u2192      console.log(`Facing case \"${result.case}\": expected=${result.expected}, actual=${result.actual}`);\n   186\u2192      expect(result.actual, `Facing case \"${result.case}\" failed`).toBe(result.expected);\n   187\u2192    }\n   188\u2192  });\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 3: Talk arrival \u2014 characters face each other when one arrives \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192  test('characters face each other when one arrives near the other for conversation', async ({ page }) => {\n   193\u2192    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n   194\u2192\n   195\u2192    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n   196\u2192    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n   197\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192    // Simulate the arrival handshake: set maple walking with a path to her destination,\n   200\u2192    // set targetCharId = sol, then advance the engine enough to trigger arrival.\n   201\u2192    // _updateCharacters checks: wasWalking && char.action === 'idle' (after update)\n   202\u2192    // \u2192 if targetCharId set: face each other and clear targetCharId.\n   203\u2192    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n   204\u2192      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n   205\u2192      const chars = engine.characters;\n   206\u2192      const maple = chars.find(c => c.id === 'maple');\n   207\u2192      const sol   = chars.find(c => c.id === 'sol');\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192      // Position sol to the right of maple's destination\n   210\u2192      sol.tx   = 8;  sol.ty   = 5;\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192      // Set maple walking with a single-tile path (moveSpeed=2 tiles/s \u2192 500ms/tile)\n   213\u2192      maple.tx       = 4;  maple.ty = 5;\n   214\u2192      maple.prevTx   = 4;  maple.prevTy = 5;\n   215\u2192      maple.action   = 'walking';\n   216\u2192      maple.path     = [{ tx: 5, ty: 5 }];  // destination tile\n   217\u2192      maple.moveProgress = 0;\n   218\u2192      maple.targetCharId = sol.id;\n   219\u2192\n   220\u2192      // Reset facings to neutral so we can see the change\n   221\u2192      maple.facing = 'down';\n   222\u2192      sol.facing   = 'left';\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192      // Advance 1000ms (2 tile periods) \u2192 maple's path clears \u2192 action becomes 'idle'\n   225\u2192      // then arrival handler fires: face each other, clear targetCharId\n   226\u2192      engine._updateCharacters(1000);\n   227\u2192\n   228\u2192      return {\n   229\u2192        maple: { facing: maple.facing, tx: maple.tx, ty: maple.ty },\n   230\u2192        sol:   { facing: sol.facing,   tx: sol.tx,   ty: sol.ty   },\n   231\u2192        targetCharIdCleared: maple.targetCharId === null,\n   232\u2192      };\n   233\u2192    });\n   234\u2192\n   235\u2192    console.log('After arrival, maple facing:', result.maple.facing, '| sol facing:', result.sol.facing);\n   236\u2192    console.log('targetCharId cleared:', result.targetCharIdCleared);\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192    // Maple is at x=5, sol at x=8 \u2192 maple should face right, sol should face left\n   239\u2192    expect(result.maple.facing, 'Maple should face right (toward Sol)').toBe('right');\n   240\u2192    expect(result.sol.facing,   'Sol should face left (toward Maple)').toBe('left');\n   241\u2192    expect(result.targetCharIdCleared, 'targetCharId should be cleared after arrival').toBe(true);\n   242\u2192  });\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 4: Facing toward object when already adjacent (no walk needed) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   245\u2192\n   246\u2192  test('character faces object immediately when already adjacent at wander time', async ({ page }) => {\n   247\u2192    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n   248\u2192\n   249\u2192    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n   250\u2192    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n   251\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n   252\u2192\n   253\u2192    // Directly invoke _doInteraction to verify facing is applied at that call site.\n   254\u2192    // Uses cafe_espresso (tx=2, ty=11) \u2014 a single-tile object in this branch.\n   255\u2192    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n   256\u2192      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n   257\u2192      const chars  = engine.characters;\n   258\u2192      const maple  = chars.find(c => c.id === 'maple');\n   259\u2192\n   260\u2192      // Use cafe_espresso (tx=2, ty=11) \u2014 exists in feature/frg-244 world layout\n   261\u2192      const espresso = engine.objectEngine.get('cafe_espresso');\n   262\u2192      if (!espresso) return { error: 'cafe_espresso not found' };\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192      // Place maple below the espresso so vertical (dy) delta dominates after pre-resolution:\n   265\u2192      // objCx = 2 + 0.5 = 2.5, objCy = 11 + 0.5 = 11.5\n   266\u2192      // maple at (2, 14): dx = 2.5-2 = 0.5, dy = 11.5-14 = -2.5 \u2192 |dy| > |dx| \u2192 face up\n   267\u2192      maple.tx = 2;\n   268\u2192      maple.ty = 14;\n   269\u2192      maple.facing = 'right'; // Wrong facing \u2014 should be corrected to 'up'\n   270\u2192\n   271\u2192      // Reset espresso to idle so the interaction succeeds\n   272\u2192      espresso.state = 'idle';\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192      // Call the interaction handler directly \u2014 this is what happens when a char arrives at object\n   275\u2192      engine._doInteraction(maple, espresso, 'use');\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192      const objCx = espresso.position.tx + (espresso.position.tw || 1) / 2;\n   278\u2192      const objCy = espresso.position.ty + (espresso.position.th || 1) / 2;\n   279\u2192      const dx = objCx - maple.tx;\n   280\u2192      const dy = objCy - maple.ty;\n   281\u2192      const expectedFacing = Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)\n   282\u2192        ? (dx > 0 ? 'right' : 'left')\n   283\u2192        : (dy > 0 ? 'down' : 'up');\n   284\u2192\n   285\u2192      return {\n   286\u2192        maplePos: { tx: maple.tx, ty: maple.ty },\n   287\u2192        objPos:   { tx: espresso.position.tx, ty: espresso.position.ty },\n   288\u2192        facing:   maple.facing,\n   289\u2192        expected: expectedFacing,\n   290\u2192      };\n   291\u2192    });\n   292\u2192\n   293\u2192    if (result.error) {\n   294\u2192      console.log('[SKIP]', result.error);\n   295\u2192      return;\n   296\u2192    }\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192    console.log(\n   299\u2192      `Maple at (${result.maplePos.tx},${result.maplePos.ty}) \u2192 ` +\n   300\u2192      `espresso at (${result.objPos.tx},${result.objPos.ty}): ` +\n   301\u2192      `facing=${result.facing}, expected=${result.expected}`\n   302\u2192    );\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192    expect(result.facing, `Maple should face ${result.expected} toward espresso`).toBe(result.expected);\n   305\u2192  });\n   306\u2192\n   307\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 5: Talk already-nearby \u2014 face each other immediately (AI path) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   308\u2192\n   309\u2192  test('characters face each other immediately when already close (AI talk path)', async ({ page }) => {\n   310\u2192    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n   311\u2192\n   312\u2192    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n   313\u2192    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n   314\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192    // Simulate the AI branch where dist <= 2 \u2192 face each other immediately\n   317\u2192    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n   318\u2192      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n   319\u2192      const chars  = engine.characters;\n   320\u2192      const nyx   = chars.find(c => c.id === 'nyx');\n   321\u2192      const maple  = chars.find(c => c.id === 'maple');\n   322\u2192\n   323\u2192      // Place them 1 tile apart (dist \u2264 2 \u2192 should face immediately)\n   324\u2192      nyx.tx   = 10; nyx.ty   = 5;\n   325\u2192      maple.tx = 10; maple.ty = 6;\n   326\u2192\n   327\u2192      nyx.facing   = 'left';  // Wrong \u2014 should be corrected\n   328\u2192      maple.facing = 'right'; // Wrong \u2014 should be corrected\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192      // Simulate the already-nearby branch in ai.js:\n   331\u2192      //   char._updateFacing(target.tx - char.tx, target.ty - char.ty);\n   332\u2192      //   target._updateFacing(char.tx - target.tx, char.ty - target.ty);\n   333\u2192      nyx._updateFacing(maple.tx - nyx.tx, maple.ty - nyx.ty);\n   334\u2192      maple._updateFacing(nyx.tx - maple.tx, nyx.ty - maple.ty);\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192      return {\n   337\u2192        nyx:   { facing: nyx.facing   },\n   338\u2192        maple: { facing: maple.facing },\n   339\u2192      };\n   340\u2192    });\n   341\u2192\n   342\u2192    // nyx is at y=5, maple at y=6 \u2192 nyx should face down (toward maple), maple should face up (toward nyx)\n   343\u2192    console.log('Nyx facing:', result.nyx.facing, '| Maple facing:', result.maple.facing);\n   344\u2192    expect(result.nyx.facing,   'Nyx should face down toward Maple').toBe('down');\n   345\u2192    expect(result.maple.facing, 'Maple should face up toward Nyx').toBe('up');\n   346\u2192  });\n   347\u2192});\n   348\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code.
