\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192  create() {\n   106\u2192    // Background\n   107\u2192    const img = this.add.image(0, 0, 'jp').setOrigin(0, 0);\n   108\u2192    this.cameras.main.setBounds(0, 0, img.width, img.height);\n   109\u2192    this.cameras.main.centerOn(img.width / 2, img.height / 2);\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192    // Object state sprites \u2014 overlaid on the background tile, swapped on state transitions\n   112\u2192    // Objects with spriteMap: stove, tea_set, bookshelf, easel\n   113\u2192    this._objectSprites = {};\n   114\u2192    for (const def of OBJECT_DEFS) {\n   115\u2192      if (!def.spriteMap) continue;\n   116\u2192      const initKey = def.spriteMap[def.state] ?? null;\n   117\u2192      if (!initKey) continue;\n   118\u2192      const px = def.position.tx * TILE_SIZE;\n   119\u2192      const py = def.position.ty * TILE_SIZE;\n   120\u2192      this._objectSprites[def.id] = this.add.image(px, py, initKey)\n   121\u2192        .setOrigin(0, 0)\n   122\u2192        .setDepth(1);\n   123\u2192    }\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192    // Interaction animation type \u2192 Phaser anim key suffix and spritesheet key suffix\n   126\u2192    const INTERACT_ANIM_DEFS = [\n   127\u2192      { type: 'reading', sheetKey: 'reading_48x48' },\n   128\u2192      { type: 'sit',     sheetKey: 'sit_48x48'     },\n   129\u2192      { type: 'sit2',    sheetKey: 'sit2_48x48'    },\n   130\u2192      { type: 'phone',   sheetKey: 'phone_48x48'   },\n   131\u2192    ];\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192    // Animations\n   134\u2192    // Idle spritesheet layout: 24 frames (4 directions \u00d7 6 frames)\n   135\u2192    //   down  0\u20135, left 6\u201311, right 12\u201317, up 18\u201323\n   136\u2192    const IDLE_DIRS = [\n   137\u2192      { key: 'down',  start:  0, end:  5 },\n   138\u2192      { key: 'left',  start:  6, end: 11 },\n   139\u2192      { key: 'right', start: 12, end: 17 },\n   140\u2192      { key: 'up',    start: 18, end: 23 },\n   141\u2192    ];\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192    ['Amelia', 'Lucy', 'Ash'].forEach(name => {\n   144\u2192      // Generic idle (backwards-compat, always faces down)\n   145\u2192      this.anims.create({\n   146\u2192        key: `${name}_idle_anim`,\n   147\u2192        frames: this.anims.generateFrameNumbers(`${name}_idle`, { start: 0, end: 5 }),\n   148\u2192        frameRate: 6,\n   149\u2192        repeat: -1,\n   150\u2192      });\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192      // Directional idle animations\n   153\u2192      IDLE_DIRS.forEach(({ key, start, end }) => {\n   154\u2192        this.anims.create({\n   155\u2192          key: `${name}_idle_${key}`,\n   156\u2192          frames: this.anims.generateFrameNumbers(`${name}_idle`, { start, end }),\n   157\u2192          frameRate: 6,\n   158\u2192          repeat: -1,\n   159\u2192        });\n   160\u2192      });\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192      WALK_DIRS.forEach(({ key, start, end }) => {\n   163\u2192        this.anims.create({\n   164\u2192          key: `${name}_walk_${key}`,\n   165\u2192          frames: this.anims.generateFrameNumbers(`${name}_walk`, { start, end }),\n   166\u2192          frameRate: 8,\n   167\u2192          repeat: -1,\n   168\u2192        });\n   169\u2192      });\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192      // Interaction animations (6-frame loop, 6 fps)\n   172\u2192      INTERACT_ANIM_DEFS.forEach(({ type, sheetKey }) => {\n   173\u2192        this.anims.create({\n   174\u2192          key: `${name}_interact_${type}`,\n   175\u2192          frames: this.anims.generateFrameNumbers(`${name}_${sheetKey}`, { start: 0, end: 5 }),\n   176\u2192          frameRate: 6,\n   177\u2192          repeat: -1,\n   178\u2192        });\n   179\u2192      });\n   180\u2192    });\n   181\u2192\n   182\u2192    // Engine\n   183\u2192    const walkable = buildWalkableGrid();\n   184\u2192    this.engine = new SimulationEngine({\n   185\u2192      walkable,\n   186\u2192      gridCols: GRID_COLS,\n   187\u2192      gridRows: GRID_ROWS,\n   188\u2192      aiEngine: new AIEngine({\n   189\u2192        endpoint: '/api/llm',\n   190\u2192        model: 'anthropic/claude-3-haiku',\n   191\u2192        decisionIntervalMs: 30_000,\n   192\u2192      }),\n   193\u2192    });\n   194\u2192\n   195\u2192    // Characters\n   196\u2192    CHARS.forEach(({ name, label, tx, ty, dialogue }) => {\n   197\u2192      const initPx = tx * TILE_SIZE + TILE_SIZE / 2;\n   198\u2192      const initPy = (ty + 1) * TILE_SIZE;\n   199\u2192\n   200\u2192      const sprite = this.add.sprite(initPx, initPy, `${name}_idle`)\n   201\u2192        .setOrigin(0.5, 1)\n   202\u2192        .setDepth(10);\n   203\u2192      sprite.play(`${name}_idle_anim`);\n   204\u2192\n   205\u2192      const nameLabel = this.add.text(initPx, initPy - 100, label, {\n   206\u2192        fontSize: '12px',\n   207\u2192        color: '#ffffff',\n   208\u2192        fontFamily: 'monospace',\n   209\u2192        stroke: '#000000',\n   210\u2192        strokeThickness: 2,\n   211\u2192      }).setOrigin(0.5, 1).setDepth(11);\n   212\u2192\n   213\u2192      const char = new Character({\n   214\u2192        name, label, tx, ty, sprite, nameLabel,\n   215\u2192        walkable, gridCols: GRID_COLS, gridRows: GRID_ROWS,\n   216\u2192        dialogue,\n   217\u2192      });\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192      this.engine.addCharacter(char);\n   220\u2192    });\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192    // Expose engine for the debug overlay panel\n   223\u2192    window._gameEngine = this.engine;\n   224\u2192\n   225\u2192    // Sim clock display (screen-fixed, top-left)\n   226\u2192    this.clockText = this.add.text(8, 8, '', {\n   227\u2192      fontSize: '13px',\n   228\u2192      color: '#ffe8a3',\n   229\u2192      fontFamily: 'monospace',\n   230\u2192      stroke: '#000000',\n   231\u2192      strokeThickness: 2,\n   232\u2192    }).setDepth(20).setScrollFactor(0);\n   233\u2192  }\n   234\u2192\n   235\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 Update \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192  update(_time, delta) {\n   238\u2192    this.engine.update(delta);\n   239\u2192    this.clockText.setText(this.engine.getSimTimeString());\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192    // Show speech bubbles queued by the engine\n   242\u2192    for (const char of this.engine.characters) {\n   243\u2192      if (char.pendingSpeech && char.sprite) {\n   244\u2192        showSpeech(this, char.sprite, char.pendingSpeech);\n   245\u2192        char.pendingSpeech = null;\n   246\u2192      }\n   247\u2192    }\n   248\u2192\n   249\u2192    // Process object state-transition events: swap sprites and float emojis\n   250\u2192    if (this.engine.pendingEvents.length > 0) {\n   251\u2192      for (const ev of this.engine.pendingEvents) {\n   252\u2192        const obj = this.engine.objectEngine.getById(ev.objectId);\n   253\u2192        if (!obj) continue;\n   254\u2192\n   255\u2192        // Swap object sprite texture on state change\n   256\u2192        if (ev.spriteKey && this._objectSprites[ev.objectId]) {\n   257\u2192          this._objectSprites[ev.objectId].setTexture(ev.spriteKey);\n   258\u2192        }\n   259\u2192\n   260\u2192        // Float emoji above the object\n   261\u2192        if (ev.emoji) {\n   262\u2192          const px = obj.tx * TILE_SIZE + TILE_SIZE / 2;\n   263\u2192          const py = obj.ty * TILE_SIZE;\n   264\u2192          const label = this.add.text(px, py, ev.emoji, { fontSize: '24px' }).setDepth(25);\n   265\u2192          this.tweens.add({\n   266\u2192            targets: label,\n   267\u2192            y:       label.y - 40,\n   268\u2192            alpha:   0,\n   269\u2192            duration: 2000,\n   270\u2192            onComplete: () => label.destroy(),\n   271\u2192          });\n   272\u2192        }\n   273\u2192      }\n   274\u2192      this.engine.pendingEvents = [];\n   275\u2192    }\n   276\u2192  }\n   277\u2192}\n   278\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}

SUCCESS 
     1→/**
     2→ * Character.js — tile-based character with A* pathfinding and state machine.
     3→ * Pure JS, no Phaser dependency. Sprite/label references are set externally.
     4→ */
     5→
     6→const TILE_SIZE = 48;
     7→
     8→// ─── A* pathfinding ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     9→
    10→function heuristic(ax, ay, bx, by) {
    11→  return Math.abs(ax - bx) + Math.abs(ay - by);
    12→}
    13→
    14→/**
    15→ * Find a path from (startX, startY) to (goalX, goalY) on a walkable grid.
    16→ * @param {boolean[][]} walkable  - walkable[row][col]
    17→ * @param {number} cols
    18→ * @param {number} rows
    19→ * @returns {Array<{tx,ty}>|null}  - list of steps NOT including start; null if no path
    20→ */
    21→function findPath(walkable, cols, rows, startX, startY, goalX, goalY) {
    22→  if (startX === goalX && startY === goalY) return [];
    23→  if (!walkable[startY]?.[startX] || !walkable[goalY]?.[goalX]) return null;
    24→
    25→  const key = (x, y) => x * 1000 + y;
    26→
    27→  const open = new Map();
    28→  const closed = new Set();
    29→
    30→  const startNode = { x: startX, y: startY, g: 0, h: heuristic(startX, startY, goalX, goalY), parent: null };
    31→  open.set(key(startX, startY), startNode);
    32→
    33→  while (open.size > 0) {
    34→    // Pick node with lowest f = g + h
    35→    let current = null;
    36→    for (const node of open.values()) {
    37→      if (!current || node.g + node.h < current.g + current.h) current = node;
    38→    }
    39→
    40→    if (current.x === goalX && current.y === goalY) {
    41→      // Reconstruct path
    42→      const path = [];
    43→      let node = current;
    44→      while (node.parent) {
    45→        path.unshift({ tx: node.x, ty: node.y });
    46→        node = node.parent;
    47→      }
    48→      return path;
    49→    }
    50→
    51→    open.delete(key(current.x, current.y));
    52→    closed.add(key(current.x, current.y));
    53→
    54→    const neighbors = [
    55→      { x: current.x + 1, y: current.y },
    56→      { x: current.x - 1, y: current.y },
    57→      { x: current.x,     y: current.y + 1 },
    58→      { x: current.x,     y: current.y - 1 },
    59→    ];
    60→
    61→    for (const n of neighbors) {
    62→      if (n.x < 0 || n.x >= cols || n.y < 0 || n.y >= rows) continue;
    63→      if (!walkable[n.y]?.[n.x]) continue;
    64→      if (closed.has(key(n.x, n.y))) continue;
    65→
    66→      const g = current.g + 1;
    67→      const existing = open.get(key(n.x, n.y));
    68→      if (existing) {
    69→        if (g < existing.g) {
    70→          existing.g = g;
    71→          existing.parent = current;
    72→        }
    73→      } else {
    74→        open.set(key(n.x, n.y), {
    75→          x: n.x, y: n.y,
    76→          g, h: heuristic(n.x, n.y, goalX, goalY),
    77→          parent: current,
    78→        });
    79→      }
    80→    }
    81→  }
    82→
    83→  return null; // no path
    84→}
    85→
    86→// ─── Character ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    87→
    88→export class Character {
    89→  /**
    90→   * @param {object} config
    91→   * @param {string}   config.name        - sprite key prefix (e.g. 'Amelia')
    92→   * @param {string}   config.label       - display name (e.g. 'Maple')
    93→   * @param {number}   config.tx          - starting tile X
    94→   * @param {number}   config.ty          - starting tile Y
    95→   * @param {boolean[][]} config.walkable - walkable grid [row][col]
    96→   * @param {number}   config.gridCols
    97→   * @param {number}   config.gridRows
    98→   * @param {object}   [config.sprite]    - Phaser sprite (set after construction)
    99→   * @param {object}   [config.nameLabel] - Phaser Text object
   100→   * @param {number}   [config.moveSpeed] - tiles per second (default 2)
   101→   */
   102→  constructor(config) {
   103→    this.name      = config.name;
   104→    this.label     = config.label;
   105→    this.sprite    = config.sprite    || null;
   106→    this.nameLabel = config.nameLabel || null;
   107→
   108→    this.tx     = config.tx;
   109→    this.ty     = config.ty;
   110→    this.prevTx = config.tx;
   111→    this.prevTy = config.ty;
   112→
   113→    this.path        = [];
   114→    this.moveProgress = 0;
   115→    this.moveSpeed   = config.moveSpeed || 2; // tiles/sec
   116→
   117→    // State: 'idle' | 'walking' | 'interacting' | 'talking'
   118→    this.action = 'idle';
   119→    this.facing = 'down';
   120→
   121→    // Wander: timer counts DOWN; engine starts wander when <= 0
   122→    this.wanderTimer = 3000 + Math.random() * 5000; // 3–8 s
   123→
   124→    // Interaction
   125→    this.interactTimer    = 0;
   126→    this.interactTimerMax = 0; // initial duration; used by UI for progress bar
   127→    this.interactTarget   = null;
   128→
   129→    // Personality stats (0–100)
   130→    this.mood   = 100;
   131→    this.energy = 100;
   132→
   133→    // Optional dialogue driven by AI
   134→    this.currentDialogue = null;
   135→    this.dialogueTimer   = 0;
   136→
   137→    // Preset dialogue lines (used for random speech bubbles)
   138→    this.dialogue     = config.dialogue || [];
   139→    // Counts down; when it hits 0 Engine sets pendingSpeech
   140→    this.speechTimer  = 20000 + Math.random() * 20000; // 20–40 s
   141→    // Scene reads this each frame and shows a bubble, then clears it
   142→    this.pendingSpeech = null;
   143→
   144→    this.walkable  = config.walkable;
   145→    this.gridCols  = config.gridCols;
   146→    this.gridRows  = config.gridRows;
   147→
   148→    // Object interaction targeting — set by engine, cleared on arrival
   149→    this.targetObjectId = null;
   150→    this.pendingAction  = null;
   151→
   152→    // Fallback/re-routing: counts down; engine updates talk route when <= 0
   153→    this._talkRetargetTimer = 0;
   154→
   155→    // Deadlock escape: counts consecutive failed wander attempts; resets on success
   156→    this._failedMoveCount = 0;
   157→
   158→    // Talk targeting — set by AIEngine when walking to meet another character
   159→    this.pendingTalkTarget   = null;  // Character ref awaiting arrival
   160→    this.pendingTalkDialogue = null;  // What A will say when conversation starts
   161→    this.pendingTalkResponse = null;  // What B will say when conversation starts
   162→
   163→    // Active conversation state
   164→    this.talkTimer    = 0;
   165→    this.talkTimerMax = 0;
   166→    this.talkTarget   = null;  // Character ref of current conversation partner
   167→  }
   168→
   169→  // ── Computed pixel position (Phaser setOrigin(0.5, 1) — feet at py) ─────────
   170→
   171→  get px() {
   172→    if (this.path.length === 0) {
   173→      return this.tx * TILE_SIZE + TILE_SIZE / 2;
   174→    }
   175→    const next = this.path[0];
   176→    return (this.prevTx + (next.tx - this.prevTx) * this.moveProgress) * TILE_SIZE + TILE_SIZE / 2;
   177→  }
   178→
   179→  get py() {
   180→    if (this.path.length === 0) {
   181→      return (this.ty + 1) * TILE_SIZE;
   182→    }
   183→    const next = this.path[0];
   184→    return (this.prevTy + (next.ty - this.prevTy) * this.moveProgress + 1) * TILE_SIZE;
   185→  }
   186→
   187→  // ── Movement ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   188→
   189→  /**
   190→   * @param {number} goalTx
   191→   * @param {number} goalTy
   192→   * @param {boolean[][]} [walkable] - optional dynamic walkable grid; defaults to this.walkable
   193→   */
   194→  moveTo(goalTx, goalTy, walkable = this.walkable) {
   195→    const sx = Math.round(this.tx);
   196→    const sy = Math.round(this.ty);
   197→    const path = findPath(walkable, this.gridCols, this.gridRows, sx, sy, goalTx, goalTy);
   198→
   199→    if (path && path.length > 0) {
   200→      this.path         = path;
   201→      this.prevTx       = sx;
   202→      this.prevTy       = sy;
   203→      this.moveProgress = 0;
   204→      this.action       = 'walking';
   205→
   206→      // Reset deadlock counter on successful path
   207→      this._failedMoveCount = 0;
   208→
   209→      // Set initial facing from first step
   210→      this._updateFacingFromNextTile();
   211→      this._playWalkAnim();
   212→      return true;
   213→    }
   214→    return false;
   215→  }
   216→
   217→  /** Convenience wrapper — start interaction with a named object. */
   218→  startInteraction(objectId, durationMs, animType) {
   219→    this.startInteract(objectId, durationMs, animType);
   220→  }
   221→
   222→  startInteract(target, durationMs, animType) {
   223→    this.action           = 'interacting';
   224→    this.interactTarget   = target;
   225→    this.interactTimer    = durationMs;
   226→    this.interactTimerMax = durationMs;
   227→    if (this.sprite) {
   228→      const interactKey = animType ? `${this.name}_interact_${animType}` : null;
   229→      if (interactKey && this.sprite.anims.exists(interactKey)) {
   230→        this.sprite.play(interactKey, true);
   231→      } else {
   232→        // Use directional idle so the character faces the object
   233→        this._playDirectionalIdle();
   234→      }
   235→    }
   236→  }
   237→
   238→  /** Enter a shared talking state with `partner` for `durationMs` milliseconds. */
   239→  startTalk(partner, durationMs) {
   240→    // Stop any current movement
   241→    this.path        = [];
   242→    this.moveProgress = 0;
   243→
   244→    this.action       = 'talking';
   245→    this.talkTarget   = partner;
   246→    this.talkTimer    = durationMs;
   247→    this.talkTimerMax = durationMs;
   248→
   249→    // Clear any pending object interaction targeting
   250→    this.targetObjectId      = null;
   251→    this.pendingAction       = null;
   252→    // Clear any pending talk targeting (e.g. B was also walking to someone)
   253→    this.pendingTalkTarget   = null;
   254→    this.pendingTalkDialogue = null;
   255→    this.pendingTalkResponse = null;
   256→
   257→    // Play directional idle so the character visually faces the right way
   258→    this._playDirectionalIdle();
   259→  }
   260→
   261→  // ── Per-frame update ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   262→
   263→  update(deltaMs) {
   264→    if (this.action === 'walking' && this.path.length > 0) {
   265→      const tileDurationMs = 1000 / this.moveSpeed;
   266→      this.moveProgress += deltaMs / tileDurationMs;
   267→
   268→      // Advance tiles
   269→      while (this.moveProgress >= 1 && this.path.length > 0) {
   270→        this.moveProgress -= 1;
   271→        const arrived = this.path.shift();
   272→        this.prevTx = arrived.tx;
   273→        this.prevTy = arrived.ty;
   274→        this.tx     = arrived.tx;
   275→        this.ty     = arrived.ty;
   276→      }
   277→
   278→      // Update facing after tile advance so animation matches the current movement direction
   279→      this._updateFacingFromNextTile();
   280→
   281→      if (this.path.length === 0) {
   282→        this.moveProgress = 0;
   283→        this.action       = 'idle';
   284→        this.wanderTimer  = 3000 + Math.random() * 5000;
   285→        this._playAnim('idle');
   286→      } else {
   287→        this._playWalkAnim();
   288→      }
   289→    } else if (this.action === 'idle') {
   290→      this.wanderTimer -= deltaMs;
   291→      // Ensure idle animation is always playing in idle state (handles external action resets)
   292→      this._playAnim('idle');
   293→    } else if (this.action === 'interacting') {
   294→      this.interactTimer -= deltaMs;
   295→      if (this.interactTimer <= 0) {
   296→        this.action        = 'idle';
   297→        this.interactTarget = null;
   298→        this.wanderTimer   = 3000 + Math.random() * 5000;
   299→        this._playAnim('idle');
   300→      }
   301→    } else if (this.action === 'talking') {
   302→      this.talkTimer -= deltaMs;
   303→      if (this.talkTimer <= 0) {
   304→        this.action      = 'idle';
   305→        this.talkTarget  = null;
   306→        this.wanderTimer = 3000 + Math.random() * 5000;
   307→        this._playAnim('idle');
   308→      }
   309→    }
   310→
   311→    // Tick dialogue
   312→    if (this.dialogueTimer > 0) {
   313→      this.dialogueTimer -= deltaMs;
   314→      if (this.dialogueTimer <= 0) this.currentDialogue = null;
   315→    }
   316→
   317→    // Sync Phaser sprite
   318→    if (this.sprite) {
   319→      this.sprite.x = this.px;
   320→      this.sprite.y = this.py;
   321→    }
   322→
   323→    if (this.nameLabel && this.sprite) {
   324→      this.nameLabel.setPosition(this.sprite.x, this.sprite.y - 100);
   325→    }
   326→  }
   327→
   328→  // ── Perception ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   329→
   330→  /** Returns objects within `radius` tiles. */
   331→  getPerceivedObjects(objects, radius = 4) {
   332→    return objects.filter(obj => {
   333→      const dx = obj.tx - this.tx;
   334→      const dy = obj.ty - this.ty;
   335→      return Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy) <= radius;
   336→    });
   337→  }
   338→
   339→  // ── Animation helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   340→
   341→  _updateFacingFromNextTile() {
   342→    if (this.path.length === 0) return;
   343→    const next = this.path[0];
   344→    const dx   = next.tx - this.tx;
   345→    const dy   = next.ty - this.ty;
   346→    if (Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)) {
   347→      this.facing = dx >= 0 ? 'right' : 'left';
   348→    } else {
   349→      this.facing = dy > 0 ? 'down' : 'up';
   350→    }
   351→  }
   352→
   353→  _playAnim(type) {
   354→    if (!this.sprite) return;
   355→    const key = type === 'idle'
   356→      ? `${this.name}_idle_anim`
   357→      : `${this.name}_walk_${this.facing}`;
   358→    if (this.sprite.anims.currentAnim?.key !== key) {
   359→      this.sprite.play(key, true);
   360→    }
   361→    this.sprite.flipX = (type === 'walk' && this.facing === 'left');
   362→  }
   363→
   364→  /** Play the directional idle animation matching this.facing. */
   365→  _playDirectionalIdle() {
   366→    if (!this.sprite) return;
   367→    const key = `${this.name}_idle_${this.facing}`;
   368→    // Use scene.anims (global manager) — sprite.anims.exists only knows previously played keys
   369→    const manager = this.sprite.scene?.anims;
   370→    if (manager?.exists(key)) {
   371→      if (this.sprite.anims.currentAnim?.key !== key) {
   372→        this.sprite.play(key, true);
   373→      }
   374→      this.sprite.flipX = false;
   375→    } else {
   376→      // Fallback to generic idle
   377→      this._playAnim('idle');
   378→    }
   379→  }
   380→
   381→  _playWalkAnim() {
   382→    this._playAnim('walk');
   383→  }
   384→}
   385→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

{
  "content": {
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_01Y1CxaNB7tg3UtYAXexdSqV",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "     1\u2192/**\n     2\u2192 * Character.js \u2014 tile-based character with A* pathfinding and state machine.\n     3\u2192 * Pure JS, no Phaser dependency. Sprite/label references are set externally.\n     4\u2192 */\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192const TILE_SIZE = 48;\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 A* pathfinding \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192function heuristic(ax, ay, bx, by) {\n    11\u2192  return Math.abs(ax - bx) + Math.abs(ay - by);\n    12\u2192}\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192/**\n    15\u2192 * Find a path from (startX, startY) to (goalX, goalY) on a walkable grid.\n    16\u2192 * @param {boolean[][]} walkable  - walkable[row][col]\n    17\u2192 * @param {number} cols\n    18\u2192 * @param {number} rows\n    19\u2192 * @returns {Array<{tx,ty}>|null}  - list of steps NOT including start; null if no path\n    20\u2192 */\n    21\u2192function findPath(walkable, cols, rows, startX, startY, goalX, goalY) {\n    22\u2192  if (startX === goalX && startY === goalY) return [];\n    23\u2192  if (!walkable[startY]?.[startX] || !walkable[goalY]?.[goalX]) return null;\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192  const key = (x, y) => x * 1000 + y;\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192  const open = new Map();\n    28\u2192  const closed = new Set();\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192  const startNode = { x: startX, y: startY, g: 0, h: heuristic(startX, startY, goalX, goalY), parent: null };\n    31\u2192  open.set(key(startX, startY), startNode);\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192  while (open.size > 0) {\n    34\u2192    // Pick node with lowest f = g + h\n    35\u2192    let current = null;\n    36\u2192    for (const node of open.values()) {\n    37\u2192      if (!current || node.g + node.h < current.g + current.h) current = node;\n    38\u2192    }\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192    if (current.x === goalX && current.y === goalY) {\n    41\u2192      // Reconstruct path\n    42\u2192      const path = [];\n    43\u2192      let node = current;\n    44\u2192      while (node.parent) {\n    45\u2192        path.unshift({ tx: node.x, ty: node.y });\n    46\u2192        node = node.parent;\n    47\u2192      }\n    48\u2192      return path;\n    49\u2192    }\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192    open.delete(key(current.x, current.y));\n    52\u2192    closed.add(key(current.x, current.y));\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192    const neighbors = [\n    55\u2192      { x: current.x + 1, y: current.y },\n    56\u2192      { x: current.x - 1, y: current.y },\n    57\u2192      { x: current.x,     y: current.y + 1 },\n    58\u2192      { x: current.x,     y: current.y - 1 },\n    59\u2192    ];\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192    for (const n of neighbors) {\n    62\u2192      if (n.x < 0 || n.x >= cols || n.y < 0 || n.y >= rows) continue;\n    63\u2192      if (!walkable[n.y]?.[n.x]) continue;\n    64\u2192      if (closed.has(key(n.x, n.y))) continue;\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192      const g = current.g + 1;\n    67\u2192      const existing = open.get(key(n.x, n.y));\n    68\u2192      if (existing) {\n    69\u2192        if (g < existing.g) {\n    70\u2192          existing.g = g;\n    71\u2192          existing.parent = current;\n    72\u2192        }\n    73\u2192      } else {\n    74\u2192        open.set(key(n.x, n.y), {\n    75\u2192          x: n.x, y: n.y,\n    76\u2192          g, h: heuristic(n.x, n.y, goalX, goalY),\n    77\u2192          parent: current,\n    78\u2192        });\n    79\u2192      }\n    80\u2192    }\n    81\u2192  }\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192  return null; // no path\n    84\u2192}\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Character \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192export class Character {\n    89\u2192  /**\n    90\u2192   * @param {object} config\n    91\u2192   * @param {string}   config.name        - sprite key prefix (e.g. 'Amelia')\n    92\u2192   * @param {string}   config.label       - display name (e.g. 'Maple')\n    93\u2192   * @param {number}   config.tx          - starting tile X\n    94\u2192   * @param {number}   config.ty          - starting tile Y\n    95\u2192   * @param {boolean[][]} config.walkable - walkable grid [row][col]\n    96\u2192   * @param {number}   config.gridCols\n    97\u2192   * @param {number}   config.gridRows\n    98\u2192   * @param {object}   [config.sprite]    - Phaser sprite (set after construction)\n    99\u2192   * @param {object}   [config.nameLabel] - Phaser Text object\n   100\u2192   * @param {number}   [config.moveSpeed] - tiles per second (default 2)\n   101\u2192   */\n   102\u2192  constructor(config) {\n   103\u2192    this.name      = config.name;\n   104\u2192    this.label     = config.label;\n   105\u2192    this.sprite    = config.sprite    || null;\n   106\u2192    this.nameLabel = config.nameLabel || null;\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192    this.tx     = config.tx;\n   109\u2192    this.ty     = config.ty;\n   110\u2192    this.prevTx = config.tx;\n   111\u2192    this.prevTy = config.ty;\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192    this.path        = [];\n   114\u2192    this.moveProgress = 0;\n   115\u2192    this.moveSpeed   = config.moveSpeed || 2; // tiles/sec\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192    // State: 'idle' | 'walking' | 'interacting' | 'talking'\n   118\u2192    this.action = 'idle';\n   119\u2192    this.facing = 'down';\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192    // Wander: timer counts DOWN; engine starts wander when <= 0\n   122\u2192    this.wanderTimer = 3000 + Math.random() * 5000; // 3\u20138 s\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192    // Interaction\n   125\u2192    this.interactTimer    = 0;\n   126\u2192    this.interactTimerMax = 0; // initial duration; used by UI for progress bar\n   127\u2192    this.interactTarget   = null;\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192    // Personality stats (0\u2013100)\n   130\u2192    this.mood   = 100;\n   131\u2192    this.energy = 100;\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192    // Optional dialogue driven by AI\n   134\u2192    this.currentDialogue = null;\n   135\u2192    this.dialogueTimer   = 0;\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192    // Preset dialogue lines (used for random speech bubbles)\n   138\u2192    this.dialogue     = config.dialogue || [];\n   139\u2192    // Counts down; when it hits 0 Engine sets pendingSpeech\n   140\u2192    this.speechTimer  = 20000 + Math.random() * 20000; // 20\u201340 s\n   141\u2192    // Scene reads this each frame and shows a bubble, then clears it\n   142\u2192    this.pendingSpeech = null;\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192    this.walkable  = config.walkable;\n   145\u2192    this.gridCols  = config.gridCols;\n   146\u2192    this.gridRows  = config.gridRows;\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192    // Object interaction targeting \u2014 set by engine, cleared on arrival\n   149\u2192    this.targetObjectId = null;\n   150\u2192    this.pendingAction  = null;\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192    // Fallback/re-routing: counts down; engine updates talk route when <= 0\n   153\u2192    this._talkRetargetTimer = 0;\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192    // Deadlock escape: counts consecutive failed wander attempts; resets on success\n   156\u2192    this._failedMoveCount = 0;\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192    // Talk targeting \u2014 set by AIEngine when walking to meet another character\n   159\u2192    this.pendingTalkTarget   = null;  // Character ref awaiting arrival\n   160\u2192    this.pendingTalkDialogue = null;  // What A will say when conversation starts\n   161\u2192    this.pendingTalkResponse = null;  // What B will say when conversation starts\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192    // Active conversation state\n   164\u2192    this.talkTimer    = 0;\n   165\u2192    this.talkTimerMax = 0;\n   166\u2192    this.talkTarget   = null;  // Character ref of current conversation partner\n   167\u2192  }\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 Computed pixel position (Phaser setOrigin(0.5, 1) \u2014 feet at py) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192  get px() {\n   172\u2192    if (this.path.length === 0) {\n   173\u2192      return this.tx * TILE_SIZE + TILE_SIZE / 2;\n   174\u2192    }\n   175\u2192    const next = this.path[0];\n   176\u2192    return (this.prevTx + (next.tx - this.prevTx) * this.moveProgress) * TILE_SIZE + TILE_SIZE / 2;\n   177\u2192  }\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192  get py() {\n   180\u2192    if (this.path.length === 0) {\n   181\u2192      return (this.ty + 1) * TILE_SIZE;\n   182\u2192    }\n   183\u2192    const next = this.path[0];\n   184\u2192    return (this.prevTy + (next.ty - this.prevTy) * this.moveProgress + 1) * TILE_SIZE;\n   185\u2192  }\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 Movement \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192  /**\n   190\u2192   * @param {number} goalTx\n   191\u2192   * @param {number} goalTy\n   192\u2192   * @param {boolean[][]} [walkable] - optional dynamic walkable grid; defaults to this.walkable\n   193\u2192   */\n   194\u2192  moveTo(goalTx, goalTy, walkable = this.walkable) {\n   195\u2192    const sx = Math.round(this.tx);\n   196\u2192    const sy = Math.round(this.ty);\n   197\u2192    const path = findPath(walkable, this.gridCols, this.gridRows, sx, sy, goalTx, goalTy);\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192    if (path && path.length > 0) {\n   200\u2192      this.path         = path;\n   201\u2192      this.prevTx       = sx;\n   202\u2192      this.prevTy       = sy;\n   203\u2192      this.moveProgress = 0;\n   204\u2192      this.action       = 'walking';\n   205\u2192\n   206\u2192      // Reset deadlock counter on successful path\n   207\u2192      this._failedMoveCount = 0;\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192      // Set initial facing from first step\n   210\u2192      this._updateFacingFromNextTile();\n   211\u2192      this._playWalkAnim();\n   212\u2192      return true;\n   213\u2192    }\n   214\u2192    return false;\n   215\u2192  }\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192  /** Convenience wrapper \u2014 start interaction with a named object. */\n   218\u2192  startInteraction(objectId, durationMs, animType) {\n   219\u2192    this.startInteract(objectId, durationMs, animType);\n   220\u2192  }\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192  startInteract(target, durationMs, animType) {\n   223\u2192    this.action           = 'interacting';\n   224\u2192    this.interactTarget   = target;\n   225\u2192    this.interactTimer    = durationMs;\n   226\u2192    this.interactTimerMax = durationMs;\n   227\u2192    if (this.sprite) {\n   228\u2192      const interactKey = animType ? `${this.name}_interact_${animType}` : null;\n   229\u2192      if (interactKey && this.sprite.anims.exists(interactKey)) {\n   230\u2192        this.sprite.play(interactKey, true);\n   231\u2192      } else {\n   232\u2192        // Use directional idle so the character faces the object\n   233\u2192        this._playDirectionalIdle();\n   234\u2192      }\n   235\u2192    }\n   236\u2192  }\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192  /** Enter a shared talking state with `partner` for `durationMs` milliseconds. */\n   239\u2192  startTalk(partner, durationMs) {\n   240\u2192    // Stop any current movement\n   241\u2192    this.path        = [];\n   242\u2192    this.moveProgress = 0;\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192    this.action       = 'talking';\n   245\u2192    this.talkTarget   = partner;\n   246\u2192    this.talkTimer    = durationMs;\n   247\u2192    this.talkTimerMax = durationMs;\n   248\u2192\n   249\u2192    // Clear any pending object interaction targeting\n   250\u2192    this.targetObjectId      = null;\n   251\u2192    this.pendingAction       = null;\n   252\u2192    // Clear any pending talk targeting (e.g. B was also walking to someone)\n   253\u2192    this.pendingTalkTarget   = null;\n   254\u2192    this.pendingTalkDialogue = null;\n   255\u2192    this.pendingTalkResponse = null;\n   256\u2192\n   257\u2192    // Play directional idle so the character visually faces the right way\n   258\u2192    this._playDirectionalIdle();\n   259\u2192  }\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 Per-frame update \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   262\u2192\n   263\u2192  update(deltaMs) {\n   264\u2192    if (this.action === 'walking' && this.path.length > 0) {\n   265\u2192      const tileDurationMs = 1000 / this.moveSpeed;\n   266\u2192      this.moveProgress += deltaMs / tileDurationMs;\n   267\u2192\n   268\u2192      // Advance tiles\n   269\u2192      while (this.moveProgress >= 1 && this.path.length > 0) {\n   270\u2192        this.moveProgress -= 1;\n   271\u2192        const arrived = this.path.shift();\n   272\u2192        this.prevTx = arrived.tx;\n   273\u2192        this.prevTy = arrived.ty;\n   274\u2192        this.tx     = arrived.tx;\n   275\u2192        this.ty     = arrived.ty;\n   276\u2192      }\n   277\u2192\n   278\u2192      // Update facing after tile advance so animation matches the current movement direction\n   279\u2192      this._updateFacingFromNextTile();\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192      if (this.path.length === 0) {\n   282\u2192        this.moveProgress = 0;\n   283\u2192        this.action       = 'idle';\n   284\u2192        this.wanderTimer  = 3000 + Math.random() * 5000;\n   285\u2192        this._playAnim('idle');\n   286\u2192      } else {\n   287\u2192        this._playWalkAnim();\n   288\u2192      }\n   289\u2192    } else if (this.action === 'idle') {\n   290\u2192      this.wanderTimer -= deltaMs;\n   291\u2192      // Ensure idle animation is always playing in idle state (handles external action resets)\n   292\u2192      this._playAnim('idle');\n   293\u2192    } else if (this.action === 'interacting') {\n   294\u2192      this.interactTimer -= deltaMs;\n   295\u2192      if (this.interactTimer <= 0) {\n   296\u2192        this.action        = 'idle';\n   297\u2192        this.interactTarget = null;\n   298\u2192        this.wanderTimer   = 3000 + Math.random() * 5000;\n   299\u2192        this._playAnim('idle');\n   300\u2192      }\n   301\u2192    } else if (this.action === 'talking') {\n   302\u2192      this.talkTimer -= deltaMs;\n   303\u2192      if (this.talkTimer <= 0) {\n   304\u2192        this.action      = 'idle';\n   305\u2192        this.talkTarget  = null;\n   306\u2192        this.wanderTimer = 3000 + Math.random() * 5000;\n   307\u2192        this._playAnim('idle');\n   308\u2192      }\n   309\u2192    }\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192    // Tick dialogue\n   312\u2192    if (this.dialogueTimer > 0) {\n   313\u2192      this.dialogueTimer -= deltaMs;\n   314\u2192      if (this.dialogueTimer <= 0) this.currentDialogue = null;\n   315\u2192    }\n   316\u2192\n   317\u2192    // Sync Phaser sprite\n   318\u2192    if (this.sprite) {\n   319\u2192      this.sprite.x = this.px;\n   320\u2192      this.sprite.y = this.py;\n   321\u2192    }\n   322\u2192\n   323\u2192    if (this.nameLabel && this.sprite) {\n   324\u2192      this.nameLabel.setPosition(this.sprite.x, this.sprite.y - 100);\n   325\u2192    }\n   326\u2192  }\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 Perception \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192  /** Returns objects within `radius` tiles. */\n   331\u2192  getPerceivedObjects(objects, radius = 4) {\n   332\u2192    return objects.filter(obj => {\n   333\u2192      const dx = obj.tx - this.tx;\n   334\u2192      const dy = obj.ty - this.ty;\n   335\u2192      return Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy) <= radius;\n   336\u2192    });\n   337\u2192  }\n   338\u2192\n   339\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 Animation helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   340\u2192\n   341\u2192  _updateFacingFromNextTile() {\n   342\u2192    if (this.path.length === 0) return;\n   343\u2192    const next = this.path[0];\n   344\u2192    const dx   = next.tx - this.tx;\n   345\u2192    const dy   = next.ty - this.ty;\n   346\u2192    if (Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)) {\n   347\u2192      this.facing = dx >= 0 ? 'right' : 'left';\n   348\u2192    } else {\n   349\u2192      this.facing = dy > 0 ? 'down' : 'up';\n   350\u2192    }\n   351\u2192  }\n   352\u2192\n   353\u2192  _playAnim(type) {\n   354\u2192    if (!this.sprite) return;\n   355\u2192    const key = type === 'idle'\n   356\u2192      ? `${this.name}_idle_anim`\n   357\u2192      : `${this.name}_walk_${this.facing}`;\n   358\u2192    if (this.sprite.anims.currentAnim?.key !== key) {\n   359\u2192      this.sprite.play(key, true);\n   360\u2192    }\n   361\u2192    this.sprite.flipX = (type === 'walk' && this.facing === 'left');\n   362\u2192  }\n   363\u2192\n   364\u2192  /** Play the directional idle animation matching this.facing. */\n   365\u2192  _playDirectionalIdle() {\n   366\u2192    if (!this.sprite) return;\n   367\u2192    const key = `${this.name}_idle_${this.facing}`;\n   368\u2192    // Use scene.anims (global manager) \u2014 sprite.anims.exists only knows previously played keys\n   369\u2192    const manager = this.sprite.scene?.anims;\n   370\u2192    if (manager?.exists(key)) {\n   371\u2192      if (this.sprite.anims.currentAnim?.key !== key) {\n   372\u2192        this.sprite.play(key, true);\n   373\u2192      }\n   374\u2192      this.sprite.flipX = false;\n   375\u2192    } else {\n   376\u2192      // Fallback to generic idle\n   377\u2192      this._playAnim('idle');\n   378\u2192    }\n   379\u2192  }\n   380\u2192\n   381\u2192  _playWalkAnim() {\n   382\u2192    this._playAnim('walk');\n   383\u2192  }\n   384\u2192}\n   385\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}

Now I understand the code structure. Let me also look at the Engine.js and objects.js to understand the full picture before writing tests. Key findings so far: 1. **FRG-204**: `WALK_DIRS` in `scene.js` now correctly has `{ key: 'up', start: 48, end: 53 }` (not `start: 0, end: 3`). The test needs to verify that the walk-up animation uses frames 48-53. 2. **FRG-244**: `_playDirectionalIdle()` is implemented in `Character.js` and `startTalk()` calls it. The test should verify that when talking, `{name}_idle_{facing}` is played, not `{name}_idle_anim`. 3. **FRG-246**: For the stove assets, I can verify they're different by comparing file sizes of stove_off.png and stove_on.png. Now I need to check Engine.js for the `_updateCharacters` method that's used in existing tests, and look at objects.js to understand how objects are defined.

SUCCESS 
     1→/**
     2→ * Engine.js — SimulationEngine: manages characters, object state machines, and
     3→ * default random-wander behaviour (with 30% chance of object interaction).
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { ObjectEngine, OBJECT_DEFS } from './objects.js';
     7→
     8→export class SimulationEngine {
     9→  /**
    10→   * @param {object}      config
    11→   * @param {boolean[][]} config.walkable   - walkable[row][col]
    12→   * @param {number}      config.gridCols
    13→   * @param {number}      config.gridRows
    14→   * @param {object}      [config.aiEngine] - AIEngine instance (optional)
    15→   */
    16→  constructor(config = {}) {
    17→    this.walkable  = config.walkable  || [];
    18→    this.gridCols  = config.gridCols  || 19;
    19→    this.gridRows  = config.gridRows  || 13;
    20→    this.aiEngine  = config.aiEngine  || null;
    21→
    22→    this.characters = [];
    23→
    24→    // Object state machine engine — pre-loaded with Japanese Home objects
    25→    this.objectEngine = new ObjectEngine();
    26→    this.objectEngine.addObjects(OBJECT_DEFS);
    27→
    28→    // Events queued for scene to consume (emoji floats, etc.)
    29→    this.pendingEvents = [];
    30→
    31→    // Sim clock — starts at 08:00 AM game-time
    32→    this.simTimeMs = 8 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
    33→    // 1 real second = 60 sim seconds  (1 real min = 1 sim hour)
    34→    this.simSpeed  = 60;
    35→
    36→    // Cache walkable tiles list (built lazily)
    37→    this._walkableTiles = null;
    38→  }
    39→
    40→  addCharacter(char) {
    41→    this.characters.push(char);
    42→  }
    43→
    44→  // ── Main update loop ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    45→
    46→  update(deltaMs) {
    47→    // Advance sim clock
    48→    this.simTimeMs += deltaMs * this.simSpeed;
    49→
    50→    // Optional AI decisions — pass live object list so AI sees current states
    51→    if (this.aiEngine) {
    52→      this.aiEngine.update(deltaMs, this.characters, this.objectEngine.getAll(), {
    53→        timeString: this.getSimTimeString(),
    54→        findAdjacentWalkable: this._findAdjacentWalkable.bind(this),
    55→        buildDynamicWalkable: (char) => this._buildDynamicWalkable(char),
    56→        pushEvent: (ev) => this.pendingEvents.push(ev),
    57→      });
    58→    }
    59→
    60→    // Tick object state machines; collect auto-transition events
    61→    const objEvents = this.objectEngine.tick(deltaMs);
    62→    for (const ev of objEvents) {
    63→      if (ev.emoji || ev.spriteKey) this.pendingEvents.push(ev);
    64→    }
    65→
    66→    for (const char of this.characters) {
    67→      // Defensive release: if interactTarget is set but the character is no longer
    68→      // interacting, an external change (e.g. a stale async AI decision resolving
    69→      // between frames) moved them out of 'interacting' without triggering the
    70→      // normal detection below.  Release the slot now so the object doesn't stay
    71→      // permanently full.
    72→      if (char.interactTarget && char.action !== 'interacting') {
    73→        console.warn(
    74→          `[Engine] defensive release: ${char.name} has interactTarget ` +
    75→          `'${char.interactTarget}' but action='${char.action}' — releasing slot`
    76→        );
    77→        this.objectEngine.release(char.interactTarget, char.name);
    78→        char.interactTarget = null;
    79→      }
    80→
    81→      // Save pre-update state to detect interaction-end transitions
    82→      const prevAction         = char.action;
    83→      const prevInteractTarget = char.interactTarget;
    84→
    85→      // Handle arrival: character reached target object tile
    86→      if (char.action === 'idle' && char.targetObjectId) {
    87→        this._handleObjectArrival(char);
    88→      }
    89→
    90→      // Handle arrival: character reached talk target
    91→      if (char.action === 'idle' && char.pendingTalkTarget) {
    92→        this._handleTalkArrival(char);
    93→      }
    94→
    95→      // Mid-walk: periodically re-route toward a moving talk target
    96→      if (char.action === 'walking' && char.pendingTalkTarget) {
    97→        char._talkRetargetTimer -= deltaMs;
    98→        if (char._talkRetargetTimer <= 0) {
    99→          char._talkRetargetTimer = 2000; // re-check every 2 s
   100→          this._updateTalkRoute(char);

✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→/**
     2→ * objects.js — interactive object state machines for the Japanese Home scene.
     3→ *
     4→ * Each object definition has:
     5→ *   { id, name, position: { tx, ty }, interactMs, state, states }
     6→ *
     7→ * Each state can have:
     8→ *   transitions: { use: { next: 'stateName' } }   — triggered by interact()
     9→ *   auto_next:   { state: 'stateName', after: N }  — fires automatically after N seconds
    10→ *   speech:      'string'                           — shown as character speech bubble
    11→ *   emoji:       '🍳'                               — floating emoji on transition
    12→ */
    13→
    14→// ── Object definitions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    15→// Japanese Home (912×642, 19×13 tiles, 48px each).
    16→// Walkable interior: cols 1–17, rows 2–9.
    17→
    18→export const OBJECT_DEFS = [
    19→  {
    20→    id: 'stove',
    21→    name: 'stove',
    22→    capacity: 1,
    23→    interactAnim: 'phone',
    24→    position: { tx: 3, ty: 3 },
    25→    interactMs: 8000,
    26→    state: 'idle',
    27→    spriteMap: { idle: 'stove_off', cooking: 'stove_on', done: 'stove_on' },
    28→    states: {
    29→      idle: {
    30→        transitions: { use: { next: 'cooking' } },
    31→      },
    32→      cooking: {
    33→        speech: 'Let me cook something...',
    34→        auto_next: { state: 'done', after: 30 },
    35→      },
    36→      done: {
    37→        emoji: '🍳',
    38→        speech: "Dinner's ready! 🍳",
    39→        auto_next: { state: 'idle', after: 60 },
    40→        transitions: { use: { next: 'idle' } },
    41→      },
    42→    },
    43→  },
    44→  {
    45→    id: 'fridge',
    46→    name: 'fridge',
    47→    capacity: 1,
    48→    interactAnim: 'phone',
    49→    position: { tx: 2, ty: 3 },
    50→    interactMs: 5000,
    51→    state: 'idle',
    52→    states: {
    53→      idle: {
    54→        transitions: { use: { next: 'open' } },
    55→      },
    56→      open: {
    57→        speech: 'Hmm, what to eat...',
    58→        auto_next: { state: 'idle', after: 10 },
    59→        transitions: { use: { next: 'idle' } },
    60→      },
    61→    },
    62→  },
    63→  {
    64→    id: 'tea_set',
    65→    name: 'tea set',
    66→    capacity: 1,
    67→    interactAnim: 'phone',
    68→    position: { tx: 5, ty: 5 },
    69→    interactMs: 6000,
    70→    state: 'idle',
    71→    spriteMap: { idle: 'cup_empty', brewing: 'cup_full', ready: 'cup_full' },
    72→    states: {
    73→      idle: {
    74→        transitions: { use: { next: 'brewing' } },
    75→      },
    76→      brewing: {
    77→        speech: 'Brewing some tea~',
    78→        auto_next: { state: 'ready', after: 15 },
    79→      },
    80→      ready: {

5\u2192 *   { id, name, position: { tx, ty }, interactMs, state, states }\n     6\u2192 *\n     7\u2192 * Each state can have:\n     8\u2192 *   transitions: { use: { next: 'stateName' } }   \u2014 triggered by interact()\n     9\u2192 *   auto_next:   { state: 'stateName', after: N }  \u2014 fires automatically after N seconds\n    10\u2192 *   speech:      'string' ASSISTANTMESSAGE #80
Key observations: 1. `stove_off.png` = 395 bytes, `stove_on.png` = 430 bytes - they're different sizes, confirming the fix is in place. 2. Character walk spritesheets are ~42-43KB each (large files with many frames). 3. The idle spritesheets are ~5KB each with 24 frames (4 directions × 6 frames). Now let me check the `_updateFacing` method in Character.js that I missed, and also examine the `_startConversation` method in Engine.js. Actually, wait. Looking at the Character.js, I don't see `_updateFacing` method - it's in the old character-facing.spec.js tests. In the new code, I see `_updateFacingFromNextTile()` for walk direction, `_playDirectionalIdle()` that plays the directional idle animations, and `startTalk()` which calls `_playDirectionalIdle()` at the end. But I'm noticing `_updateFacing(dx, dy)` is referenced in the old test specs but doesn't appear in the current implementation - I need to search for where it actually exists. Looking at the current master version, the character-facing.spec.js tests were written for an older version of the code. Let me focus on what's actually in the current codebase. Now I'm planning out tests for the three bug fixes. For FRG-204, I need to add a test to walk-animation.spec.js that verifies the walk-up animation uses the correct frame range instead of the wrong one. For FRG-244, I need to test that when `startTalk()` is called, it properly invokes `_playDirectionalIdle()` and plays the correct idle animation based on the current facing direction. For FRG-246, I can either compare file sizes between stove_off.png and stove_on.png or use canvas pixel data comparison. Now I'm realizing the port situation is more complex—the existing tests were written for different branch-specific servers at 5173 and 5174, but master is running at 5177, so I need to either update those tests or write new ones that all run against the current master instance. The existing tests cover movement behavior but don't validate the actual Phaser animation frame definitions, so I need to add a test that checks the walk-up animation is configured to use frames 48-53 instead of 0-3. The current test approach using `moveTo` won't catch this kind of animation setup bug, so I'll need to inspect the animation object directly to verify the frame ranges are correct. For the FRG-244 issue, I'm seeing that characters are playing the wrong idle animation during conversations—they're using a generic idle instead of the directional variant. I need to write a test that starts a conversation and verifies the sprite is playing the correct facing-specific animation key. For FRG-246, the stove sprites are visually identical when they shouldn't be, so I'll add a test that loads both images and compares their pixel data to confirm they're actually different. Now I'm deciding which test to prioritize—the animation frame range check for FRG-204 seems like the most straightforward starting point since I can leverage the existing scene exposure pattern. I'll use `window._gameScene` injection to access the animation manager directly. For the walk-animation test, I can inject the scene reference and then verify that animations like `Amelia_walk_up` pull frames 48-53 with a total count of 6 frames instead of 4. I'm also planning two more tests: one to check that characters maintain their directional idle animation during conversations, and another to verify that the stove PNG assets are visually distinct by comparing their file sizes or pixel data. Looking at the injection patterns across the test files, I notice that `_gameEngine` is already exposed in the scene's `create()` method, so I don't need to inject it separately. For `_gameScene`, I'll use the same injection approach that works in the object-sprite-swap tests by modifying the scene.js file directly. Since these new tests target port 5177, I need to make sure I'm using the correct injection pattern and verifying the right properties for each test case. Now examining what the task actually requires versus what's already implemented: the FRG-204 bug test needs to verify that when characters walk in all four directions, the sprite displays at normal size (48×96 pixels, not the thumbnail dimensions) and the walk animation plays smoothly without glitches or stretching. The fix involved switching from row 0 (which contains thumbnails) to row 1 (which has the actual character frames), so I need to check that the sprite is pulling from the correct row during movement. For FRG-244, after a conversation starts and `_playDirectionalIdle()` is called, the animation key should be formatted as `{characterName}_idle_{direction}` rather than `{characterName}_idle_anim`, which means the directional idle animation is being selected correctly based on which way the character is facing. The test setup needs to ensure both characters have access to the scene's animation manager so that when `startTalk()` triggers the directional idle animation, the sprite can actually play the correct keyed animation and we can verify it's using the right one. the browser, the animations are registered. But we need `window._gameScene` to be exposed for checking. Actually, wait. Looking at how walk-animation.spec.js tests work - they call `nyx.update(1000)` directly in page.evaluate. Similarly for talk tests, they can call `maple.startTalk(sol, 5000)` and check `maple.sprite.anims.currentAnim.key`. But there's a subtlety: `_playDirectionalIdle` checks `manager?.exists(key)` where `manager = this.sprite.scene?.anims`. This requires the sprite to be part of a Phaser scene. Since the page is loaded and Phaser is running, the sprites should have their scene references. **Bug 3 (FRG-246)**: The simplest test is to fetch the two PNG files and compare their content. Since the server is running, I can fetch them directly or do a server-side comparison. Looking at the existing object-sprite-swap tests, they already verify that texture keys change and sprites update correctly. The new test for FRG-246 needs to specifically confirm that stove_on.png and stove_off.png have different pixel data, not just different keys. For the FRG-204 tests, I'll add them to walk-animation.spec.js. Now I need to figure out the port situation. The existing tests use hardcoded ports (5173, 5174, 5176) but the task says to run against a local server. I should check if those ports are still valid or if I need to update them to 5177. I'm realizing the existing tests were written for a feature branch running on specific ports, but now I'm testing against master on 5177. I should either update the existing test files to use 5177, or create new spec files for the master branch tests. The task says "Run tests against that local server," which suggests using the actual running server at 5177. Let me look at the existing test files to see how they're structured and whether they hardcode the ports. The tests are indeed locked to specific feature branch servers—walk-animation targets 5174, character-facing targets 5173, and object-sprite-swap targets 5176. Since master at 5177 now contains all these fixes, I need to decide whether to update those URLs or create separate tests. Changing the URLs would break the original feature branch tests if those servers are still running, so I should add new test sections within each file that specifically target 5177 to validate the fixes on master. The injection patterns these tests use are looking for specific markers in the old index.html structure, but the current codebase has already integrated those features directly into scene.js, so the injection approach is outdated. I need to verify what's actually in the current index.html to see if the injection pattern will work with the updated LOCAL_URL.
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

  "content": {
    "type": "thinking",
    "thinking": "Key observations:\n1. `stove_off.png` = 395 bytes, `stove_on.png` = 430 bytes - they're different sizes, confirming the fix is in place.\n\n2. Character walk spritesheets are ~42-43KB each (large files with many frames).\n\n3. The idle spritesheets are ~5KB each with 24 frames (4 directions \u00d7 6 frames).\n\nNow let me check the `_updateFacing` method in Character.js that I missed, and also examine the `_startConversation` method in Engine.js.\n\nActually, wait. Looking at the Character.js, I don't see `_updateFacing` method - it's in the old character-facing.spec.js tests. \n\nIn the new code, I see `_updateFacingFromNextTile()` for walk direction, `_playDirectionalIdle()` that plays the directional idle animations, and `startTalk()` which calls `_playDirectionalIdle()` at the end. But I'm noticing `_updateFacing(dx, dy)` is referenced in the old test specs but doesn't appear in the current implementation - I need to search for where it actually exists.\n\nLooking at the current master version, the character-facing.spec.js tests were written for an older version of the code. Let me focus on what's actually in the current codebase.\n\nNow I'm planning out tests for the three bug fixes. For FRG-204, I need to add a test to walk-animation.spec.js that verifies the walk-up animation uses the correct frame range instead of the wrong one. For FRG-244, I need to test that when `startTalk()` is called, it properly invokes `_playDirectionalIdle()` and plays the correct idle animation based on the current facing direction.\n\nFor FRG-246, I can either compare file sizes between stove_off.png and stove_on.png or use canvas pixel data comparison. Now I'm realizing the port situation is more complex\u2014the existing tests were written for different branch-specific servers at 5173 and 5174, but master is running at 5177, so I need to either update those tests or write new ones that all run against the current master instance. The existing tests cover movement behavior but don't validate the actual Phaser animation frame definitions, so I need to add a test that checks the walk-up animation is configured to use frames 48-53 instead of 0-3. The current test approach using `moveTo` won't catch this kind of animation setup bug, so I'll need to inspect the animation object directly to verify the frame ranges are correct.\n\nFor the FRG-244 issue, I'm seeing that characters are playing the wrong idle animation during conversations\u2014they're using a generic idle instead of the directional variant. I need to write a test that starts a conversation and verifies the sprite is playing the correct facing-specific animation key.\n\nFor FRG-246, the stove sprites are visually identical when they shouldn't be, so I'll add a test that loads both images and compares their pixel data to confirm they're actually different.\n\nNow I'm deciding which test to prioritize\u2014the animation frame range check for FRG-204 seems like the most straightforward starting point since I can leverage the existing scene exposure pattern. I'll use `window._gameScene` injection to access the animation manager directly. For the walk-animation test, I can inject the scene reference and then verify that animations like `Amelia_walk_up` pull frames 48-53 with a total count of 6 frames instead of 4.\n\nI'm also planning two more tests: one to check that characters maintain their directional idle animation during conversations, and another to verify that the stove PNG assets are visually distinct by comparing their file sizes or pixel data.\n\nLooking at the injection patterns across the test files, I notice that `_gameEngine` is already exposed in the scene's `create()` method, so I don't need to inject it separately. For `_gameScene`, I'll use the same injection approach that works in the object-sprite-swap tests by modifying the scene.js file directly.\n\nSince these new tests target port 5177, I need to make sure I'm using the correct injection pattern and verifying the right properties for each test case.\n\nNow examining what the task actually requires versus what's already implemented: the FRG-204 bug test needs to verify that when characters walk in all four directions, the sprite displays at normal size (48\u00d796 pixels, not the thumbnail dimensions) and the walk animation plays smoothly without glitches or stretching. The fix involved switching from row 0 (which contains thumbnails) to row 1 (which has the actual character frames), so I need to check that the sprite is pulling from the correct row during movement.\n\nFor FRG-244, after a conversation starts and `_playDirectionalIdle()` is called, the animation key should be formatted as `{characterName}_idle_{direction}` rather than `{characterName}_idle_anim`, which means the directional idle animation is being selected correctly based on which way the character is facing.\n\nThe test setup needs to ensure both characters have access to the scene's animation manager so that when `startTalk()` triggers the directional idle animation, the sprite can actually play the correct keyed animation and we can verify it's using the right one. the browser, the animations are registered. But we need `window._gameScene` to be exposed for checking.\n\nActually, wait. Looking at how walk-animation.spec.js tests work - they call `nyx.update(1000)` directly in page.evaluate. Similarly for talk tests, they can call `maple.startTalk(sol, 5000)` and check `maple.sprite.anims.currentAnim.key`.\n\nBut there's a subtlety: `_playDirectionalIdle` checks `manager?.exists(key)` where `manager = this.sprite.scene?.anims`. This requires the sprite to be part of a Phaser scene. Since the page is loaded and Phaser is running, the sprites should have their scene references.\n\n**Bug 3 (FRG-246)**:\nThe simplest test is to fetch the two PNG files and compare their content. Since the server is running, I can fetch them directly or do a server-side comparison. Looking at the existing object-sprite-swap tests, they already verify that texture keys change and sprites update correctly. The new test for FRG-246 needs to specifically confirm that stove_on.png and stove_off.png have different pixel data, not just different keys.\n\nFor the FRG-204 tests, I'll add them to walk-animation.spec.js.\n\nNow I need to figure out the port situation. The existing tests use hardcoded ports (5173, 5174, 5176) but the task says to run against a local server. I should check if those ports are still valid or if I need to update them to 5177.\n\nI'm realizing the existing tests were written for a feature branch running on specific ports, but now I'm testing against master on 5177. ASSISTANTMESSAGE #84
So `window._gameEngine = this.engine;` is already set unconditionally in `scene.js create()`, as the object-sprite-swap.spec.js comment says. No injection is needed for `_gameEngine`. For `_gameScene`, I need to inject it. The injection in `object-sprite-swap.spec.js` works by intercepting `scene.js` requests and replacing: ```javascript 'window._gameEngine = this.engine;' ``` with: ```javascript 'window._gameEngine = this.engine;\n window._gameScene = this;' ``` This still works for the master branch since `scene.js` still has `window._gameEngine = this.engine;`. Now I'm planning how to update the test files for FRG-204. The current walk-animation tests target localhost:5174 with an injection that looks for a comment that doesn't exist in master, so I'll need to write new tests for the master branch at 5177 that leverage the existing `_gameEngine` exposure and inject `_gameScene` to access the animation manager. For the test itself, I need to verify the walk-up sprite row by checking the frame indices. In Phaser 3, when animations are created with `generateFrameNumbers`, each frame object has a `frame` property with a `name` that represents the frame number as a string, so I can map through the frames and parse those names to get the numeric indices and verify they're in the 48-53 range. I'm checking the frame dimensions to verify they match the expected "normal size" of 48x96 pixels. Now I need to decide how to structure the tests — I could add new test blocks to the existing spec files using port 5177, or update the LOCAL_URL globally, but changing the existing URLs would break tests that depend on the other branch servers. The cleanest approach is to add new test cases alongside the existing ones that specifically target the local server. However, there's a complication with the walk-animation tests: the injection logic that tries to replace a specific comment string in the HTML won't work on master since that string doesn't exist there. Since `window._gameEngine` is already exposed in master's scene.js, the injection isn't necessary — the test should access the engine directly without needing to inject it first. The injection in object-sprite-swap.spec.js similarly adds `window._gameScene = this;` after the existing engine assignment, which works fine since master already has that line. So my plan is to update walk-animation.spec.js to use the correct localhost port, but I need to verify whether the character lookup uses `id` or `name` since the Character.js constructor only defines a `name` property, not an `id`.

USERMESSAGE #86
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
   100→          this._updateTalkRoute(char);
   101→        }
   102→      }
   103→
   104→      // Trigger random wander (or object visit) when idle and timer expired
   105→      if (char.action === 'idle' && char.wanderTimer <= 0) {
   106→        this._doWander(char);
   107→      }
   108→
   109→      // Tick speech timer — fire a random line when it expires
   110→      if (char.dialogue.length > 0 && char.pendingSpeech === null) {
   111→        char.speechTimer -= deltaMs;
   112→        if (char.speechTimer <= 0) {
   113→          const idx = Math.floor(Math.random() * char.dialogue.length);
   114→          char.pendingSpeech = char.dialogue[idx];
   115→          char.speechTimer   = 20000 + Math.random() * 20000; // reset 20–40 s
   116→        }
   117→      }
   118→
   119→      // Per-character update (movement, animations, sprite sync)
   120→      char.update(deltaMs);
   121→
   122→      // Detect interaction end: release slot so other characters can use the object
   123→      if (prevAction === 'interacting' && char.action !== 'interacting' && prevInteractTarget) {
   124→        this.objectEngine.release(prevInteractTarget, char.name);
   125→      }
   126→    }
   127→  }
   128→
   129→  // ── Object interaction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   130→
   131→  /**
   132→   * Called when a character arrives at an object's adjacent tile.
   133→   * Fires the 'use' transition and puts the character into 'interacting' state.
   134→   * If the object has no valid transition, finds an alternative instead of idling.
   135→   */
   136→  _handleObjectArrival(char) {
   137→    const objectId = char.targetObjectId;
   138→    const action   = char.pendingAction || 'use';
   139→
   140→    // Clear targeting immediately so we don't re-fire on the next idle frame.
   141→    // _fallbackToAlternativeObject may set a new targetObjectId if it finds one.
   142→    char.targetObjectId = null;
   143→    char.pendingAction  = null;
   144→
   145→    const event = this.objectEngine.interact(objectId, action);
   146→
   147→    if (event) {
   148→      // Convert reservation → active occupancy
   149→      this.objectEngine.arrive(objectId, char.name);
   150→
   151→      const obj = this.objectEngine.getById(objectId);
   152→
   153→      // Hold the character for the full auto_next duration if the new state has one;
   154→      // otherwise fall back to the object's generic interactMs.
   155→      let holdMs = obj ? obj.interactMs : 8000;
   156→      if (obj && event.state) {
   157→        const stateDef = obj.states[event.state];
   158→        if (stateDef?.auto_next?.after) {
   159→          holdMs = stateDef.auto_next.after * 1000;
   160→        }
   161→      }
   162→
   163→      // Face toward the object tile before entering interaction
   164→      if (obj) {
   165→        const dx = obj.tx - Math.round(char.tx);
   166→        const dy = obj.ty - Math.round(char.ty);
   167→        if (Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)) {
   168→          char.facing = dx >= 0 ? 'right' : 'left';
   169→        } else {
   170→          char.facing = dy >= 0 ? 'down' : 'up';
   171→        }
   172→      }
   173→
   174→      char.startInteraction(objectId, holdMs, obj?.interactAnim ?? null);
   175→
   176→      if (event.speech)    char.pendingSpeech = event.speech;
   177→      if (event.emoji || event.spriteKey) this.pendingEvents.push(event);
   178→    } else {
   179→      // No valid transition (object changed state mid-walk) — release the
   180→      // reservation and try to find an alternative object.
   181→      this.objectEngine.release(objectId, char.name);
   182→      this._fallbackToAlternativeObject(char, objectId);
   183→    }
   184→  }
   185→
   186→  // ── Talk handshake ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   187→
   188→  /**
   189→   * Called when a character arrives at the tile adjacent to their talk target.
   190→   * Checks proximity and re-evaluates B's availability before starting conversation.
   191→   */
   192→  _handleTalkArrival(char) {
   193→    const target = char.pendingTalkTarget;
   194→    char.pendingTalkTarget = null;
   195→
   196→    // Proximity check: target may have moved significantly during the walk.
   197→    const dx   = Math.round(target.tx) - Math.round(char.tx);
   198→    const dy   = Math.round(target.ty) - Math.round(char.ty);
   199→    const dist = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);
   200→
   201→    if (dist > 3) {
   202→      // Target has drifted — try to follow if not too far
   203→      if (dist <= 10) {
   204→        const dynWalkable = this._buildDynamicWalkable(char);
   205→        const adjTile = this._findAdjacentWalkable(Math.round(target.tx), Math.round(target.ty), dynWalkable);
   206→        if (adjTile && char.moveTo(adjTile.tx, adjTile.ty, dynWalkable)) {
   207→          // Re-attach pending talk so we retry on next arrival
   208→          char.pendingTalkTarget   = target;
   209→          char._talkRetargetTimer  = 2000;
   210→          return;
   211→        }
   212→      }
   213→      // Too far or unreachable — abort
   214→      this.pendingEvents.push({
   215→        type:      'fallback',
   216→        charLabel: char.label,
   217→        reason:    'talk_target_moved',
   218→        fromLabel: target.label,
   219→        toLabel:   null,
   220→      });
   221→      char.pendingTalkDialogue = null;
   222→      char.pendingTalkResponse = null;
   223→      char.wanderTimer = 3000 + Math.random() * 5000;
   224→      return;
   225→    }
   226→
   227→    // Re-check availability: accept if idle or walking, reject if busy
   228→    const canTalk = target.action === 'idle' || target.action === 'walking';
   229→
   230→    if (!canTalk) {
   231→      this.pendingEvents.push({
   232→        type:           'talk_rejected',
   233→        initiatorLabel: char.label,
   234→        targetLabel:    target.label,
   235→      });
   236→      char.wanderTimer = 3000 + Math.random() * 5000;
   237→      return;
   238→    }
   239→
   240→    this._startConversation(char, target);
   241→  }
   242→
   243→  /**
   244→   * Configurable conversation duration range (ms).
   245→   * Defaults to 10–15 s; override via constructor config.
   246→   */
   247→  get _talkDurationMs() {
   248→    return 10_000 + Math.random() * 5_000;
   249→  }
