=== 'interacting' && char.action !== 'interacting' && prevInteractTarget) {\n   124\u2192        this.objectEngine.release(prevInteractTarget, char.name);\n   125\u2192      }\n   126\u2192    }\n   127\u2192  }\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 Object interaction \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192  /**\n   132\u2192   * Called when a character arrives at an object's adjacent tile.\n   133\u2192   * Fires the 'use' transition and puts the character into 'interacting' state.\n   134\u2192   * If the object has no valid transition, finds an alternative instead of idling.\n   135\u2192   */\n   136\u2192  _handleObjectArrival(char) {\n   137\u2192    const objectId = char.targetObjectId;\n   138\u2192    const action   = char.pendingAction || 'use';\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192    // Clear targeting immediately so we don't re-fire on the next idle frame.\n   141\u2192    // _fallbackToAlternativeObject may set a new targetObjectId if it finds one.\n   142\u2192    char.targetObjectId = null;\n   143\u2192    char.pendingAction  = null;\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192    const event = this.objectEngine.interact(objectId, action);\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192    if (event) {\n   148\u2192      // Convert reservation \u2192 active occupancy\n   149\u2192      this.objectEngine.arrive(objectId, char.name);\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192      const obj = this.objectEngine.getById(objectId);\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192      // Hold the character for the full auto_next duration if the new state has one;\n   154\u2192      // otherwise fall back to the object's generic interactMs.\n   155\u2192      let holdMs = obj ? obj.interactMs : 8000;\n   156\u2192      if (obj && event.state) {\n   157\u2192        const stateDef = obj.states[event.state];\n   158\u2192        if (stateDef?.auto_next?.after) {\n   159\u2192          holdMs = stateDef.auto_next.after * 1000;\n   160\u2192        }\n   161\u2192      }\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192      // Face toward the object tile before entering interaction\n   164\u2192      if (obj) {\n   165\u2192        const dx = obj.tx - Math.round(char.tx);\n   166\u2192        const dy = obj.ty - Math.round(char.ty);\n   167\u2192        if (Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)) {\n   168\u2192          char.facing = dx >= 0 ? 'right' : 'left';\n   169\u2192        } else {\n   170\u2192          char.facing = dy >= 0 ? 'down' : 'up';\n   171\u2192        }\n   172\u2192      }\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192      char.startInteraction(objectId, holdMs, obj?.interactAnim ?? null);\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192      if (event.speech)    char.pendingSpeech = event.speech;\n   177\u2192      if (event.emoji || event.spriteKey) this.pendingEvents.push(event);\n   178\u2192    } else {\n   179\u2192      // No valid transition (object changed state mid-walk) \u2014 release the\n   180\u2192      // reservation and try to find an alternative object.\n   181\u2192      this.objectEngine.release(objectId, char.name);\n   182\u2192      this._fallbackToAlternativeObject(char, objectId);\n   183\u2192    }\n   184\u2192  }\n   185\u2192\n   186\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 Talk handshake \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192  /**\n   189\u2192   * Called when a character arrives at the tile adjacent to their talk target.\n   190\u2192   * Checks proximity and re-evaluates B's availability before starting conversation.\n   191\u2192   */\n   192\u2192  _handleTalkArrival(char) {\n   193\u2192    const target = char.pendingTalkTarget;\n   194\u2192    char.pendingTalkTarget = null;\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192    // Proximity check: target may have moved significantly during the walk.\n   197\u2192    const dx   = Math.round(target.tx) - Math.round(char.tx);\n   198\u2192    const dy   = Math.round(target.ty) - Math.round(char.ty);\n   199\u2192    const dist = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);\n   200\u2192\n   201\u2192    if (dist > 3) {\n   202\u2192      // Target has drifted \u2014 try to follow if not too far\n   203\u2192      if (dist <= 10) {\n   204\u2192        const dynWalkable = this._buildDynamicWalkable(char);\n   205\u2192        const adjTile = this._findAdjacentWalkable(Math.round(target.tx), Math.round(target.ty), dynWalkable);\n   206\u2192        if (adjTile && char.moveTo(adjTile.tx, adjTile.ty, dynWalkable)) {\n   207\u2192          // Re-attach pending talk so we retry on next arrival\n   208\u2192          char.pendingTalkTarget   = target;\n   209\u2192          char._talkRetargetTimer  = 2000;\n   210\u2192          return;\n   211\u2192        }\n   212\u2192      }\n   213\u2192      // Too far or unreachable \u2014 abort\n   214\u2192      this.pendingEvents.push({\n   215\u2192        type:      'fallback',\n   216\u2192 221\u2192      char.pendingTalkDialogue = null;\n   222\u2192      char.pendingTalkResponse = null;\n   223\u2192      char.wanderTimer = 3000 + Math.random() * 5000;\n   224\u2192      return;\n   225\u2192    }\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192    // Re-check availability: accept if idle or walking, reject if busy\n   228\u2192    const canTalk = target.action === 'idle' || target.action === 'walking';\n   229\u2192\n   230\u2192    if (!canTalk) {\n   231\u2192      this.pendingEvents.push({\n   232\u2192        type:           'talk_rejected',\n   233\u2192        initiatorLabel:    248→    return 10_000 + Math.random() * 5_000;
   249→  }
   250→
   251→  /**
   252→   * Synchronise charA and charB into a shared 'talking' state.
   253→   * Both face each other, AI timers are paused (via action check in AIEngine),
   254→   * and conversation ends naturally when the timer expires.
   255→   */
   256→  _startConversation(charA, charB) {
   257→    // Release any pending object reservation charB held while walking.
   258→    // startTalk() clears targetObjectId without calling objectEngine.release(),
   259→    // so we must do it here before the slot becomes permanently orphaned.
   260→    if (charB.targetObjectId) {
   261→      this.objectEngine.release(charB.targetObjectId, charB.name);
   262→      charB.targetObjectId = null;
   263→      charB.pendingAction  = null;
   264→    }
   265→
   266→    const duration = this._talkDurationMs;
   267→
   268→    // Face each other
   269→    const dx = Math.round(charB.tx) - Math.round(charA.tx);
   270→    const dy = Math.round(charB.ty) - Math.round(charA.ty);
   271→    if (Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)) {
   272→      charA.facing = dx >= 0 ? 'right' : 'left';
   273→      charB.facing = dx >= 0 ? 'left'  : 'right';
   274→    } else {
   275→      charA.facing = dy >= 0 ? 'down' : 'up';
   276→      charB.facing = dy >= 0 ? 'up'   : 'down';
   277→    }
   278→
   279→    // Transfer queued dialogue to speech bubbles
   280→    if (charA.pendingTalkDialogue) {
   281→      charA.pendingSpeech          = charA.pendingTalkDialogue;
   282→      charA.pendingTalkDialogue    = null;
   283→    }
   284→    if (charA.pendingTalkResponse) {
   285→      charB.pendingSpeech          = charA.pendingTalkResponse;
   286→      charA.pendingTalkResponse    = null;
   287→    }
   288→
   289→    charA.startTalk(charB, duration);
   290→    charB.startTalk(charA, duration);
   291→
   292→    this.pendingEvents.push({
   293→      type:           'conversation_start',
   294→      initiatorLabel: charA.label,
   295→      targetLabel:    charB.label,
   296→    });
   297→  }
   298→
   299→  // ── Wander ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   300→
   301→  _doWander(char) {
   302→    // Build a walkable grid that treats other characters' tiles as obstacles
   303→    const dynWalkable = this._buildDynamicWalkable(char);
   304→
   305→    // 30% chance: walk to a random interactive object instead of wandering freely
   306→    if (Math.random() < 0.3) {
   307→      const objects = this.objectEngine.getAll();
   308→      if (objects.length > 0) {
   309→        // Shuffle and try each until one has an available slot and a reachable adjacent tile
   310→        const shuffled = objects.slice().sort(() => Math.random() - 0.5);
   311→        for (const obj of shuffled) {
   312→          if (obj.isFull()) continue; // skip objects at capacity
   313→          // Only target objects whose current state accepts a 'use' interaction
   314→          const stateDef = obj.states[obj.state];
   315→          if (!stateDef?.transitions?.use) continue;
   316→          const tile = this._findAdjacentWalkable(obj.tx, obj.ty, dynWalkable);
   317→          if (tile && char.moveTo(tile.tx, tile.ty, dynWalkable)) {
   318→            obj.reserve(char.name); // claim a slot before walking
   319→            char.targetObjectId = obj.id;
   320→            char.pendingAction  = 'use';
   321→            return;
   322→          }
   323→        }
   324→        // All objects full/non-interactable/unreachable — fall through to random wander
   325→      }
   326→    }
   327→
   328→    // Random walkable tile
   329→    const tiles = this._getWalkableTiles();
   330→    if (tiles.length === 0) {
   331→      char.wanderTimer = 2000;
   332→      return;
   333→    }
   334→
   335→    const cx = Math.round(char.tx);
   336→    const cy = Math.round(char.ty);
   337→
   338→    let moved = false;
   339→    for (let attempt = 0; attempt < 15; attempt++) {
   340→      const tile = tiles[Math.floor(Math.random() * tiles.length)];
   341→      if (tile.tx === cx && tile.ty === cy) continue;
   342→      moved = char.moveTo(tile.tx, tile.ty, dynWalkable);
   343→      if (moved) break;
   344→    }
   345→
   346→    if (!moved) {
   347→      char._failedMoveCount++;

No `snapshot()` method and no `.id` property on characters. The characters are identified by `.name` (which is 'Amelia', 'Lucy', 'Ash') and `.label` (which is 'Maple', 'Sol', 'Nyx'). Now let me write the new tests. I'll append new test sections to each of the 3 relevant spec files, targeting port 5177. I'll use the character `.name` property ('Amelia', 'Lucy', 'Ash') instead of `.id`. Let me create the test approach for each: ### FRG-204: Walk-up sprite row test For the walk animation spec, I need to verify that the Amelia walk-up animation pulls frames from the correct row starting at index 48 with proper 48×96 dimensions, and that during actual walk-up movement the sprite cycles through frames 48-53. ### FRG-244: Directional idle during talk test For the character facing spec, I'll trigger a conversation between two characters and confirm both play the appropriate directional idle animation based on their facing direction. ### FRG-246: Stove PNG asset visual difference I need to load both stove sprite states and verify they're visually distinct by comparing their dimensions and pixel data. Now I'm thinking through how to access the animation system in Phaser when `window._gameScene` is exposed—I can retrieve animation frames directly and inspect their properties like frame indices and dimensions. For the walk test specifically, I need to grab the animation object, examine its frame array, and validate both the starting frame index and the frame range used during movement. I'm also checking whether `_updateCharacters` is a method on SimulationEngine so I understand the right place to hook into the character update cycle.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #100
There's no `_updateCharacters` method. The engine has `update(deltaMs)` which calls `char.update(deltaMs)`. There's no `_startConversation` exposed either (it's a private method). Actually wait, let me check if `_startConversation` is accessible from window._gameEngine... It is because `window._gameEngine = this.engine;` and `engine._startConversation` is a method on the engine object. JavaScript doesn't enforce private methods via naming convention only, so `window._gameEngine._startConversation(charA, charB)` should work. Let me now write the tests. I'll append new test sections to the three existing files. Now I'm planning out what to add to each test file. For walk-animation.spec.js, I need to verify that the walk-up animation uses frames 48-53 instead of the default 0-3. For character-facing.spec.js, I'm adding tests for directional idle animations during conversations. And for object-sprite-swap.spec.js, I'm checking that the stove PNG assets are visually distinct. All three will use port 5177 with scene injection. I'm keeping in mind the API constraints: character objects use `.name` and `.label` properties (not `.id`), I should call `char.update()` or `engine.update()` instead of the private `_updateCharacters()` method, and I can access animations through `window._gameScene.anims`. The `engine._startConversation()` method is available for triggering conversations, and when called it faces characters toward each other and updates their facing direction. Now I'm setting up the test file and need to inject scene exposure into the page so I can verify Phaser animation definitions are loaded correctly. Writing test setup code... Writing test assertions... Writing test assertions... Writing test assertions... Writing test assertions... I'm checking what animation is currently playing and extracting the frame numbers from the walk-up animation to verify the movement is working correctly, then logging the results to see the facing direction and animation state. Now I'm verifying the test assertions—making sure the movement succeeded, Amelia is facing the right direction, and critically, that the walk-up animation is using the correct frame range (48-53) rather than the thumbnail frames (0-3) that were causing the FRG-204 bug. I need to double-check how the scene.js injection works with the query string parameter. The import statement includes `?v=15`, so the actual request URL will have that query string, and my route pattern should still match it correctly. Setting up the test infrastructure for FRG-244... Writing test logic... Now I'm verifying the conversation state by checking that both characters face each other correctly and play the right directional idle animations instead of a generic one. I'm also setting up another test to validate that directional idle animations match the facing direction across all four directions, starting with injecting the scene and waiting for the game engine to load three characters. Now I'm evaluating the animation state by cycling through each direction, setting Amelia's facing property, triggering the directional idle animation, and capturing which animation key is currently playing for each direction. Then I'm logging the results and asserting that each direction produces the correct animation key format (like `Amelia_idle_down` rather than a generic `Amelia_idle_anim`), which is a regression test to ensure the bug doesn't resurface. I'm also setting up another test case to verify the same behavior during dialogue. Now I'm setting up the test environment by injecting the scene and navigating to the local URL, then waiting for the game engine to load three characters. Once they're available, I'm positioning Ash above Lucy so they face each other, clearing their movement paths, and setting them to idle state. Then I'm triggering a conversation between them and checking what animation keys are playing and which direction each character is facing. I'm logging the results and verifying that Ash faces down while Lucy faces up based on their vertical positioning. Writing test assertions... Writing the test assertions... Writing pixel comparison logic... Comparing the PNG files byte-by-byte to check if they're identical, then logging the results and asserting they should match. Now I'm thinking about how to integrate these tests into the existing spec files — I need to append new test blocks to each one and verify the localhost ports match what's already configured in those files. I need to define a new constant for the master branch URL at the module level in each test file, but I should also check whether the existing injection patterns will work with the master branch code—particularly for the character-facing tests where the current injection method relies on a marker that doesn't exist in master, so I'll need to use the scene exposure pattern instead. Actually, I'm realizing I can simplify this significantly: for the FRG-244 tests I only need `_gameEngine` since the character sprites already have access to their scene through `this.sprite.scene.anims`, and for the FRG-204 tests I can traverse through the sprite to reach the scene and its animation registry rather than injecting `_gameScene` separately. Writing the test cases...

USERMESSAGE #105
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-204
     4→ * Walk animations stutter/freeze mid-movement due to timer accumulation issues.
     5→ *
     6→ * Root cause: walk frame timer was not using a while-loop, so large deltaMs values
     7→ * (e.g. when tab was backgrounded or frame rate dropped) only advanced walkFrame
     8→ * once per update() call, leaving walkTimer > walkFrameMs and causing the animation
     9→ * to stutter or freeze.
    10→ *

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

  "content": {

    "content": "     1\u2192'use strict';\n     2\u2192/**\n     3\u2192 * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-204\n     4\u2192 * Walk animations stutter/freeze mid-movement due to timer accumulation issues.\n     5\u2192 *\n     6\u2192 * Root cause: walk frame timer was not using a while-loop, so large deltaMs values\n     7\u2192 * (e.g. when tab was backgrounded or frame rate dropped) only advanced walkFrame\n     8\u2192 * once per update() call, leaving walkTimer > walkFrameMs and causing the animation\n     9\u2192 * to stutter or freeze.\n    10\u2192 *\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. Edit TOOL CALL 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/walk-animation.spec.js",
  "old_string": "    // (without the while-loop fix, walkTimer could be up to 1000ms after a large-delta update)\n    expect(result.timerViolations.length,\n      `walkTimer exceeded walkFrameMs (${WALK_FRAME_MS}ms) on ${result.timerViolations.length} step(s): ` +\n      JSON.stringify(result.timerViolations)\n    ).toBe(0);\n  });\n});",
  "new_string": "    // (without the while-loop fix, walkTimer could be up to 1000ms after a large-delta update)\n    expect(result.timerViolations.length,\n      `walkTimer exceeded walkFrameMs (${WALK_FRAME_MS}ms) on ${result.timerViolations.length} step(s): ` +\n      JSON.stringify(result.timerViolations)\n    ).toBe(0);\n  });\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 FRG-204 (sprite-row fix): walk-up uses row 1 frames (48-53), not row 0 \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n//\n// Runs against the master dev server at localhost:5177.\n// window._gameEngine is set unconditionally in scene.js create() \u2014 no injection needed.\n// The Phaser scene is accessible via character.sprite.scene.\n//\n// Bug: WALK_DIRS had { key: 'up', start: 0, end: 3 } \u2014 row 0 contains tiny placeholder\n//      thumbnails, causing the walk-up animation to render with wrong-sized frames.\n// Fix: Changed to { key: 'up', start: 48, end: 53 } \u2014 row 1 is the real up-walk frames.\n\nconst LOCAL_URL_MASTER = 'http://localhost:5177';\n\ntest.describe('FRG-204 (sprite-row): walk-up uses frames 48-53, not row 0 placeholders', () => {\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 5: walk-up Phaser animation is registered with frames 48-53 \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('Amelia/Lucy/Ash walk_up animation uses frames 48-53 (row 1), not 0-5 (row 0 thumbnails)', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL_MASTER);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const results = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      // Access the Phaser scene via the first character's sprite\n      const scene = engine.characters[0]?.sprite?.scene;\n      if (!scene) return { error: 'Phaser scene not accessible via sprite.scene' };\n\n      const names = ['Amelia', 'Lucy', 'Ash'];\n      const animResults = {};\n\n      for (const name of names) {\n        const anim = scene.anims.get(`${name}_walk_up`);\n        if (!anim) { animResults[name] = { error: `${name}_walk_up animation not found` }; continue; }\n\n        const frameNums = anim.frames.map(f => parseInt(f.frame.name, 10));\n        const firstFrame = anim.frames[0];\n        animResults[name] = {\n          frameNums,\n          frameCount:  frameNums.length,\n          minFrame:    Math.min(...frameNums),\n          maxFrame:    Math.max(...frameNums),\n          // Frame dimensions: row 1 frames should be 48\u00d796, row 0 was too small\n          frameWidth:  firstFrame?.frame?.realWidth,\n          frameHeight: firstFrame?.frame?.realHeight,\n        };\n      }\n\n      return animResults;\n    });\n\n    console.log('Walk-up animation registration:', JSON.stringify(results, null, 2));\n\n    for (const name of ['Amelia', 'Lucy', 'Ash']) {\n      const r = results[name];\n      expect(r?.error, `${name}_walk_up animation error`).toBeUndefined();\n\n      // Core FRG-204 sprite-row fix: frames must be 48-53 (row 1), not 0-3 (row 0)\n      expect(r.minFrame, `${name} walk-up first frame must be 48 (row 1 start, not row 0)`).toBe(48);\n      expect(r.maxFrame, `${name} walk-up last frame must be 53 (row 1 end)`).toBe(53);\n      expect(r.frameCount, `${name} walk-up must have 6 frames`).toBe(6);\n\n      // Frame size sanity check: row 1 frames are 48\u00d796 (full character height)\n      expect(r.frameWidth,  `${name} walk-up frame width must be 48`).toBe(48);\n      expect(r.frameHeight, `${name} walk-up frame height must be 96`).toBe(96);\n    }\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 6: all walk directions avoid row 0 placeholder frames \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('all 4 walk directions use correct non-placeholder frame ranges', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL_MASTER);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const results = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const scene  = engine.characters[0]?.sprite?.scene;\n      if (!scene) return { error: 'Phaser scene not accessible' };\n\n      const name = 'Amelia';\n      const dirs = ['up', 'down', 'left', 'right'];\n      const animData = {};\n\n      for (const dir of dirs) {\n        const anim = scene.anims.get(`${name}_walk_${dir}`);\n        if (!anim) { animData[dir] = { error: 'not found' }; continue; }\n        const frameNums = anim.frames.map(f => parseInt(f.frame.name, 10));\n        animData[dir] = {\n          frameNums,\n          minFrame:   Math.min(...frameNums),\n          maxFrame:   Math.max(...frameNums),\n          frameCount: frameNums.length,\n        };\n      }\n\n      return animData;\n    });\n\n    console.log('All walk direction frames:', JSON.stringify(results, null, 2));\n\n    // Each direction must have 6 frames and must NOT use row 0 (frames 0\u201347 are thumbnails)\n    for (const dir of ['up', 'down', 'left', 'right']) {\n      const r = results[dir];\n      expect(r?.error, `walk_${dir} animation error`).toBeUndefined();\n      expect(r.frameCount, `walk_${dir} must have 6 frames`).toBe(6);\n      expect(r.minFrame, `walk_${dir} must not use row 0 placeholder frames (frame < 48)`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(48);\n    }\n\n    // Exact expected frame ranges per direction (from WALK_DIRS in scene.js)\n    expect(results.up.minFrame,    'walk-up starts at frame 48 (row 1)').toBe(48);\n    expect(results.up.maxFrame,    'walk-up ends at frame 53').toBe(53);\n    expect(results.down.minFrame,  'walk-down starts at frame 288 (row 6)').toBe(288);\n    expect(results.down.maxFrame,  'walk-down ends at frame 293').toBe(293);\n    expect(results.right.minFrame, 'walk-right starts at frame 144 (row 3)').toBe(144);\n    expect(results.right.maxFrame, 'walk-right ends at frame 149').toBe(149);\n    expect(results.left.minFrame,  'walk-left mirrors right (starts at 144 with flipX)').toBe(144);\n    expect(results.left.maxFrame,  'walk-left mirrors right (ends at 149 with flipX)').toBe(149);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 7: walk-up animation plays with correct key during upward movement \u2500\u2500\n\n  test('character playing walk-up uses Amelia_walk_up animation key during upward movement', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL_MASTER);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const amelia = engine.characters.find(c => c.name === 'Amelia');\n      if (!amelia)         return { error: 'Amelia not found' };\n      if (!amelia.sprite)  return { error: 'Amelia has no sprite' };\n\n      const scene = amelia.sprite.scene;\n\n      // Place Amelia at a walkable tile and issue an upward move\n      amelia.tx = 8; amelia.ty = 7;\n      amelia.prevTx = 8; amelia.prevTy = 7;\n      amelia.action = 'idle';\n      amelia.path   = [];\n      amelia.moveProgress = 0;\n\n      // Move up: ty decreases from 7 \u2192 4 (all interior rows are walkable)\n      const ok = amelia.moveTo(8, 4);\n      if (!ok) return { error: 'moveTo(8,4) failed \u2014 path not found' };\n\n      // After moveTo(), the walk animation should be playing\n      const animKey = amelia.sprite.anims.currentAnim?.key;\n      const facing  = amelia.facing;\n\n      // Verify the registered frames for this animation via scene.anims\n      const anim = scene.anims.get('Amelia_walk_up');\n      const frameNums = anim ? anim.frames.map(f => parseInt(f.frame.name, 10)) : [];\n\n      return {\n        moveTo:    ok,\n        facing,\n        animKey,\n        minFrame:  frameNums.length ? Math.min(...frameNums) : -1,\n        maxFrame:  frameNums.length ? Math.max(...frameNums) : -1,\n        frameCount: frameNums.length,\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Walk-up in-motion result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.error, result.error).toBeUndefined();\n    expect(result.moveTo, 'moveTo should succeed for upward path').toBe(true);\n    expect(result.facing, 'Amelia should be facing up after moveTo(8,4)').toBe('up');\n    expect(result.animKey, 'walk-up animation should be active').toBe('Amelia_walk_up');\n\n    // FRG-204 sprite-row fix: the registered walk-up frames must be in 48-53 range\n    expect(result.minFrame, 'walk-up frames start at 48 (not 0 \u2014 that was the bug)').toBe(48);\n    expect(result.maxFrame, 'walk-up frames end at 53').toBe(53);\n    expect(result.frameCount, 'walk-up has 6 frames').toBe(6);\n  });\n});"
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #116
Edit TOOL CALL 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/character-facing.spec.js",
  "old_string": "  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 5: Talk already-nearby \u2014 face each other immediately (AI path) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('characters face each other immediately when already close (AI talk path)', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n\n    // Simulate the AI branch where dist <= 2 \u2192 face each other immediately\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const chars  = engine.characters;\n      const nyx   = chars.find(c => c.id === 'nyx');\n      const maple  = chars.find(c => c.id === 'maple');\n\n      // Place them 1 tile apart (dist \u2264 2 \u2192 should face immediately)\n      nyx.tx   = 10; nyx.ty   = 5;\n      maple.tx = 10; maple.ty = 6;\n\n      nyx.facing   = 'left';  // Wrong \u2014 should be corrected\n      maple.facing = 'right'; // Wrong \u2014 should be corrected\n\n      // Simulate the already-nearby branch in ai.js:\n      //   char._updateFacing(target.tx - char.tx, target.ty - char.ty);\n      //   target._updateFacing(char.tx - target.tx, char.ty - target.ty);\n      nyx._updateFacing(maple.tx - nyx.tx, maple.ty - nyx.ty);\n      maple._updateFacing(nyx.tx - maple.tx, nyx.ty - maple.ty);\n\n      return {\n        nyx:   { facing: nyx.facing   },\n        maple: { facing: maple.facing },\n      };\n    });\n\n    // nyx is at y=5, maple at y=6 \u2192 nyx should face down (toward maple), maple should face up (toward nyx)\n    console.log('Nyx facing:', result.nyx.facing, '| Maple facing:', result.maple.facing);\n    expect(result.nyx.facing,   'Nyx should face down toward Maple').toBe('down');\n    expect(result.maple.facing, 'Maple should face up toward Nyx').toBe('up');\n  });\n});",
  "new_string": "  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 5: Talk already-nearby \u2014 face each other immediately (AI path) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('characters face each other immediately when already close (AI talk path)', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n\n    // Simulate the AI branch where dist <= 2 \u2192 face each other immediately\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const chars  = engine.characters;\n      const nyx   = chars.find(c => c.id === 'nyx');\n      const maple  = chars.find(c => c.id === 'maple');\n\n      // Place them 1 tile apart (dist \u2264 2 \u2192 should face immediately)\n      nyx.tx   = 10; nyx.ty   = 5;\n      maple.tx = 10; maple.ty = 6;\n\n      nyx.facing   = 'left';  // Wrong \u2014 should be corrected\n      maple.facing = 'right'; // Wrong \u2014 should be corrected\n\n      // Simulate the already-nearby branch in ai.js:\n      //   char._updateFacing(target.tx - char.tx, target.ty - char.ty);\n      //   target._updateFacing(char.tx - target.tx, char.ty - target.ty);\n      nyx._updateFacing(maple.tx - nyx.tx, maple.ty - nyx.ty);\n      maple._updateFacing(nyx.tx - maple.tx, nyx.ty - maple.ty);\n\n      return {\n        nyx:   { facing: nyx.facing   },\n        maple: { facing: maple.facing },\n      };\n    });\n\n    // nyx is at y=5, maple at y=6 \u2192 nyx should face down (toward maple), maple should face up (toward nyx)\n    console.log('Nyx facing:', result.nyx.facing, '| Maple facing:', result.maple.facing);\n    expect(result.nyx.facing,   'Nyx should face down toward Maple').toBe('down');\n    expect(result.maple.facing, 'Maple should face up toward Nyx').toBe('up');\n  });\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 FRG-244 (directional idle fix): conversations use {name}_idle_{facing} \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n//\n// Runs against master dev server at localhost:5177.\n// window._gameEngine is set unconditionally in scene.js create() \u2014 no injection needed.\n// Characters identified by .name ('Amelia', 'Lucy', 'Ash') not .id (which doesn't exist in master).\n//\n// Bug: startTalk() always played {name}_idle_anim (generic down-facing idle) for both partners,\n//      making characters appear to look away from each other during conversations.\n// Fix: Added _playDirectionalIdle() which plays {name}_idle_{facing}, and startTalk() calls it\n//      after _startConversation() sets the correct facing direction for each character.\n\nconst LOCAL_URL_MASTER_FACING = 'http://localhost:5177';\n\ntest.describe('FRG-244 (directional idle): conversations use directional idle animations', () => {\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 6: _startConversation sets facing and plays directional idle \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('_startConversation plays {name}_idle_{facing} for both partners (horizontal)', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL_MASTER_FACING);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const amelia = engine.characters.find(c => c.name === 'Amelia');\n      const lucy   = engine.characters.find(c => c.name === 'Lucy');\n\n      if (!amelia || !lucy)          return { error: 'Characters not found' };\n      if (!amelia.sprite || !lucy.sprite) return { error: 'Sprites not available' };\n\n      // Position Amelia to the left of Lucy (dx = 3 > 0 \u2192 Amelia faces right, Lucy faces left)\n      amelia.tx = 6;  amelia.ty = 5;\n      lucy.tx   = 9;  lucy.ty   = 5;\n      amelia.path = []; amelia.moveProgress = 0; amelia.action = 'idle';\n      lucy.path   = []; lucy.moveProgress   = 0; lucy.action   = 'idle';\n\n      // Reset facings to a wrong value so we can see the correction\n      amelia.facing = 'down';\n      lucy.facing   = 'down';\n\n      // engine._startConversation: faces both toward each other, then calls startTalk on each\n      engine._startConversation(amelia, lucy);\n\n      return {\n        amelia: {\n          facing:  amelia.facing,\n          animKey: amelia.sprite.anims.currentAnim?.key,\n          action:  amelia.action,\n        },\n        lucy: {\n          facing:  lucy.facing,\n          animKey: lucy.sprite.anims.currentAnim?.key,\n          action:  lucy.action,\n        },\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Horizontal conversation animation result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.error, result.error).toBeUndefined();\n\n    // Amelia (tx=6) is left of Lucy (tx=9) \u2192 Amelia faces right, Lucy faces left\n    expect(result.amelia.facing, 'Amelia should face right toward Lucy').toBe('right');\n    expect(result.lucy.facing,   'Lucy should face left toward Amelia').toBe('left');\n\n    // FRG-244 fix: animation key must be directional, NOT generic idle_anim\n    expect(result.amelia.animKey,\n      'Amelia must play Amelia_idle_right (not Amelia_idle_anim) when talking'\n    ).toBe('Amelia_idle_right');\n    expect(result.lucy.animKey,\n      'Lucy must play Lucy_idle_left (not Lucy_idle_anim) when talking'\n    ).toBe('Lucy_idle_left');\n\n    expect(result.amelia.action, 'Amelia should be in talking state').toBe('talking');\n    expect(result.lucy.action,   'Lucy should be in talking state').toBe('talking');\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 7: vertical conversation \u2014 up/down directional idles \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('_startConversation plays directional idle for vertical character arrangement', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL_MASTER_FACING);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const ash  = engine.characters.find(c => c.name === 'Ash');\n      const lucy = engine.characters.find(c => c.name === 'Lucy');\n\n      if (!ash || !lucy)           return { error: 'Characters not found' };\n      if (!ash.sprite || !lucy.sprite) return { error: 'Sprites not available' };\n\n      // Position Ash above Lucy (dx=0, dy=3 \u2192 Ash faces down, Lucy faces up)\n      ash.tx  = 10; ash.ty  = 4;\n      lucy.tx = 10; lucy.ty = 7;\n      ash.path  = []; ash.moveProgress  = 0; ash.action  = 'idle';\n      lucy.path = []; lucy.moveProgress = 0; lucy.action = 'idle';\n      ash.facing  = 'right'; // wrong \u2014 should be corrected\n      lucy.facing = 'left';  // wrong \u2014 should be corrected\n\n      engine._startConversation(ash, lucy);\n\n      return {\n        ash:  { facing: ash.facing,  animKey: ash.sprite.anims.currentAnim?.key  },\n        lucy: { facing: lucy.facing, animKey: lucy.sprite.anims.currentAnim?.key },\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Vertical conversation animation result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.error, result.error).toBeUndefined();\n\n    // Ash (ty=4) above Lucy (ty=7) \u2192 Ash faces down, Lucy faces up\n    expect(result.ash.facing,  'Ash should face down toward Lucy').toBe('down');\n    expect(result.lucy.facing, 'Lucy should face up toward Ash').toBe('up');\n\n    // FRG-244 fix: directional idle, not generic idle_anim\n    expect(result.ash.animKey).not.toBe('Ash_idle_anim');\n    expect(result.lucy.animKey).not.toBe('Lucy_idle_anim');\n    expect(result.ash.animKey,  'Ash plays Ash_idle_down during talk').toBe('Ash_idle_down');\n    expect(result.lucy.animKey, 'Lucy plays Lucy_idle_up during talk').toBe('Lucy_idle_up');\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 8: _playDirectionalIdle matches facing for all 4 directions \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('_playDirectionalIdle plays {name}_idle_{facing} for each direction', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL_MASTER_FACING);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const results = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const amelia = engine.characters.find(c => c.name === 'Amelia');\n\n      if (!amelia)        return { error: 'Amelia not found' };\n      if (!amelia.sprite) return { error: 'Amelia has no sprite' };\n\n      const directions = ['down', 'left', 'right', 'up'];\n      const animResults = {};\n\n      for (const dir of directions) {\n        amelia.facing = dir;\n        amelia._playDirectionalIdle();\n        animResults[dir] = amelia.sprite.anims.currentAnim?.key;\n      }\n\n      return animResults;\n    });\n\n    console.log('Directional idle animation keys:', JSON.stringify(results));\n\n    expect(results.error, results.error).toBeUndefined();\n\n    for (const dir of ['down', 'left', 'right', 'up']) {\n      expect(results[dir],\n        `Amelia facing ${dir} must play Amelia_idle_${dir} (not Amelia_idle_anim)`\n      ).toBe(`Amelia_idle_${dir}`);\n    }\n  });\n});"
}

      "old_string": "  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 5: Talk already-nearby \u2014 face each other immediately (AI path) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('characters face each other immediately when already close (AI talk path)', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n\n    // Simulate the AI branch where dist <= 2 \u2192 face each other immediately\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const chars  = engine.characters;\n      const nyx   = chars.find(c => c.id === 'nyx');\n      const maple  = chars.find(c => c.id === 'maple');\n\n      // Place them 1 tile apart (dist \u2264 2 \u2192 should face immediately)\n      nyx.tx   = 10; nyx.ty   = 5;\n      maple.tx = 10; maple.ty = 6;\n\n      nyx.facing   = 'left';  // Wrong \u2014 should be corrected\n      maple.facing = 'right'; // Wrong \u2014 should be corrected\n\n      // Simulate the already-nearby branch in ai.js:\n      //   char._updateFacing(target.tx - char.tx, target.ty - char.ty);\n      //   target._updateFacing(char.tx - target.tx, char.ty - target.ty);\n      nyx._updateFacing(maple.tx - nyx.tx, maple.ty - nyx.ty);\n      maple._updateFacing(nyx.tx - maple.tx, nyx.ty - maple.ty);\n\n      return {\n        nyx:   { facing: nyx.facing   },\n        maple: { facing: maple.facing },\n      };\n    });\n\n    // nyx is at y=5, maple at y=6 \u2192 nyx should face down (toward maple), maple should face up (toward nyx)\n    console.log('Nyx facing:', result.nyx.facing, '| Maple facing:', result.maple.facing);\n    expect(result.nyx.facing,   'Nyx should face down toward Maple').toBe('down');\n    expect(result.maple.facing, 'Maple should face up toward Nyx').toBe('up');\n  });\n});",
      "new_string": "  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 5: Talk already-nearby \u2014 face each other immediately (AI path) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('characters face each other immediately when already close (AI talk path)', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await injectEngineExposure(page);\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL);\n    await page.waitForFunction(() => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3, { timeout: 15_000 });\n\n    // Simulate the AI branch where dist <= 2 \u2192 face each other immediately\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const chars  = engine.characters;\n      const nyx   = chars.find(c => c.id === 'nyx');\n      const maple  = chars.find(c => c.id === 'maple');\n\n      // Place them 1 tile apart (dist \u2264 2 \u2192 should face immediately)\n      nyx.tx   = 10; nyx.ty   = 5;\n      maple.tx = 10; maple.ty = 6;\n\n      nyx.facing   = 'left';  // Wrong \u2014 should be corrected\n      maple.facing = 'right'; // Wrong \u2014 should be corrected\n\n      // Simulate the already-nearby branch in ai.js:\n      //   char._updateFacing(target.tx - char.tx, target.ty - char.ty);\n      //   target._updateFacing(char.tx - target.tx, char.ty - target.ty);\n      nyx._updateFacing(maple.tx - nyx.tx, maple.ty - nyx.ty);\n      maple._updateFacing(nyx.tx - maple.tx, nyx.ty - maple.ty);\n\n      return {\n        nyx:   { facing: nyx.facing   },\n        maple: { facing: maple.facing },\n      };\n    });\n\n    // nyx is at y=5, maple at y=6 \u2192 nyx should face down (toward maple), maple should face up (toward nyx)\n    console.log('Nyx facing:', result.nyx.facing, '| Maple facing:', result.maple.facing);\n    expect(result.nyx.facing,   'Nyx should face down toward Maple').toBe('down');\n    expect(result.maple.facing, 'Maple should face up toward Nyx').toBe('up');\n  });\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 FRG-244 (directional idle fix): conversations use {name}_idle_{facing} \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n//\n// Runs against master dev server at localhost:5177.\n// window._gameEngine is set unconditionally in scene.js create() \u2014 no injection needed.\n// Characters identified by .name ('Amelia', 'Lucy', 'Ash') not .id (which doesn't exist in master).\n//\n// Bug: startTalk() always played {name}_idle_anim (generic down-facing idle) for both partners,\n//      making characters appear to look away from each other during conversations.\n// Fix: Added _playDirectionalIdle() which plays {name}_idle_{facing}, and startTalk() calls it\n//      after _startConversation() sets the correct facing direction for each character.\n\nconst LOCAL_URL_MASTER_FACING = 'http://localhost:5177';\n\ntest.describe('FRG-244 (directional idle): conversations use directional idle animations', () => {\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 6: _startConversation sets facing and plays directional idle \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('_startConversation plays {name}_idle_{facing} for both partners (horizontal)', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL_MASTER_FACING);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const amelia = engine.characters.find(c => c.name === 'Amelia');\n      const lucy   = engine.characters.find(c => c.name === 'Lucy');\n\n      if (!amelia || !lucy)          return { error: 'Characters not found' };\n      if (!amelia.sprite || !lucy.sprite) return { error: 'Sprites not available' };\n\n      // Position Amelia to the left of Lucy (dx = 3 > 0 \u2192 Amelia faces right, Lucy faces left)\n      amelia.tx = 6;  amelia.ty = 5;\n      lucy.tx   = 9;  lucy.ty   = 5;\n      amelia.path = []; amelia.moveProgress = 0; amelia.action = 'idle';\n      lucy.path   = []; lucy.moveProgress   = 0; lucy.action   = 'idle';\n\n      // Reset facings to a wrong value so we can see the correction\n      amelia.facing = 'down';\n      lucy.facing   = 'down';\n\n      // engine._startConversation: faces both toward each other, then calls startTalk on each\n      engine._startConversation(amelia, lucy);\n\n      return {\n        amelia: {\n          facing:  amelia.facing,\n          animKey: amelia.sprite.anims.currentAnim?.key,\n          action:  amelia.action,\n        },\n        lucy: {\n          facing:  lucy.facing,\n          animKey: lucy.sprite.anims.currentAnim?.key,\n          action:  lucy.action,\n        },\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Horizontal conversation animation result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.error, result.error).toBeUndefined();\n\n    // Amelia (tx=6) is left of Lucy (tx=9) \u2192 Amelia faces right, Lucy faces left\n    expect(result.amelia.facing, 'Amelia should face right toward Lucy').toBe('right');\n    expect(result.lucy.facing,   'Lucy should face left toward Amelia').toBe('left');\n\n    // FRG-244 fix: animation key must be directional, NOT generic idle_anim\n    expect(result.amelia.animKey,\n      'Amelia must play Amelia_idle_right (not Amelia_idle_anim) when talking'\n    ).toBe('Amelia_idle_right');\n    expect(result.lucy.animKey,\n      'Lucy must play Lucy_idle_left (not Lucy_idle_anim) when talking'\n    ).toBe('Lucy_idle_left');\n\n    expect(result.amelia.action, 'Amelia should be in talking state').toBe('talking');\n    expect(result.lucy.action,   'Lucy should be in talking state').toBe('talking');\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 7: vertical conversation \u2014 up/down directional idles \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('_startConversation plays directional idle for vertical character arrangement', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL_MASTER_FACING);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const ash  = engine.characters.find(c => c.name === 'Ash');\n      const lucy = engine.characters.find(c => c.name === 'Lucy');\n\n      if (!ash || !lucy)           return { error: 'Characters not found' };\n      if (!ash.sprite || !lucy.sprite) return { error: 'Sprites not available' };\n\n      // Position Ash above Lucy (dx=0, dy=3 \u2192 Ash faces down, Lucy faces up)\n      ash.tx  = 10; ash.ty  = 4;\n      lucy.tx = 10; lucy.ty = 7;\n      ash.path  = []; ash.moveProgress  = 0; ash.action  = 'idle';\n      lucy.path = []; lucy.moveProgress = 0; lucy.action = 'idle';\n      ash.facing  = 'right'; // wrong \u2014 should be corrected\n      lucy.facing = 'left';  // wrong \u2014 should be corrected\n\n      engine._startConversation(ash, lucy);\n\n      return {\n        ash:  { facing: ash.facing,  animKey: ash.sprite.anims.currentAnim?.key  },\n        lucy: { facing: lucy.facing, animKey: lucy.sprite.anims.currentAnim?.key },\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Vertical conversation animation result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.error, result.error).toBeUndefined();\n\n    // Ash (ty=4) above Lucy (ty=7) \u2192 Ash faces down, Lucy faces up\n    expect(result.ash.facing,  'Ash should face down toward Lucy').toBe('down');\n    expect(result.lucy.facing, 'Lucy should face up toward Ash').toBe('up');\n\n    // FRG-244 fix: directional idle, not generic idle_anim\n    expect(result.ash.animKey).not.toBe('Ash_idle_anim');\n    expect(result.lucy.animKey).not.toBe('Lucy_idle_anim');\n    expect(result.ash.animKey,  'Ash plays Ash_idle_down during talk').toBe('Ash_idle_down');\n    expect(result.lucy.animKey, 'Lucy plays Lucy_idle_up during talk').toBe('Lucy_idle_up');\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 8: _playDirectionalIdle matches facing for all 4 directions \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('_playDirectionalIdle plays {name}_idle_{facing} for each direction', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL_MASTER_FACING);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const results = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const amelia = engine.characters.find(c => c.name === 'Amelia');\n\n      if (!amelia)        return { error: 'Amelia not found' };\n      if (!amelia.sprite) return { error: 'Amelia has no sprite' };\n\n      const directions = ['down', 'left', 'right', 'up'];\n      const animResults = {};\n\n      for (const dir of directions) {\n        amelia.facing = dir;\n        amelia._playDirectionalIdle();\n        animResults[dir] = amelia.sprite.anims.currentAnim?.key;\n      }\n\n      return animResults;\n    });\n\n    console.log('Directional idle animation keys:', JSON.stringify(results));\n\n    expect(results.error, results.error).toBeUndefined();\n\n    for (const dir of ['down', 'left', 'right', 'up']) {\n      expect(results[dir],\n        `Amelia facing ${dir} must play Amelia_idle_${dir} (not Amelia_idle_anim)`\n      ).toBe(`Amelia_idle_${dir}`);\n    }\n  });\n});"

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #123
Edit TOOL CALL 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/object-sprite-swap.spec.js",
  "old_string": "    console.log('Bookshelf texture after reading\u2192idle:', bookshelfIdleTexture);\n    expect(bookshelfIdleTexture,\n      'bookshelf texture should revert to book_closed after returning to idle'\n    ).toBe('book_closed');\n  });\n});",
  "new_string": "    console.log('Bookshelf texture after reading\u2192idle:', bookshelfIdleTexture);\n    expect(bookshelfIdleTexture,\n      'bookshelf texture should revert to book_closed after returning to idle'\n    ).toBe('book_closed');\n  });\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 FRG-246 (asset-diff fix): stove_on.png is a distinct visual asset \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n//\n// Runs against master dev server at localhost:5177 (same server as tests 1-5 above).\n// No injection needed; we fetch the PNG assets directly from the static server.\n//\n// Bug: stove_on.png was a byte-for-byte copy of stove_off.png, so toggling the\n//      stove texture key had no visible effect \u2014 both states looked identical.\n// Fix: stove_on.png was replaced with a new lit-stove image (distinct pixel data).\n\nconst LOCAL_URL_MASTER_SPRITE = 'http://localhost:5177';\n\ntest.describe('FRG-246 (asset-diff): stove_on.png is visually distinct from stove_off.png', () => {\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 6: file sizes differ \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('stove_off.png and stove_on.png have different byte lengths', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL_MASTER_SPRITE);\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(async () => {\n      const [offResp, onResp] = await Promise.all([\n        fetch('/assets/sprites/stove_off.png'),\n        fetch('/assets/sprites/stove_on.png'),\n      ]);\n\n      if (!offResp.ok) return { error: `stove_off.png fetch failed: ${offResp.status}` };\n      if (!onResp.ok)  return { error: `stove_on.png fetch failed: ${onResp.status}`  };\n\n      const [offBuf, onBuf] = await Promise.all([\n        offResp.arrayBuffer(),\n        onResp.arrayBuffer(),\n      ]);\n\n      return {\n        stoveOffBytes:    offBuf.byteLength,\n        stoveOnBytes:     onBuf.byteLength,\n        differentSize:    offBuf.byteLength !== onBuf.byteLength,\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Stove PNG file sizes:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.error, result.error).toBeUndefined();\n    expect(result.stoveOffBytes, 'stove_off.png must be non-empty').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n    expect(result.stoveOnBytes,  'stove_on.png must be non-empty').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n    expect(result.differentSize,\n      `stove_off.png (${result.stoveOffBytes}B) and stove_on.png (${result.stoveOnBytes}B) ` +\n      'must have different file sizes \u2014 identical sizes indicate the same asset'\n    ).toBe(true);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 7: byte content differs in PNG header / IDAT chunks \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('stove_off.png and stove_on.png have different byte content (not identical files)', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL_MASTER_SPRITE);\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(async () => {\n      const [offResp, onResp] = await Promise.all([\n        fetch('/assets/sprites/stove_off.png'),\n        fetch('/assets/sprites/stove_on.png'),\n      ]);\n\n      if (!offResp.ok || !onResp.ok) {\n        return { error: `PNG fetch failed: off=${offResp.status} on=${onResp.status}` };\n      }\n\n      const [offBuf, onBuf] = await Promise.all([\n        offResp.arrayBuffer(),\n        onResp.arrayBuffer(),\n      ]);\n\n      // Quick identity check: same byte length AND identical first 200 bytes = same file\n      const SAMPLE = Math.min(200, offBuf.byteLength, onBuf.byteLength);\n      const offBytes = new Uint8Array(offBuf, 0, SAMPLE);\n      const onBytes  = new Uint8Array(onBuf,  0, SAMPLE);\n\n      let diffCount = 0;\n      for (let i = 0; i < SAMPLE; i++) {\n        if (offBytes[i] !== onBytes[i]) diffCount++;\n      }\n\n      const sameLength = offBuf.byteLength === onBuf.byteLength;\n      const noDiffs    = diffCount === 0;\n      const filesAreIdentical = sameLength && noDiffs;\n\n      return {\n        stoveOffBytes:    offBuf.byteLength,\n        stoveOnBytes:     onBuf.byteLength,\n        sampleDiffBytes:  diffCount,\n        filesAreIdentical,\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Stove PNG content comparison:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.error, result.error).toBeUndefined();\n    expect(result.filesAreIdentical,\n      `stove_off.png (${result.stoveOffBytes}B) and stove_on.png (${result.stoveOnBytes}B) ` +\n      `must not be identical \u2014 FRG-246 bug was that stove_on.png was a copy of stove_off.png ` +\n      `(${result.sampleDiffBytes} byte differences found in first 200 bytes)`\n    ).toBe(false);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 TEST 8: Phaser loads both textures as distinct assets in the cache \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('Phaser texture cache holds distinct textures for stove_off and stove_on', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(30_000);\n\n    await page.goto(LOCAL_URL_MASTER_SPRITE);\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const result = await page.evaluate(() => {\n      const engine = window._gameEngine;\n      const scene  = engine.characters[0]?.sprite?.scene;\n      if (!scene) return { error: 'Phaser scene not accessible' };\n\n      const texOff = scene.textures.get('stove_off');\n      const texOn  = scene.textures.get('stove_on');\n\n      if (!texOff || texOff.key === '__MISSING') return { error: 'stove_off texture not in Phaser cache' };\n      if (!texOn  || texOn.key  === '__MISSING') return { error: 'stove_on texture not in Phaser cache'  };\n\n      // In Phaser 3, each texture has a Source with the actual image element.\n      // Identical files loaded under different keys may share or differ in source URL.\n      const offSrc = texOff.source?.[0]?.image?.src ?? texOff.source?.[0]?.src ?? null;\n      const onSrc  = texOn.source?.[0]?.image?.src  ?? texOn.source?.[0]?.src  ?? null;\n\n      // Both textures must exist in the cache under their own keys\n      return {\n        offKey:  texOff.key,\n        onKey:   texOn.key,\n        offSrc,\n        onSrc,\n        // Width/height: if both were identical 48\u00d748 PNGs they'd match;\n        // distinct assets may differ in dimensions or at least in source URL.\n        offWidth:  texOff.source?.[0]?.width,\n        offHeight: texOff.source?.[0]?.height,\n        onWidth:   texOn.source?.[0]?.width,\n        onHeight:  texOn.source?.[0]?.height,\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Phaser texture cache result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.error, result.error).toBeUndefined();\n\n    // Both textures must be present and have their own distinct keys\n    expect(result.offKey, 'stove_off texture key').toBe('stove_off');\n    expect(result.onKey,  'stove_on texture key').toBe('stove_on');\n\n    // Source URLs must differ (each key loads from its own PNG file)\n    expect(result.offSrc, 'stove_off.png source URL').toMatch(/stove_off\\.png/);\n    expect(result.onSrc,  'stove_on.png source URL').toMatch(/stove_on\\.png/);\n    expect(result.offSrc,\n      'stove_off and stove_on must load from different source files'\n    ).not.toBe(result.onSrc);\n  });\n});"
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #136
Test 3 (Phaser texture cache) fails because Phaser converts image sources to blob URLs when loading them from a static server. The `source[0].image.src` is a blob URL, not the original file path. I need to fix the test assertion to not check for the filename in the source URL. The good news is that the first 2 tests pass (file sizes are different, content is different). The third test's failure is a test design issue - I used the wrong approach to verify distinctness through Phaser's internal texture source. Let me fix the third test to verify that the two textures are distinct in a different way. Since Phaser uses blob URLs, the simplest approach is to just verify the blob URLs themselves are different—they'll have unique identifiers since each load generates a fresh blob URL. Alternatively, I could skip the URL check entirely and just confirm the two textures are separate objects, which the different keys already guarantee. Looking at the test output more carefully, the real evidence that the files differ is in the byte sizes—395 versus 430 bytes—which tests 1 and 2 already captured. The third test was trying to validate this through the Phaser cache, but blob URLs don't give me a reliable source URL to compare. I should rewrite that test to use canvas pixel-level comparison instead. I'm realizing the simplest fix is to just strip out those URL assertions entirely and instead verify the textures exist in Phaser's cache under their correct keys, which the test already does. Since the fetch calls in tests 1 and 2 already confirm the files are different, I just need test 3 to assert that the blob URLs themselves are different from each other—that's sufficient proof they were loaded as separate assets.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #137
Test 3 fails because Phaser converts image sources to blob URLs. The source URL approach won't show the original filename. Let me fix that assertion:
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "text",
    "text": "Test 3 fails because Phaser converts image sources to blob URLs. The source URL approach won't show the original filename. Let me fix that assertion:"
  }

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #138
Edit TOOL CALL 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/object-sprite-swap.spec.js",
  "old_string": "      // In Phaser 3, each texture has a Source with the actual image element.\n      // Identical files loaded under different keys may share or differ in source URL.\n      const offSrc = texOff.source?.[0]?.image?.src ?? texOff.source?.[0]?.src ?? null;\n      const onSrc  = texOn.source?.[0]?.image?.src  ?? texOn.source?.[0]?.src  ?? null;\n\n      // Both textures must exist in the cache under their own keys\n      return {\n        offKey:  texOff.key,\n        onKey:   texOn.key,\n        offSrc,\n        onSrc,\n        // Width/height: if both were identical 48\u00d748 PNGs they'd match;\n        // distinct assets may differ in dimensions or at least in source URL.\n        offWidth:  texOff.source?.[0]?.width,\n        offHeight: texOff.source?.[0]?.height,\n        onWidth:   texOn.source?.[0]?.width,\n        onHeight:  texOn.source?.[0]?.height,\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Phaser texture cache result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.error, result.error).toBeUndefined();\n\n    // Both textures must be present and have their own distinct keys\n    expect(result.offKey, 'stove_off texture key').toBe('stove_off');\n    expect(result.onKey,  'stove_on texture key').toBe('stove_on');\n\n    // Source URLs must differ (each key loads from its own PNG file)\n    expect(result.offSrc, 'stove_off.png source URL').toMatch(/stove_off\\.png/);\n    expect(result.onSrc,  'stove_on.png source URL').toMatch(/stove_on\\.png/);\n    expect(result.offSrc,\n      'stove_off and stove_on must load from different source files'\n    ).not.toBe(result.onSrc);",
  "new_string": "      // In Phaser 3, textures are stored as blob URLs after loading \u2014 compare the blobs.\n      // Each independently loaded PNG produces a distinct blob URL even if file content differs.\n      const offSrc = texOff.source?.[0]?.image?.src ?? texOff.source?.[0]?.src ?? null;\n      const onSrc  = texOn.source?.[0]?.image?.src  ?? texOn.source?.[0]?.src  ?? null;\n\n      // Draw each texture to a canvas and sample a pixel to confirm distinct pixel data\n      // Use Phaser's built-in canvas snapshot approach via the texture frames\n      const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');\n      canvas.width = 48; canvas.height = 48;\n      const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');\n\n      // Helper: get RGBA of pixel (0,0) from a Phaser texture\n      function samplePixel(tex) {\n        const src = tex.source?.[0]?.image;\n        if (!src) return null;\n        ctx.clearRect(0, 0, 48, 48);\n        ctx.drawImage(src, 0, 0);\n        const d = ctx.getImageData(0, 0, 48, 48).data;\n        // Return first non-transparent pixel's color as a string\n        for (let i = 0; i < d.length; i += 4) {\n          if (d[i + 3] > 0) return `${d[i]},${d[i+1]},${d[i+2]},${d[i+3]}`;\n        }\n        return null;\n      }\n\n      const offPixel = samplePixel(texOff);\n      const onPixel  = samplePixel(texOn);\n\n      return {\n        offKey:    texOff.key,\n        onKey:     texOn.key,\n        // Blob URLs will always differ between loads; just confirm they exist\n        offHasSrc: !!offSrc,\n        onHasSrc:  !!onSrc,\n        srcsDiffer: offSrc !== onSrc,\n        offPixel,\n        onPixel,\n        pixelsDiffer: offPixel !== onPixel,\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Phaser texture cache result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.error, result.error).toBeUndefined();\n\n    // Both textures must be present and have their own distinct keys\n    expect(result.offKey, 'stove_off texture key').toBe('stove_off');\n    expect(result.onKey,  'stove_on texture key').toBe('stove_on');\n\n    // Each texture must have a source (blob URL) \u2014 proves it was loaded\n    expect(result.offHasSrc, 'stove_off must have a loaded source image').toBe(true);\n    expect(result.onHasSrc,  'stove_on must have a loaded source image').toBe(true);\n\n    // The two textures must have been loaded from distinct sources (different blobs)\n    expect(result.srcsDiffer,\n      'stove_off and stove_on must have been loaded from distinct source blobs'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    // Pixel-level check: at least one pixel must differ between the two textures\n    expect(result.pixelsDiffer,\n      `stove_off pixel (${result.offPixel}) and stove_on pixel (${result.onPixel}) ` +\n      'must differ \u2014 identical pixel data would confirm the FRG-246 bug is not fixed'\n    ).toBe(true);"
}

      "replace_all": false,
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/object-sprite-swap.spec.js",
      "old_string": "      // In Phaser 3, each texture has a Source with the actual image element.\n      // Identical files loaded under different keys may share or differ in source URL.\n      const offSrc = texOff.source?.[0]?.image?.src ?? texOff.source?.[0]?.src ?? null;\n      const onSrc  = texOn.source?.[0]?.image?.src  ?? texOn.source?.[0]?.src  ?? null;\n\n      // Both textures must exist in the cache under their own keys\n      return {\n        offKey:  texOff.key,\n        onKey:   texOn.key,\n        offSrc,\n        onSrc,\n        // Width/height: if both were identical 48\u00d748 PNGs they'd match;\n        // distinct assets may differ in dimensions or at least in source URL.\n        offWidth:  texOff.source?.[0]?.width,\n        offHeight: texOff.source?.[0]?.height,\n        onWidth:   texOn.source?.[0]?.width,\n        onHeight:  texOn.source?.[0]?.height,\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Phaser texture cache result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.error, result.error).toBeUndefined();\n\n    // Both textures must be present and have their own distinct keys\n    expect(result.offKey, 'stove_off texture key').toBe('stove_off');\n    expect(result.onKey,  'stove_on texture key').toBe('stove_on');\n\n    // Source URLs must differ (each key loads from its own PNG file)\n    expect(result.offSrc, 'stove_off.png source URL').toMatch(/stove_off\\.png/);\n    expect(result.onSrc,  'stove_on.png source URL').toMatch(/stove_on\\.png/);\n    expect(result.offSrc,\n      'stove_off and stove_on must load from different source files'\n    ).not.toBe(result.onSrc);",
      "new_string": "      // In Phaser 3, textures are stored as blob URLs after loading \u2014 compare the blobs.\n      // Each independently loaded PNG produces a distinct blob URL even if file content differs.\n      const offSrc = texOff.source?.[0]?.image?.src ?? texOff.source?.[0]?.src ?? null;\n      const onSrc  = texOn.source?.[0]?.image?.src  ?? texOn.source?.[0]?.src  ?? null;\n\n      // Draw each texture to a canvas and sample a pixel to confirm distinct pixel data\n      // Use Phaser's built-in canvas snapshot approach via the texture frames\n      const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');\n      canvas.width = 48; canvas.height = 48;\n      const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');\n\n      // Helper: get RGBA of pixel (0,0) from a Phaser texture\n      function samplePixel(tex) {\n        const src = tex.source?.[0]?.image;\n        if (!src) return null;\n        ctx.clearRect(0, 0, 48, 48);\n        ctx.drawImage(src, 0, 0);\n        const d = ctx.getImageData(0, 0, 48, 48).data;\n        // Return first non-transparent pixel's color as a string\n        for (let i = 0; i < d.length; i += 4) {\n          if (d[i + 3] > 0) return `${d[i]},${d[i+1]},${d[i+2]},${d[i+3]}`;\n        }\n        return null;\n      }\n\n      const offPixel = samplePixel(texOff);\n      const onPixel  = samplePixel(texOn);\n\n      return {\n        offKey:    texOff.key,\n        onKey:     texOn.key,\n        // Blob URLs will always differ between loads; just confirm they exist\n        offHasSrc: !!offSrc,\n        onHasSrc:  !!onSrc,\n        srcsDiffer: offSrc !== onSrc,\n        offPixel,\n        onPixel,\n        pixelsDiffer: offPixel !== onPixel,\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Phaser texture cache result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.error, result.error).toBeUndefined();\n\n    // Both textures must be present and have their own distinct keys\n    expect(result.offKey, 'stove_off texture key').toBe('stove_off');\n    expect(result.onKey,  'stove_on texture key').toBe('stove_on');\n\n    // Each texture must have a source (blob URL) \u2014 proves it was loaded\n    expect(result.offHasSrc, 'stove_off must have a loaded source image').toBe(true);\n    expect(result.onHasSrc,  'stove_on must have a loaded source image').toBe(true);\n\n    // The two textures must have been loaded from distinct sources (different blobs)\n    expect(result.srcsDiffer,\n      'stove_off and stove_on must have been loaded from distinct source blobs'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    // Pixel-level check: at least one pixel must differ between the two textures\n    expect(result.pixelsDiffer,\n      `stove_off pixel (${result.offPixel}) and stove_on pixel (${result.onPixel}) ` +\n      'must differ \u2014 identical pixel data would confirm the FRG-246 bug is not fixed'\n    ).toBe(true);"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #142
The pixel comparison shows both stove_off and stove_on have the same pixel at position (0,0): `58,58,80,255`. This might be because both images share the same background color for that pixel. The image dimensions are both 48×48 and the first non-transparent pixel happens to be the same. Looking at the actual files - stove_off.png is 395 bytes and stove_on.png is 430 bytes. They are clearly different files. But the first visible pixel happens to be the same color (which is probably the background/outer edge of the tile). I need a better pixel sampling strategy - instead of just the first non-transparent pixel, I should compare a hash of more pixels, or compare multiple pixel positions to find a difference. The background color is likely identical on both sprites since they share the same outer edge, so I need to sample from the center where the burner elements would actually differ. Let me check the center pixel at coordinates (24, 24) instead, which should capture the lit versus unlit state.

Edit TOOL CALL 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/object-sprite-swap.spec.js",
  "old_string": "      // Draw each texture to a canvas and sample a pixel to confirm distinct pixel data\n      // Use Phaser's built-in canvas snapshot approach via the texture frames\n      const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');\n      canvas.width = 48; canvas.height = 48;\n      const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');\n\n      // Helper: get RGBA of pixel (0,0) from a Phaser texture\n      function samplePixel(tex) {\n        const src = tex.source?.[0]?.image;\n        if (!src) return null;\n        ctx.clearRect(0, 0, 48, 48);\n        ctx.drawImage(src, 0, 0);\n        const d = ctx.getImageData(0, 0, 48, 48).data;\n        // Return first non-transparent pixel's color as a string\n        for (let i = 0; i < d.length; i += 4) {\n          if (d[i + 3] > 0) return `${d[i]},${d[i+1]},${d[i+2]},${d[i+3]}`;\n        }\n        return null;\n      }\n\n      const offPixel = samplePixel(texOff);\n      const onPixel  = samplePixel(texOn);\n\n      return {\n        offKey:    texOff.key,\n        onKey:     texOn.key,\n        // Blob URLs will always differ between loads; just confirm they exist\n        offHasSrc: !!offSrc,\n        onHasSrc:  !!onSrc,\n        srcsDiffer: offSrc !== onSrc,\n        offPixel,\n        onPixel,\n        pixelsDiffer: offPixel !== onPixel,\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Phaser texture cache result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.error, result.error).toBeUndefined();\n\n    // Both textures must be present and have their own distinct keys\n    expect(result.offKey, 'stove_off texture key').toBe('stove_off');\n    expect(result.onKey,  'stove_on texture key').toBe('stove_on');\n\n    // Each texture must have a source (blob URL) \u2014 proves it was loaded\n    expect(result.offHasSrc, 'stove_off must have a loaded source image').toBe(true);\n    expect(result.onHasSrc,  'stove_on must have a loaded source image').toBe(true);\n\n    // The two textures must have been loaded from distinct sources (different blobs)\n    expect(result.srcsDiffer,\n      'stove_off and stove_on must have been loaded from distinct source blobs'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    // Pixel-level check: at least one pixel must differ between the two textures\n    expect(result.pixelsDiffer,\n      `stove_off pixel (${result.offPixel}) and stove_on pixel (${result.onPixel}) ` +\n      'must differ \u2014 identical pixel data would confirm the FRG-246 bug is not fixed'\n    ).toBe(true);",
  "new_string": "      // Draw each texture to a canvas and compute a pixel-sum hash across the full image\n      const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');\n      canvas.width = 48; canvas.height = 48;\n      const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');\n\n      // Helper: sum all R+G+B values across the 48\u00d748 image as a simple hash\n      function pixelSum(tex) {\n        const src = tex.source?.[0]?.image;\n        if (!src) return null;\n        ctx.clearRect(0, 0, 48, 48);\n        ctx.drawImage(src, 0, 0);\n        const d = ctx.getImageData(0, 0, 48, 48).data;\n        let sum = 0;\n        for (let i = 0; i < d.length; i += 4) {\n          sum += d[i] + d[i + 1] + d[i + 2];\n        }\n        return sum;\n      }\n\n      const offSum = pixelSum(texOff);\n      const onSum  = pixelSum(texOn);\n\n      return {\n        offKey:    texOff.key,\n        onKey:     texOn.key,\n        offHasSrc: !!offSrc,\n        onHasSrc:  !!onSrc,\n        srcsDiffer: offSrc !== onSrc,\n        offPixelSum: offSum,\n        onPixelSum:  onSum,\n        pixelSumDiffers: offSum !== onSum,\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Phaser texture cache result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.error, result.error).toBeUndefined();\n\n    // Both textures must be present and have their own distinct keys\n    expect(result.offKey, 'stove_off texture key').toBe('stove_off');\n    expect(result.onKey,  'stove_on texture key').toBe('stove_on');\n\n    // Each texture must have a source (blob URL) \u2014 proves it was loaded\n    expect(result.offHasSrc, 'stove_off must have a loaded source image').toBe(true);\n    expect(result.onHasSrc,  'stove_on must have a loaded source image').toBe(true);\n\n    // The two textures must have been loaded from distinct sources (different blobs)\n    expect(result.srcsDiffer,\n      'stove_off and stove_on must have been loaded from distinct source blobs'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    // Full-image pixel-sum hash must differ: two visually distinct sprites have different\n    // total RGB sums. Identical files would produce identical sums.\n    expect(result.pixelSumDiffers,\n      `stove_off pixel sum (${result.offPixelSum}) equals stove_on pixel sum (${result.onPixelSum}) ` +\n      '\u2014 identical pixel sums indicate identical visual content (FRG-246 bug not fixed)'\n    ).toBe(true);"
}
