      "old_string": "      // Draw each texture to a canvas and sample a pixel to confirm distinct pixel data\n      // Use Phaser's built-in canvas snapshot approach via the texture frames\n      const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');\n      canvas.width = 48; canvas.height = 48;\n      const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');\n\n      // Helper: get RGBA of pixel (0,0) from a Phaser texture\n      function samplePixel(tex) {\n        const src = tex.source?.[0]?.image;\n        if (!src) return null;\n        ctx.clearRect(0, 0, 48, 48);\n        ctx.drawImage(src, 0, 0);\n        const d = ctx.getImageData(0, 0, 48, 48).data;\n        // Return first non-transparent pixel's color as a string\n        for (let i = 0; i < d.length; i += 4) {\n          if (d[i + 3] > 0) return `${d[i]},${d[i+1]},${d[i+2]},${d[i+3]}`;\n        }\n        return null;\n      }\n\n      const offPixel = samplePixel(texOff);\n      const onPixel  = samplePixel(texOn);\n\n      return {\n        offKey:    texOff.key,\n        onKey:     texOn.key,\n        // Blob URLs will always differ between loads; just confirm they exist\n        offHasSrc: !!offSrc,\n        onHasSrc:  !!onSrc,\n        srcsDiffer: offSrc !== onSrc,\n        offPixel,\n        onPixel,\n        pixelsDiffer: offPixel !== onPixel,\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Phaser texture cache result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.error, result.error).toBeUndefined();\n\n    // Both textures must be present and have their own distinct keys\n    expect(result.offKey, 'stove_off texture key').toBe('stove_off');\n    expect(result.onKey,  'stove_on texture key').toBe('stove_on');\n\n    // Each texture must have a source (blob URL) \u2014 proves it was loaded\n    expect(result.offHasSrc, 'stove_off must have a loaded source image').toBe(true);\n    expect(result.onHasSrc,  'stove_on must have a loaded source image').toBe(true);\n\n    // The two textures must have been loaded from distinct sources (different blobs)\n    expect(result.srcsDiffer,\n      'stove_off and stove_on must have been loaded from distinct source blobs'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    // Pixel-level check: at least one pixel must differ between the two textures\n    expect(result.pixelsDiffer,\n      `stove_off pixel (${result.offPixel}) and stove_on pixel (${result.onPixel}) ` +\n      'must differ \u2014 identical pixel data would confirm the FRG-246 bug is not fixed'\n    ).toBe(true);",
      "new_string": "      // Draw each texture to a canvas and compute a pixel-sum hash across the full image\n      const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');\n      canvas.width = 48; canvas.height = 48;\n      const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');\n\n      // Helper: sum all R+G+B values across the 48\u00d748 image as a simple hash\n      function pixelSum(tex) {\n        const src = tex.source?.[0]?.image;\n        if (!src) return null;\n        ctx.clearRect(0, 0, 48, 48);\n        ctx.drawImage(src, 0, 0);\n        const d = ctx.getImageData(0, 0, 48, 48).data;\n        let sum = 0;\n        for (let i = 0; i < d.length; i += 4) {\n          sum += d[i] + d[i + 1] + d[i + 2];\n        }\n        return sum;\n      }\n\n      const offSum = pixelSum(texOff);\n      const onSum  = pixelSum(texOn);\n\n      return {\n        offKey:    texOff.key,\n        onKey:     texOn.key,\n        offHasSrc: !!offSrc,\n        onHasSrc:  !!onSrc,\n        srcsDiffer: offSrc !== onSrc,\n        offPixelSum: offSum,\n        onPixelSum:  onSum,\n        pixelSumDiffers: offSum !== onSum,\n      };\n    });\n\n    console.log('Phaser texture cache result:', JSON.stringify(result));\n\n    expect(result.error, result.error).toBeUndefined();\n\n    // Both textures must be present and have their own distinct keys\n    expect(result.offKey, 'stove_off texture key').toBe('stove_off');\n    expect(result.onKey,  'stove_on texture key').toBe('stove_on');\n\n    // Each texture must have a source (blob URL) \u2014 proves it was loaded\n    expect(result.offHasSrc, 'stove_off must have a loaded source image').toBe(true);\n    expect(result.onHasSrc,  'stove_on must have a loaded source image').toBe(true);\n\n    // The two textures must have been loaded from distinct sources (different blobs)\n    expect(result.srcsDiffer,\n      'stove_off and stove_on must have been loaded from distinct source blobs'\n    ).toBe(true);\n\n    // Full-image pixel-sum hash must differ: two visually distinct sprites have different\n    // total RGB sums. Identical files would produce identical sums.\n    expect(result.pixelSumDiffers,\n      `stove_off pixel sum (${result.offPixelSum}) equals stove_on pixel sum (${result.onPixelSum}) ` +\n      '\u2014 identical pixel sums indicate identical visual content (FRG-246 bug not fixed)'\n    ).toBe(true);"

}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #160
Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfeat(qa): FRG-204/244/246 visual bug fix regression suites\n\nAdd 9 new tests (3 per bug) against master branch at localhost:5177:\n\nFRG-204 (sprite-row): walk_up animation uses frames 48-53 (row 1),\nnot 0-5 (row 0 placeholder thumbnails). Checks frame registration via\nscene.anims, all 4 walk directions avoid row 0, and walk-up plays the\ncorrect animation key during upward movement (frame dimensions 48\u00d796).\n\nFRG-244 (directional idle): _startConversation plays {name}_idle_{facing}\nfor both conversation partners instead of the generic {name}_idle_anim.\nTests horizontal (right/left) and vertical (down/up) arrangements, and\nverifies _playDirectionalIdle() works for all 4 facing directions.\n\nFRG-246 (asset-diff): stove_on.png is visually distinct from stove_off.png.\nVerifies via file size (395B vs 430B), byte content (4 differing bytes in\nfirst 200B), and Phaser texture cache pixel-sum comparison (508581 vs 567861).\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\"",
  "description": "Commit new test suites"
}

      "command": "git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfeat(qa): FRG-204/244/246 visual bug fix regression suites\n\nAdd 9 new tests (3 per bug) against master branch at localhost:5177:\n\nFRG-204 (sprite-row): walk_up animation uses frames 48-53 (row 1),\nnot 0-5 (row 0 placeholder thumbnails). Checks frame registration via\nscene.anims, all 4 walk directions avoid row 0, and walk-up plays the\ncorrect animation key during upward movement (frame dimensions 48\u00d796).\n\nFRG-244 (directional idle): _startConversation plays {name}_idle_{facing}\nfor both conversation partners instead of the generic {name}_idle_anim.\nTests horizontal (right/left) and vertical (down/up) arrangements, and\nverifies _playDirectionalIdle() works for all 4 facing directions.\n\nFRG-246 (asset-diff): stove_on.png is visually distinct from stove_off.png.\nVerifies via file size (395B vs 430B), byte content (4 differing bytes in\nfirst 200B), and Phaser texture cache pixel-sum comparison (508581 vs 567861).\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\"",
      "description": "Commit new test suites"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #169
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/.claude/projects/-home-gem-project/memory/MEMORY.md",
  "content": "# AI Aquarium QA \u2014 Project Memory\n\n## Project Overview\nPlaywright regression test suite for AI Aquarium (Phaser 3 simulation game).\n- Test repo: `/home/gem/project` (QA tests)\n- Source repo: cloned on-demand from `$SOURCE_REPO` env var, usually from `.env`\n- Tests run against local dev servers at hardcoded ports per feature branch\n\n## Test Structure\n- `tests/smoke/` \u2014 fast smoke (8 tests)\n- `tests/regression/` \u2014 feature regression suites\n- `tests/critical/` \u2014 end-to-end sim loop\n- `tests/helpers.js` \u2014 shared helpers\n\n## Source Repo Architecture (master)\n- **Characters**: `Character.js` \u2014 `name` ('Amelia','Lucy','Ash'), `label` ('Maple','Sol','Nyx')\n  - NO `.id` property (old tests used `.id` but master uses `.name`)\n  - Methods: `moveTo()`, `startTalk()`, `startInteract()`, `_playDirectionalIdle()`, `_updateFacingFromNextTile()`\n- **Engine**: `Engine.js` \u2014 `_startConversation(charA, charB)`, `objectEngine.getById(id)`\n- **Scene**: `scene.js` \u2014 `window._gameEngine = this.engine` set unconditionally in `create()`\n  - Access Phaser scene: `engine.characters[0].sprite.scene`\n  - `WALK_DIRS` defines animation frame ranges (up=48-53, down=288-293, right/left=144-149)\n  - `IDLE_DIRS` defines idle frame ranges (down=0-5, left=6-11, right=12-17, up=18-23)\n- **Objects**: `objects.js` \u2014 `getById(id)`, `getAll()`, objects have `spriteMap`, `state`\n\n## Dev Server Setup\n- Source repo has NO `package.json` \u2014 serve with `python3 -m http.server <PORT> &`\n- Wait for server: `curl -s -o /dev/null -w \"%{http_code}\" http://localhost:<PORT>/`\n- `window._gameEngine` already exposed in master's `scene.js` \u2014 no injection needed\n- For `window._gameScene`: inject via `page.route('**/scene.js*')` replacing the engine line\n\n## Key Injection Patterns\n```javascript\n// Inject _gameScene (for Phaser animation checks)\nawait page.route('**/scene.js*', async (route) => {\n  const response = await route.fetch();\n  let body = await response.text();\n  body = body.replace(\n    'window._gameEngine = this.engine;',\n    'window._gameEngine = this.engine;\\n    window._gameScene = this;'\n  );\n  await route.fulfill({ response, body });\n});\n\n// Access Phaser scene without injection (simpler)\nconst scene = engine.characters[0].sprite.scene;\nconst anim = scene.anims.get('Amelia_walk_up');\n```\n\n## Port History\n- 5173: character-facing (old feature branch)\n- 5174: walk-animation (old feature branch)\n- 5176: object-sprite-swap (old feature branch)\n- 5177: master branch (2026-03-13)\n\n## Phaser Gotchas\n- Animation frames: `anim.frames.map(f => parseInt(f.frame.name, 10))` to get frame numbers\n- Texture source URLs are blob URLs in headless Chrome \u2014 can't match filename in URL\n- Compare textures via pixel-sum: draw to canvas, sum all RGB values\n- Idle animations: 4 directional \u00d7 6 frames = 24 total frames per idle spritesheet\n- Walk animations: 6 frames per direction from walk spritesheet (2304\u00d7864, 48\u00d796 each)\n\n## Bug History\n- FRG-204 (timer): walk frame timer while-loop fix (localhost:5174)\n- FRG-204 (sprite-row): walk_up changed from frames 0-3 (row 0) to 48-53 (row 1)\n- FRG-244 (facing): characters face objects/each other during talk\n- FRG-244 (directional idle): startTalk plays {name}_idle_{facing} not {name}_idle_anim\n- FRG-243: contextual interaction animations (reading/sit/phone)\n- FRG-245: collision avoidance\n- FRG-246: stove_on.png asset was identical to stove_off.png \u2014 replaced with distinct lit variant\n  - stove_off.png = 395 bytes, stove_on.png = 430 bytes (master, 2026-03-13)\n  - Pixel sum: off=508581, on=567861\n\n## Test Results Summary (2026-03-13)\nTotal: 111 tests passing (102 original + 9 new for FRG-204/244/246 visual fixes)\n"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_016hCwAiBMP4WNbVZGkbECzc",
    "name": "Write",
    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/.claude/projects/-home-gem-project/memory/MEMORY.md",
      "content": "# AI Aquarium QA \u2014 Project Memory\n\n## Project Overview\nPlaywright regression test suite for AI Aquarium (Phaser 3 simulation game).\n- Test repo: `/home/gem/project` (QA tests)\n- Source repo: cloned on-demand from `$SOURCE_REPO` env var, usually from `.env`\n- Tests run against local dev servers at hardcoded ports per feature branch\n\n## Test Structure\n- `tests/smoke/` \u2014 fast smoke (8 tests)\n- `tests/regression/` \u2014 feature regression suites\n- `tests/critical/` \u2014 end-to-end sim loop\n- `tests/helpers.js` \u2014 shared helpers\n\n## Source Repo Architecture (master)\n- **Characters**: `Character.js` \u2014 `name` ('Amelia','Lucy','Ash'), `label` ('Maple','Sol','Nyx')\n  - NO `.id` property (old tests used `.id` but master uses `.name`)\n  - Methods: `moveTo()`, `startTalk()`, `startInteract()`, `_playDirectionalIdle()`, `_updateFacingFromNextTile()`\n- **Engine**: `Engine.js` \u2014 `_startConversation(charA, charB)`, `objectEngine.getById(id)`\n- **Scene**: `scene.js` \u2014 `window._gameEngine = this.engine` set unconditionally in `create()`\n  - Access Phaser scene: `engine.characters[0].sprite.scene`\n  - `WALK_DIRS` defines animation frame ranges (up=48-53, down=288-293, right/left=144-149)\n  - `IDLE_DIRS` defines idle frame ranges (down=0-5, left=6-11, right=12-17, up=18-23)\n- **Objects**: `objects.js` \u2014 `getById(id)`, `getAll()`, objects have `spriteMap`, `state`\n\n## Dev Server Setup\n- Source repo has NO `package.json` \u2014 serve with `python3 -m http.server <PORT> &`\n- Wait for server: `curl -s -o /dev/null -w \"%{http_code}\" http://localhost:<PORT>/`\n- `window._gameEngine` already exposed in master's `scene.js` \u2014 no injection needed\n- For `window._gameScene`: inject via `page.route('**/scene.js*')` replacing the engine line\n\n## Key Injection Patterns\n```javascript\n// Inject _gameScene (for Phaser animation checks)\nawait page.route('**/scene.js*', async (route) => {\n  const response = await route.fetch();\n  let body = await response.text();\n  body = body.replace(\n    'window._gameEngine = this.engine;',\n    'window._gameEngine = this.engine;\\n    window._gameScene = this;'\n  );\n  await route.fulfill({ response, body });\n});\n\n// Access Phaser scene without injection (simpler)\nconst scene = engine.characters[0].sprite.scene;\nconst anim = scene.anims.get('Amelia_walk_up');\n```\n\n## Port History\n- 5173: character-facing (old feature branch)\n- 5174: walk-animation (old feature branch)\n- 5176: object-sprite-swap (old feature branch)\n- 5177: master branch (2026-03-13)\n\n## Phaser Gotchas\n- Animation frames: `anim.frames.map(f => parseInt(f.frame.name, 10))` to get frame numbers\n- Texture source URLs are blob URLs in headless Chrome \u2014 can't match filename in URL\n- Compare textures via pixel-sum: draw to canvas, sum all RGB values\n- Idle animations: 4 directional \u00d7 6 frames = 24 total frames per idle spritesheet\n- Walk animations: 6 frames per direction from walk spritesheet (2304\u00d7864, 48\u00d796 each)\n\n## Bug History\n- FRG-204 (timer): walk frame timer while-loop fix (localhost:5174)\n- FRG-204 (sprite-row): walk_up changed from frames 0-3 (row 0) to 48-53 (row 1)\n- FRG-244 (facing): characters face objects/each other during talk\n- FRG-244 (directional idle): startTalk plays {name}_idle_{facing} not {name}_idle_anim\n- FRG-243: contextual interaction animations (reading/sit/phone)\n- FRG-245: collision avoidance\n- FRG-246: stove_on.png asset was identical to stove_off.png \u2014 replaced with distinct lit variant\n  - stove_off.png = 395 bytes, stove_on.png = 430 bytes (master, 2026-03-13)\n  - Pixel sum: off=508581, on=567861\n\n## Test Results Summary (2026-03-13)\nTotal: 111 tests passing (102 original + 9 new for FRG-204/244/246 visual fixes)\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #171
## Status DONE ## What Changed - `tests/regression/walk-animation.spec.js` — added 3 FRG-204 sprite-row tests: verify all 3 characters' `walk_up` animations use frames 48-53 (row 1), not row 0 placeholders; checks frame dimensions (48×96) and all 4 walk directions; targets `localhost:5177` - `tests/regression/character-facing.spec.js` — added 3 FRG-244 directional idle tests: verify `_startConversation()` plays `{name}_idle_{facing}` for both partners (horizontal + vertical arrangements) and `_playDirectionalIdle()` works for all 4 directions; targets `localhost:5177` - `tests/regression/object-sprite-swap.spec.js` — added 3 FRG-246 asset-diff tests: verify `stove_off.png` and `stove_on.png` differ by file size (395B vs 430B), byte content, and Phaser texture pixel-sum (508,581 vs 567,861); targets `localhost:5177` - `README.md` — updated test totals (102 → 111) and added new test summary table ## Key Decisions - Served static source repo with `python3 -m http.server 5177` (no package.json in repo) - Accessed Phaser scene via `engine.characters[0].sprite.scene` instead of injecting `window._gameScene` — simpler and avoids route interception for most tests - FRG-246 test 3 (Phaser texture cache): blob URLs can't be matched against filenames in headless Chrome, so used full-image pixel-sum comparison via canvas instead - Did not change existing `LOCAL_URL` values in spec files — appended new `describe` blocks with `LOCAL_URL_MASTER_*` constants to avoid breaking old tests ## PR / Branch - Committed directly to `main` (branch `main`, commit `ab1406b`)
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "text",
    "text": "## Status\nDONE\n\n## What Changed\n- `tests/regression/walk-animation.spec.js` \u2014 added 3 FRG-204 sprite-row tests: verify all 3 characters' `walk_up` animations use frames 48-53 (row 1), not row 0 placeholders; checks frame dimensions (48\u00d796) and all 4 walk directions; targets `localhost:5177`\n- `tests/regression/character-facing.spec.js` \u2014 added 3 FRG-244 directional idle tests: verify `_startConversation()` plays `{name}_idle_{facing}` for both partners (horizontal + vertical arrangements) and `_playDirectionalIdle()` works for all 4 directions; targets `localhost:5177`\n- `tests/regression/object-sprite-swap.spec.js` \u2014 added 3 FRG-246 asset-diff tests: verify `stove_off.png` and `stove_on.png` differ by file size (395B vs 430B), byte content, and Phaser texture pixel-sum (508,581 vs 567,861); targets `localhost:5177`\n- `README.md` \u2014 updated test totals (102 \u2192 111) and added new test summary table\n\n## Key Decisions\n- Served static source repo with `python3 -m http.server 5177` (no package.json in repo) \n- Accessed Phaser scene via `engine.characters[0].sprite.scene` instead of injecting `window._gameScene` \u2014 simpler and avoids route interception for most tests\n- FRG-246 test 3 (Phaser texture cache): blob URLs can't be matched against filenames in headless Chrome, so used full-image pixel-sum comparison via canvas instead\n- Did not change existing `LOCAL_URL` values in spec files \u2014 appended new `describe` blocks with `LOCAL_URL_MASTER_*` constants to avoid breaking old tests\n\n## PR / Branch\n- Committed directly to `main` (branch `main`, commit `ab1406b`)"
  }
}
